## PAGE 00212: (2026x Refresh1) C7 Performance Parameters

- page_id: `303990056`
- space_key: `MT`

- labels: ['c7-performance-parameters', 'c7', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

61788572

61788575

61788573

**Description**

The C7 Performance Parameters view lists the measures of effectiveness and the Capabilities they are applicable to. The capability requirements (and existing capabilities) are often expressed in terms of measures of effectiveness (MoE). These are high-level metrics used to judge the level of capability.

The C7 view does not present any values for measures of effectiveness, it simply specifies what types of measures apply to each Capability.

The C7 view may be used to:

- Set a Capability Requirements.
- Military Estimates.
- Strategic Reviews.
- Plan an Assumptions.

**Implementation**

The C7 view can be represented using:

- C7 Actual Performance Parameters table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- C7 Performance Parameters table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with hierarchical Actual Measurements table' space='']

## PAGE 00213: (2026x Refresh1) C8 Planning Assumptions

- page_id: `303990083`

- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

148777069

148777089

148777079

**Description**

The C8 Planning Assumptions Viewpoint is concerned with the identification and description of assumptions that have been made for the implementation of capabilities.

**Implementation**

The **C8 Planning Assumptions**view is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating C8 diagram' space=''] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating C8 table' space=''] . The table identifies and describes assumptions that have been made for the implementation of capabilities.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) C8 Planning Assumptions Parametric' space=''] diagram

**Constraint owner**

You can choose where all the constraints will be stored through the**Project Options**.

To select a constraint owner

1. In the main menu, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. Go to General > UAF , and in the Default Constraint Owners property group, choose a needed owner.

148777058

**Related pages**

- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF 4.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Concepts viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) C8 Planning Assumptions
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1993049271

1993049291

1993049281

**Description**

A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='MED'] to constrain the properties of another Capability. It contains [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='MED'] and constraint parameters as well as other properties from within that internal block context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a constraint parameter or contain a property that is bound to a constraint parameter (through any number of containment levels). A constraint block generally contains many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.

1993049270

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995020 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00215: (2026x Refresh1) Calculating metrics in the Metric Table

- page_id: `303995020`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995020/2026x+Refresh1+Calculating+metrics+in+the+Metric+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:00.443+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Tables > (2026x Refresh1) Metric table
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Excluding auxiliary resources from validation-based metrics calculation

1908376956

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Excluding auxiliary resources from validation-based metrics calculation

1908376958

1908376955

**On this page**

4

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Excluding auxiliary resources from validation-based metrics calculation

1908376957

Once you have created the Metric Table, which represents the metrics calculated at the time you created, you can make further calculations and track the evolution of your model development. 
If you need to append new metrics to your table, you can easily open it.

##### Opening the Metric Table

To open the Metric Table

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Containment tab' space=''] , right-click the element whose Metric Table you need to append.
2. On the shortcut menu of the element, click Tools > Metrics and then select the Metric Table you need to append. [ATTACHMENT filename='ms_open_metric_suite_menu.png']

The selected Metric Table opens. Now you are ready to calculate new metrics.

##### Calculating new metrics

To calculate new metrics

1. In the Metric Table, select the row. Below that, you need to add a row with newly calculated metrics.
2. Do one of the following:

- 
  - On the Edit toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='calc_metrics_whole_button.png'] and then select Calculate New Metric [ATTACHMENT filename='calc_new_metric.png'] from the open menu.
  - Right-click the selected row and then click Calculate New Metric [ATTACHMENT filename='calc_new_metric.png'] on the shortcut menu.

The new metrics are calculated and represented in the table below the selected row.

#### NOTE: Excluding auxiliary resources from validation-based metrics calculation

Excluding auxiliary resources from validation-based metrics calculation

By default, validation-based metrics are calculated in used read-only projects (auxiliary resources) as well. To exclude auxiliary resources from metric calculation scope, click[IMAGE alt='' src='']in the Metric Table toolbar and select the**Exclude Elements from Used Read-Only Projects**option.

##### Recalculating metrics

To recalculate metrics

1. In the Metric Table, select the row whose metrics you need to recalculate.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Edit toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='calc_metrics_whole_button.png'] and then select Recalculate [IMAGE alt='' src=''] from the open menu.
  - Right-click the selected row and then click Recalculate [ATTACHMENT filename='recalculate.png'] on the shortcut menu.

The new metrics are calculated and represented in the table, instead of the selected row.

##### Calculating new metrics with different parameter values

To calculate new metrics with different parameter values

1. In the Metric Table, select the row. Below that, you need to add a row for representing newly calculated metrics.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Edit toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='calc_metrics_whole_button.png'] and then select Add New Metric with Different Parameters [ATTACHMENT filename='new_metric_with_diff_pars.png'] from the open menu.
  - Right-click the selected row and then click Add New Metric with Different Parameters [ATTACHMENT filename='new_metric_with_diff_pars.png'] on the shortcut menu.
3. A new row with copied parameters is added to the Metric table. If the new row is empty, it might be that you haven’t selected any row. You may continue the procedure.
4. Change parameter values.
5. Select the newly created row and do one of the following:
  - On the Edit toolbar, click[IMAGE alt='' src='']and then select**Recalculate [IMAGE alt='' src='']**from the open menu.
  - Right-click the row and then select**Recalculate**[IMAGE alt='' src='']from the shortcut menu.

The new metrics are calculated according to the specified parameter values.

##### Calculating metrics when using multiple models

Metrics can be calculated when the model is stored in multiple projects. For example, the requirements are stored in the used project, and the blocks appears in the main project. If you want to calculate how many requirements are covered by system architecture blocks, you should run the calculations in the main project. Because in the main project you can see all model elements.

To calculate metrics when using multiple model

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment Tree' space='MED'], right-click the Requirements Package from the used project, select **Tools** > **Metrics** > **New Metric Table**. In the **Create Metric Table** dialog make sure, the scope and the Metric Table Owner is the same Package, as shown in the figure below. Click **OK**. Initially the scope for calculations is the same package where you choose to store the metrics. Therefore, the metrics may be not calculated on the metrics table creation. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='three_dots.png'] and in the Select Package dialog, choose another scope and click OK . As shown in the figure below, the System Requirements Specification shared Package is selected. [ATTACHMENT filename='changing_scope.png']
3. Re-calculate metrics of the first row.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The metrics are calculated by using multiple projects.

option.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h3>Recalculating metrics</h3><p><br /></p><p>To recalculate metrics</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Metric Table, select the row whose metrics you need to recalculate.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>On the Edit toolbar, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="calc_metrics_whole_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Calculating metrics in the Metric Table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> and then select </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Recalculate <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="recalculate.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Calculating metrics in the Metric Table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">from the open menu.</span></li><li>Right-click the selected row and then click <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Recalculate</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="recalculate.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Calculating metrics in the Metric Table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> on the shortcut menu.</span></li></ul></li></ol><p><br />The new metrics are calculated and represented in the table, instead of the selected row.<br /><br /></p><h3>Calculating new metrics with different parameter values</h3><p><br /></p><p>To calculate new metrics with different parameter values</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Metric Table, select the row. Below that, you need to add a row for representing newly calculated metrics.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the Edit toolbar, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="calc_metrics_whole_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Calculating metrics in the Metric Table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and then select <strong>Add New Metric with Different Parameters</strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_metric_with_diff_pars.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Calculating metrics in the Metric Table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> from the open menu.</li><li>Right-click the selected row and then click <strong>Add New Metric with Different Parameters</strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_metric_with_diff_pars.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Calculating metrics in the Metric Table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the shortcut menu.</li></ul></li><li><p>A new row with copied parameters is added to the Metric table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="276e088d-4f65-4ebc-9e66-04b1c2356f75"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the new row is empty, it might be that you haven’t selected any row. You may continue the procedure.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Change parameter values.</li><li><p>Select the newly created row and do one of the following:</p><ul><li><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">On the Edit toolbar, click </span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="calc_metrics_whole_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Calculating metrics in the Metric Table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> and then select </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Recalculate <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="recalculate.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Calculating metrics in the Metric Table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">from the open menu.</span></p></li><li><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Right-click the row and then select </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Recalculate</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="recalculate.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Calculating metrics in the Metric Table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> from the shortcut menu.</span></p></li></ul></li></ol><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><br />The new metrics are calculated according to the specified parameter values.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><br /></p><h3 style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Calculating metrics when using multiple models</h3><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Metrics can be calculated when the model is stored in multiple projects. For example, the requirements are stored in the used project, and the blocks appears in the main project. If you want to calculate how many requirements are covered by system architecture blocks, you should run the calculations in the main project. Because in the main project you can see all model elements.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><br /></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">To calculate metrics when using multiple model</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Containment Tree" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, right-click the Requirements Package from the used project, select <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Metrics</strong> &gt; <strong>New Metric Table</strong>. In the <strong>Create Metric Table</strong> dialog make sure, the scope and the Metric Table Owner is the same Package, as shown in the figure below. Click <strong>OK</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b495cf53-d765-4cdf-aa29-c672a9a56f00"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Initially the scope for calculations is the same package where you choose to store the metrics. Therefore, the metrics may be not calculated on the metrics table creation.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="metric_table_owner.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Calculating metrics in the Metric Table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li>Click<ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="13"><ri:attachment ri:filename="three_dots.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="CM" ri:content-title="Opening Resource Usage Map" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and in the <strong>Select Package</strong> dialog, choose another scope and click <strong>OK</strong>. As shown in the figure below, the <em>System Requirements Specification</em> shared Package is selected.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="changing_scope.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Calculating metrics in the Metric Table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Re-calculate metrics of the first row.</li></ol><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:height="159"><ri:attachment ri:filename="recalculate.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Calculating metrics in the Metric Table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The metrics are calculated by using multiple projects.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998390 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00216: (2026x Refresh1) Call Activity

- page_id: `303998390`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998390/2026x+Refresh1+Call+Activity
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:47:33.997+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN Process Diagram > (2026x Refresh1) Activities
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1487542023

1487542024

1487542024

**Description**

A Call Activity identifies a point in a process where a global process is used. The Call Activity acts as a wrapper for the invocation of the global process within the execution. The activation of the Call Activity results in the transfer of control to the called global process.

A Call Activity shares the same notation as a Task or SubProcess with a thick line around the boundary of its shape.

The BPMN2 Call Activity corresponds to the Reusable SubProcess of BPMN 1.2, and the BPMN2 SubProcess corresponds to the Embedded SubProcess of BPMN 1.2.

**Notation**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1487542022

**Related elements**

- BPMN Process
- Activities

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram

The Call Activity acts as a wrapper for the invocation of the global process within the execution. The activation of the Call Activity results in the transfer of control to the called global process.</p><p>A Call Activity shares the same notation as a Task or SubProcess with a thick line around the boundary of its shape.</p><p>The BPMN2 Call Activity corresponds to the Reusable SubProcess of BPMN 1.2, and the BPMN2 SubProcess corresponds to the Embedded SubProcess of BPMN 1.2.</p><p><strong>Notation</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="callactivity.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Call Activity" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fcbd208b-2b74-46e3-8ae9-4f5538669713"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1487542022</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) BPMN+Process">BPMN Process</a></li><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Activities">Activities</a></li></ul><p class="ancestor-link parent-link"><strong>Related diagrams</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) BPMN+Process+Diagram">BPMN Process Diagram</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) BPMN+Collaboration+Diagram">BPMN Collaboration Diagram</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00217: (2026x Refresh1) Call Choreography

- page_id: `303991291`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991291/2026x+Refresh1+Call+Choreography
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:47:41.641+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN Choreography Diagram > (2026x Refresh1) Choreography Activities
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1486708882

1486708882

1486708882

**Description**

A Call Choreography identifies the point in a process where a global choreography is used. It acts as a place holder to include a choreography element it is calling.

A Call Choreography is with a thick border.

**Notation**

- Call Choreography [ATTACHMENT filename='call_choreography.png']
- A Call Choreography referencing another Choreography is marked with a plus (+) sign [ATTACHMENT filename='call_choreography_ref.png']

1486708881

**Related elements**

- Organization Unit
- Role
- Person
- Choreography Activities
- Choreography

**Related diagram**

- BPMN Choreography Diagram

**Related procedures**

- Creating Call Choreography Activity

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995730 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00219: (2026x Refresh1) Calling operations from the model

- page_id: `303995730`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995730/2026x+Refresh1+Calling+operations+from+the+model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:58.737+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Specifying criteria for querying model > (2026x Refresh1) Creating new operations
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1325379609

1325379611

1325379608

**On this page**

****

1325379610

In a structured expression, you can call an operation defined in the model. An operation from the model can be either built-in or custom. The built-in operations, such as Union, Find, or Filter, are stored as executable opaque behaviors in the *Built-In Operations* package, which is owned by *UML Standard Profile::MagicDraw Profile*.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Be sure the **Exclude Used Projects** command on the Filter Options button[IMAGE alt='' src=''] menu is unchecked!

1325389280

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating new operations' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating script operations' space='']

****

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36cdc1d7-9a16-45e3-a0f9-fab1835cc33e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1325379609</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c6e695dc-d260-4cb6-8aa5-735ece55345a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1325379611</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36f940b9-32ed-4620-8486-7a2fe162cd51"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1325379608</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="4740461d-120b-4365-a5bb-acea529e16e9" /><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9fd86cdc-93a0-4623-b0c7-3f3eed808853"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1325379610</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In a structured expression, you can call an operation defined in the model. An operation from the model can be either built-in or custom. The built-in operations, such as Union, Find, or Filter, are stored as executable opaque behaviors in the <em>Built-In Operations</em> package, which is owned by <em>UML Standard Profile::MagicDraw Profile</em>.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_opaque_behavior_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Calling operations from the model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1669fbc7-3eea-4fe1-9ceb-e3edc9bfdf3b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Be sure the <strong>Exclude Used Projects</strong> command on the Filter Options <span>button</span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="filter.gif"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Calling operations from the model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> menu is unchecked!</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e81bef9b-1159-4066-ba5a-61ec11c4b409"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1325389280</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating new operations" /></ac:link><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating script operations" /></ac:link></li></ul></li></ul><p><strong><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991807 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00220: (2026x Refresh1) Cancel Boundary Event

- page_id: `303991807`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991807/2026x+Refresh1+Cancel+Boundary+Event
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:47:38.490+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN Process Diagram > (2026x Refresh1) Boundary Events
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A Cancel Boundary Event is used within a Transaction SubProcess.

This type of Event must be attached to the boundary of a SubProcess and will be triggered if the following conditions are satisfied:

- A Cancel End Event is reached within the Transaction SubProcess.
- A Transaction Protocol Cancel message is received while a transaction is being performed.

A Cancel Boundary Event always interrupts the Activity to which it is attached. The boundary of the event is always solid.

**Notation**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Related elements**

- Boundary Events
- Activities

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram
- BPMN Choreography Diagram

**Related procedure**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating and Using a BPMN Event' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>A Cancel Boundary Event is used within a Transaction SubProcess.</p><p>This type of Event must be attached to the boundary of a SubProcess and will be triggered if the following conditions are satisfied:</p><ul><li data-uuid="6c399906-7e31-4de5-b5d6-161b20359ba4">A Cancel End Event is reached within the Transaction SubProcess.</li><li data-uuid="da4a2be5-9c07-4990-84ab-823ac45a1c69">A Transaction Protocol Cancel message is received while a transaction is being performed.</li></ul><p>A Cancel Boundary Event always interrupts the Activity to which it is attached. The boundary of the event is always solid.</p><p><strong>Notation</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="boundary_event_cancel.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Cancel Boundary Event" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link parent-link" data-uuid="6aa87b4c-88dd-4f57-a876-4b19d7e4a6ce"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Boundary+Events">Boundary Events</a></li><li data-uuid="dbb5b6b2-10e7-495b-bbd1-9d8be05af3c7"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Activities">Activities</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related diagrams</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link" data-uuid="b2c48f68-1741-4768-860a-ad03879e337e"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) BPMN+Process+Diagram">BPMN Process Diagram</a></li><li data-uuid="448b6ae8-f9bd-458d-afa6-38ddb0b0b3eb"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) BPMN+Collaboration+Diagram">BPMN Collaboration Diagram</a></li><li data-uuid="d0edb8d0-4d3f-4f76-8edc-7e6fd8defa79"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) BPMN+Choreography+Diagram">BPMN Choreography Diagram</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedure</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="9e67a256-f2e8-412c-aa6e-784af8236c31"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating and Using a BPMN Event" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating and Using an Event]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998946 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00221: (2026x Refresh1) Cancel End Event

- page_id: `303998946`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998946/2026x+Refresh1+Cancel+End+Event
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:30:18.268+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN Process Diagram > (2026x Refresh1) End Events
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1485509671

1485509672

1485509672

**Description**

A Cancel End Event is used within a Transaction SubProcess. It indicates that the transaction will be canceled and a Cancel Boundary Event attached to the SubProcess boundary will be triggered. It also indicates that a Transaction Protocol Cancel message have to be sent to all entities involved in the transaction.

**Notation**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1485509670

**Related element**

- End Events

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram

**Related procedure**

- Creating and Using an Event

It indicates that the transaction will be canceled and a Cancel Boundary Event attached to the SubProcess boundary will be triggered. It also indicates that a Transaction Protocol Cancel message have to be sent to all entities involved in the transaction.</p><p><strong>Notation</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="end_event_cancel.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Cancel End Event" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f164b614-6821-4537-8c83-eb9aab06638d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1485509670</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related element</strong></p><ul><li>End Events</li></ul><p><strong>Related diagrams</strong></p><ul><li>BPMN Process Diagram</li><li>BPMN Collaboration Diagram</li></ul><p><strong>Related procedure</strong></p><ul><li>Creating and Using an Event</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000009 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00222: (2026x Refresh1) Capability Provision Creation Wizard

- page_id: `304000009`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000009/2026x+Refresh1+Capability+Provision+Creation+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:36.279+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Dialogs
- labels: ['capability-provision-creation-wizard', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Capability Provision Creation Wizard** is used to define a Capability Provision by one or more Resources at a specific period of time.

To open the **Capability Provision Creation Wizard**

1. Open a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) CV-3 Capability Phasing' space=''] (DoDAF), StV-3 (MODAF), NCV-3 (NAF), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Cr Capability Roadmap' space=''] (NAF 4.0), or Strategic Phasing diagram (UAF).
2. Select a Capability.
3. Do one of the following: The Capability Provision Creation Wizard opens.
  - On the <viewpoint name> diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability Provision .
  - On the diagram pane, right-click the Capability and select Add New Capability Provision .

The wizard consists of three steps:

truenone

##### Resources selection

In this step, you can select the existing or create newResources.

For more information on selecting elements, see **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']**, Step #2.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Capability service date specification

In this step, you can set the date and time, when the Resource should begin provisioning the Capability. The In Service Milestone is created.

For more information on setting the day and time, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Date and Time Settings dialog' space='']**Date and Time Settings** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Out of service date specification (Optional)

In this step, you can specify when the Resource should stop provisioning the Capability. The Out Of Service Milestone is created. This step is optional. If you skip this step, the Capability exhibition bar on the chart will have a triangle ending.

For more information on setting date ant time, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Date and Time Settings dialog' space='']**Date and Time Settings** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Related diagrams**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) CV-3 Capability Phasing' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) MODAF. StV-3 Capability Phasing' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) NCV-3 Capability Phasing' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Cr Capability Roadmap' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Strategic Roadmap' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990439 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00223: (2026x Refresh1) Capability viewpoint

- page_id: `303990439`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990439/2026x+Refresh1+Capability+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:30.194+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) DoDAF 2.0
- labels: ['capability-viewpoint', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

229515785

229515787

229515786

“The Capability Viewpoint and the DoDAF-described Models within the viewpoint are introduced into DoDAF V2.0 to address the concerns of Capability Portfolio Managers. In particular, the Capability Models describe capability taxonomy and capability evolution.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

“The DoD increasingly employs incremental acquisition to help manage the risks of complex procurements. Consequently, there is a need to provide visualizations of the evolving capabilities so that Portfolio Managers can synchronize the introduction of capability increments across a portfolio of projects. The Capability Models included within DoDAF are based on the program and capability information used by Portfolio Managers to capture the increasingly complex relationships between interdependent projects and capabilities.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

The basic modeling principles are demonstrated in the demo:

- How to model and relate capabilities to each other, watch demo**[*UPDM: Strategic**.**Part 1*](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xD91EYVU-bM).
- How to relate capabilities to the elements from other viewpoints and analyze capability provision and deployment gaps,watch demo **[*UPDM: Strategic**.**Part 2*](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LB1Ure3ravQ) *.*

Some elements in the demo are different from UAF Plugin but the modeling principles are the same.

229515784

**Views**

## PAGE 00224: (2026x Refresh1) Case 1. Coloring Ports by Type using Contains operation

- page_id: `303995825`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995825/2026x+Refresh1+Case+1.+Coloring+Ports+by+Type+using+Contains+operation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:26:05.684+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Specifying criteria for querying model > (2026x Refresh1) Case Studies for Querying the Model
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

967284465

967284478

967284468

To color ports by their type

1. Create a **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Legends' space='']** and a **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating and managing Legend Items' space='']**.
2. Right-click the Legend Item to open its Specification window .
3. C lick three dots [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the Adornment property to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying Legend Item adornment' space=''] . Click OK .
4. Click three dots [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the **Elements by Condition**property to edit it. **Elements by Condition** dialog opens.
5. Select Create operation > **Metachain Navigation** .
6. Click **Insert**.
7. Under **Metaclass or Stereotype**, select **Port**, and under**Property**, select****Type****. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
8. Select Create operation > **Operation from Model** > **Contains** .
9. Drag-and-drop Metachain Navigation onto Input parameter. [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-28_11-52-55.png']
10. Select Obj > Element > select the port type to verify. [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-28_11-58-25.png']
11. Click OK .

967284464

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Case Studies for Querying the Model' space='']true

Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click three dots <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Case 1. Coloring Ports by Type using Contains operation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> next to the</span> <strong>Elements by Condition</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">property to edit it. <strong>Elements by Condition</strong> dialog opens.</span></span></li><li>Select <strong>Create operation </strong>&gt;<strong> <strong>Metachain Navigation</strong></strong>.</li><li><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Click <strong>Insert</strong>.</span></p></li><li><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Under <strong>Metaclass or Stereotype</strong>, select <strong>Port</strong>, and under<strong> Property</strong>, select<strong> <strong>Type</strong></strong>.</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2021-12-28_11-52-6.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Case 1. Coloring Ports by Type using Contains operation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p></li><li>Select <strong>Create operation </strong>&gt;<strong> <strong>Operation from Model</strong> </strong>&gt;<strong> <strong>Contains</strong></strong>.</li><li>Drag-and-drop <strong>Metachain Navigation </strong>onto <strong>Input </strong>parameter.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2021-12-28_11-52-55.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Case 1. Coloring Ports by Type using Contains operation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Select <strong>Obj</strong> &gt; <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Element </strong>&gt; select the port type to verify.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2021-12-28_11-58-25.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Case 1. Coloring Ports by Type using Contains operation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6b0a77d5-2e8d-4629-b174-ac62fea15d1a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">967284464</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt-include" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="16c59260-4602-4361-9b7f-2b8afeed86e1"><ac:parameter ac:name=""><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Case Studies for Querying the Model" /></ac:link></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="nopanel">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00225: (2026x Refresh1) Case 10. Filtering Diagrams by Modification Date

- page_id: `303995909`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995909/2026x+Refresh1+Case+10.+Filtering+Diagrams+by+Modification+Date
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:26:13.478+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Specifying criteria for querying model > (2026x Refresh1) Case Studies for Querying the Model
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

1360697410

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Groovy

1360697431

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Groovy

1360697421

To filter diagrams by modification date

1. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Generic Table' space=''] .
2. Set the Element Type to Diagram . Make sure to select the Include Subtypes check box. Click OK .
3. Set the Scope where the diagrams will be searched for.
4. Click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] icon****in the**Scope**field and s elect **Custom** . The Query dialog opens.
5. Select **Create operation** > **Filter**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. Select Filter > Input > Find . [ATTACHMENT filename='3_case_10.png']
7. Set the Type to Diagram . [ATTACHMENT filename='4_case_10.png']
8. Expand Find > Scope > Contextual Variable . [ATTACHMENT filename='5_case_10.png']
9. Select **Filter**> **Predicate** > **Operation from Model** > **GreaterThanOrEquals**. If you cannot see the **GreaterThanOrEquals** operation, make sure the**Apply Filter**box is not checked. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
10. Select Predicate > Body > A > Script . [ATTACHMENT filename='7_case_10.png']
11. From the Language drop-down list, select **Groovy**.
12. Insert the following script as the **Body**: Groovy [IMAGE alt='' src='']
13. Select A > arg1 > Reset . [ATTACHMENT filename='9_case_10.png']
14. Select A > arg1 > Simple Navigation . [ATTACHMENT filename='10_case_10.png']
15. Select Modification date and set Is Applied to true . [ATTACHMENT filename='11_case_10.png']
16. Select Predicate > Body > B > Script . [ATTACHMENT filename='12_case_10.png']
17. From the Language drop-down list, select **Groovy**.
18. Insert the following script as the **Body**: Groovy [IMAGE alt='' src='']
19. Click **OK**. To view the column, make sure to display it via the **Columns** button in the diagram toolbar > **Select Columns** > set the **Modification date** column to **true**.

1360697408

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Case Studies for Querying the Model' space='']true

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995937 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00226: (2026x Refresh1) Case 11. Server Project Version in Diagram

- page_id: `303995937`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995937/2026x+Refresh1+Case+11.+Server+Project+Version+in+Diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:26:14.763+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Specifying criteria for querying model > (2026x Refresh1) Case Studies for Querying the Model
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: JavaScript Rhino

1476040892

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: JavaScript Rhino

1476040912

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: JavaScript Rhino

1476040902

To retrieve the Server Project Version in a Diagram

1. Right-click the empty space on the diagram pane and open the Specification window .
2. On the left side of the dialog, select Traceability > Create . Expression dialog opens.
3. Select **Create operation**>******Script**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Specify the Name for the derived property.
5. Set the Type as String .
6. Specify the Operation Name .
7. From the Language drop-down list, select JavaScript Rhino .
8. Insert the following script as the **Body**. JavaScript Rhino [IMAGE alt='' src='']
9. Click **OK**.

To display the Server Project Version in a Diagram

1. Create the **Note** symbol in the diagram and anchor it to the diagram.
2. Right-click the **Note** symbol and deselect **Represent Diagram Context (Package)**.
3. (Optional) Right-click the **Note** symbol and deselect **Apply SysML Callout Style**.
4. Right-click the **Note** symbol and select **Edit Compartments**.
5. In the **Element Properties** tab of the **Compartment Edit** dialog, select the name of the property you created. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. Click OK . You can see the property name and the server project version in the **Note** symbol. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1361035242

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Case Studies for Querying the Model' space='']true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e097c4b1-b463-4d21-ae0a-c523a6c4048b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1476040892</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac4b068f-5ee1-4baa-b399-0cc53ce9af2b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1476040912</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0705b70d-5668-46d9-bd64-3b74603d81fc"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1476040902</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To retrieve the Server Project Version in a Diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the empty space on the diagram pane and open the <strong>Specification</strong> <strong>window</strong>.</li><li>On the left side of the dialog, select <strong>Traceability</strong> &gt; <strong>Create</strong>. <strong>Expression</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select <strong>Create operation </strong>&gt;<strong> </strong><strong>Script</strong>.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="1_create_operation_script.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Case 11. Server Project Version in Diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li>Specify the <strong>Name</strong> for the derived property.</li><li>Set the <strong>Type</strong> as <strong>String</strong>.</li><li>Specify the <strong>Operation Name</strong>.</li><li>From the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Language </strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">drop-down list, select </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">JavaScript Rhino</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> Insert the following script as the <strong>Body</strong></span></span>. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7534e3e7-a876-4dd7-92cb-a43fd7a08dde"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">JavaScript Rhino</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[var prj = com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.Application.getInstance().getProject().getPrimaryProject();
var ver = com.nomagic.magicdraw.core.ProjectUtilities.getVersion(prj);
ver.getName();]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="1_expression_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Case 11. Server Project Version in Diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> Click <strong>OK</strong>.</span></li></ol><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><br /></span></p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">To display the Server Project Version in a Diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Create the <strong>Note</strong> symbol in the diagram and </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">anchor it to the diagram.<br /></span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Right-click the <strong>Note</strong> symbol and deselect <strong>Represent Diagram Context (Package)</strong>.<br /></span></li><li><span>(Optional) Right-click the <strong>Note</strong> symbol and deselect <strong>Apply SysML Callout Style</strong>.</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><br /></span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Right-click the <strong>Note</strong> symbol and select <strong>Edit Compartments</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">In the <strong>Element Properties</strong> tab of the <strong>Compartment Edit</strong> dialog, select the name of the property you created.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="5_edit_compartment.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Case 11. Server Project Version in Diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color: rgb(62,63,64);">OK</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. You can see the property name and the server project version in the <strong>Note</strong> symbol.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="6_display_server_project_version.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Case 11. Server Project Version in Diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ea815d25-8915-4e8d-8447-68b1b6b3121d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1361035242</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt-include" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7cf102d3-32cc-4d79-9f1d-6e4906d9ae99"><ac:parameter ac:name=""><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Case Studies for Querying the Model" /></ac:link></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="nopanel">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995925 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00227: (2026x Refresh1) Case 12. Activity Decomposition Table

- page_id: `303995925`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995925/2026x+Refresh1+Case+12.+Activity+Decomposition+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:26:14.151+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Specifying criteria for querying model > (2026x Refresh1) Case Studies for Querying the Model
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1660070392

1660070413

1660070403

To create an Activity Decomposition Table

1. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Generic Table' space=''] .
2. Set the Element Type to Activity .
3. Set the Scope to the Activity element for which decomposition hierarchy will be displayed.
4. Open the Specification window of the Generic Table and click three dots [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the Query property to edit it.
5. In the **Query** dialog, select **Custom**.
6. Select **Metachain Navigation**.
7. Click **Insert**.
8. Under Metaclass or Stereotype , select CallBehaviorAction , and under Property , select Behavior . [ATTACHMENT filename='1_metachain_navigation.png']
9. Select **Context** > **Reset**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
10. Select **Context** > **Operation from Model**>**Behavior Decomposition**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
11. Select **Element** > **Reset**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
12. Select **Element** > **Contextual Variable**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
13. Select the **Build Hierarchy** check box to recursively execute the specified expression for building a multilevel hierarchy starting from the scope as the root.
14. Click **OK**.

1361105380

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Case Studies for Querying the Model' space='']true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2308f3c6-bf2d-4c59-8bea-45a35f447d5b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1660070392</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5c77a125-2d17-47f6-a5f7-990d84828778"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1660070413</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5a198be4-6a2e-48f0-a551-51a6c56c8c47"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1660070403</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an Activity Decomposition Table</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Create a <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating Generic Table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Generic Table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li><li>Set the <strong>Element Type </strong>to <strong>Activity</strong>.</li><li>Set the <strong>Scope </strong>to the Activity element for which decomposition hierarchy will be displayed.</li><li>Open the <strong>Specification window</strong> of the <strong>Generic Table </strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">and click three dots <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Case 12. Activity Decomposition Table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> next to the </span><strong>Query</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> property to edit it.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the <strong>Query</strong> dialog, select <strong>Custom</strong>.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select <strong>Metachain Navigation</strong>.</span></p></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click <strong>Insert</strong>.</span></li><li>Under <strong>Metaclass or Stereotype</strong>, select <strong>CallBehaviorAction</strong>, and under<strong> Property</strong>, select<strong> Behavior</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="1_metachain_navigation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Case 12. Activity Decomposition Table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select <strong>Context</strong> &gt; <strong>Reset</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="2_reset_context.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Case 12. Activity Decomposition Table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select <strong>Context</strong> &gt; <strong>Operation from Model</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> &gt; </span><strong>Behavior Decomposition</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3_context_behavior_decomposition.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Case 12. Activity Decomposition Table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select <strong>Element</strong> &gt; <strong>Reset</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="4_element_reset.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Case 12. Activity Decomposition Table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select <strong>Element</strong> &gt; <strong>Contextual Variable</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="5_element_contextual_variable.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Case 12. Activity Decomposition Table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select the <strong>Build Hierarchy</strong> check box to recursively execute the specified expression for building a multilevel hierarchy starting from the scope as the root.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click <strong>OK</strong>.</span></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ce2296f6-bc40-4c66-b894-30efcd2f9b15"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1361105380</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt-include" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c51e0a3f-25e4-4bd4-a6cb-f5a91019fe19"><ac:parameter ac:name=""><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Case Studies for Querying the Model" /></ac:link></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="nopanel">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995973 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00228: (2026x Refresh1) Case 13. Coloring Associations between Block and Use Case

- page_id: `303995973`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995973/2026x+Refresh1+Case+13.+Coloring+Associations+between+Block+and+Use+Case
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:26:16.785+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Specifying criteria for querying model > (2026x Refresh1) Case Studies for Querying the Model
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1904151418

1904151438

1904151428

Prerequisites:

To color Associations between a Block and a Use Case

1. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Legends' space=''] and a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating and managing Legend Items' space=''] .
2. Right-click the Legend Item and open its Specification window .
3. Click three dots [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] next to the Adornment property to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying Legend Item adornment' space=''] . Click OK .
4. Click three dots [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] next to the Elements by Condition property to edit it. Elements by Condition dialog opens.
5. In the Elements by Condition dialog, set the Element Type to Association .
6. Select **Create Operation** > **Operation from Model** > **IfThenElse**. If you cannot see the **IfThenElse** operation, make sure the **Apply Filter** box is not checked. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. Select IfThenElse > Condition > Reset . [ATTACHMENT filename='2_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
8. Select Condition > Type Test . [ATTACHMENT filename='3_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
9. Set the Operation Name to ‘1st End is Actor’ and Type to Block . [ATTACHMENT filename='4_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
10. Select Condition > Context > Reset . [ATTACHMENT filename='5_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
11. Select Condition > Context > Operation from Model > First End of Association . [ATTACHMENT filename='6_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
12. Select **Condition** > **Context** > **Association** > **Reset**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
13. Select **Association** > **Contextual Variable**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
14. Select IfThenElse > Then > Type Test . [ATTACHMENT filename='9_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
15. Set the Operation Name to ‘2nd end is Use Case’ and Type to UseCase . [ATTACHMENT filename='10_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
16. Select Then > Context > Reset . [ATTACHMENT filename='11_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
17. Select Then > Context > Operation from Model > Second End of Association . [ATTACHMENT filename='12_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
18. Select **Then** > **Context** > **Association** > **Reset**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
19. Select **Association** > **Contextual Variable**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
20. Select IfThenElse > Else > Operation from Model > IfThenElse . [ATTACHMENT filename='15_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
21. Select Else > Condition > Reset . [ATTACHMENT filename='16_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
22. Select Condition > Type Test . [ATTACHMENT filename='17_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
23. Set the Operation Name to ‘1st End is Use Case’ and Type to UseCase . [ATTACHMENT filename='18_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
24. Select Condition > Context > Reset . [ATTACHMENT filename='19_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
25. Select Condition > Context > Operation from Model > First End of Association. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
26. Select **Condition** > **Context** > **Association** > **Reset**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
27. Select **Association** > **Contextual Variable**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
28. Select Else > Then > Type Test . [ATTACHMENT filename='23_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
29. Set the Operation Name to ‘2nd End is Actor’ and Type to Block . [ATTACHMENT filename='24_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
30. Select Then > Context > Reset . [ATTACHMENT filename='25_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
31. Select Then > Context > Operation from Model > Second End of Association . [ATTACHMENT filename='26_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
32. Select **Then** > **Context** > **Association** > **Reset**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
33. Select **Association** > **Contextual Variable**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
34. Select Then > Else > Boolean > false . [ATTACHMENT filename='29_Coloring_Associations_between_Block_and_Use_Case.png']
35. Click OK .

1904151417

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Case Studies for Querying the Model' space='']true

1361152895

To create the First End Of Association operation for coloring Associations between a Block and a Use Case

1. Create an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opaque Behavior' space='MED'] named 'First End Of Association'.
2. Right-click the Opaque Behavior element and open its Specification window . Do one of the following:
  1. Click the plus sign next to the **Owned Parameter** property to edit it. If you cannot see the****Owned Parameter****property, make sure the **Expert** mode is enabled.
  2. On the left side menu, select Parameters > Create .
3. In the Specification of Parameter window , set Name to Association , Type to Association , and Direction to in . Click Back .
4. In the Opaque Behavior Specification window , click three dots [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the **Body and Language** property to edit it.
5. In the Body and Language dialog, select Language > StructuredExpression .
6. Click **Create operation** > **Script**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. Set the Operation Name to ‘First End’.
8. From the Language drop-down list, select Javascript Rhino .
9. Insert the following script as the **Body**: jsJavascript Rhino0 ? arg1.iterator().next() : null]]> [IMAGE alt='' src='']
10. Select First End > arg1 > Reset . [ATTACHMENT filename='3_First_End_Of_Association.png']
11. Select First End > arg1 > Metachain Navigation . [ATTACHMENT filename='4_First_End_Of_Association.png']
12. Click Insert .
13. Under Metaclass or Stereotype , select Association , and under Property , select End Type . [ATTACHMENT filename='5_First_End_Of_Association.png']
14. Click OK .

1361152883

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Case Studies for Querying the Model' space='']true

To create the Second End Of Association operation for coloring Associations between a Block and a Use Case

1. Create an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opaque Behavior' space='MED'] named 'Second End Of Association'.
2. Right-click the Opaque Behavior element and open its Specification window . Do one of the following:
  1. Click the plus sign next to the **Owned Parameter** property to edit it. If you cannot see the****Owned Parameter****property, make sure the **Expert** mode is enabled.
  2. On the left side menu, select Parameters > Create .
3. In the Specification of Parameter window , set Name to Association , Type to Association , and Direction to in . Click Back .
4. In the Opaque Behavior Specification window , click three dots [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the **Body and Language** property to edit it.
5. In the Body and Language dialog, select Language > StructuredExpression .
6. Click **Create operation** > **Script**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. Set the Operation Name to ‘Second End’.
8. From the Language drop-down list, select Javascript Rhino .
9. Insert the following script as the **Body**: jsJavascript Rhino [IMAGE alt='' src='']
10. Select Second End > arg1 > Reset . [ATTACHMENT filename='3_Second_End_Of_Association.png']
11. Select Second End > arg1 > Metachain Navigation . [ATTACHMENT filename='4_Second_End_Of_Association.png']
12. Click Insert .
13. Under Metaclass or Stereotype , select Association , and under Property , select End Type . [ATTACHMENT filename='5_Second_End_Of_Association.png']
14. Click OK .

## PAGE 00231: (2026x Refresh1) Case 14. Incoming Association of Class

- page_id: `303995952`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995952/2026x+Refresh1+Case+14.+Incoming+Association+of+Class
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:26:15.811+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Specifying criteria for querying model > (2026x Refresh1) Case Studies for Querying the Model
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1361199137

1361199148

1361199147

To get Incoming Associations of a Class

1. Right-click a Class element and open the Specification window > Traceability > Create .
2. Set the Name to 'Incoming associations'.
3. Click **Create operation** > **Filter**.
4. Select Input > Metachain Navigation .
5. Click Insert .
6. Under Metaclass or Stereotype , select Class, and under Property , select _associationOfEndType . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-30_13-48-40.png']
7. Select Predicate > Operation from Model > Equals . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-30_13-49-54.png']
8. Select A > Metachain Navigation . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2022-1-4_17-25-30.png']
9. Click Insert .
10. Under Metaclass or Stereotype , select Property, and under Property , select Type . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2022-1-4_17-27-8.png']
11. Select Context > Reset . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2022-1-4_17-27-49.png']
12. Select Context > Filter . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2022-1-4_17-34-13.png']
13. Select**Input** > **Simple Navigation**. Select **Member End** and set **Is Applied** to **true**, and **Direction** to **Source To Target**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] If you cannot see the **Member End******property, make sure the 'Show relations criteria available only for context' check box is not selected.
14. Select Predicate > Nested Operation . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2022-1-4_17-37-28.png']
15. Select Body > Simple Navigation . Select isNavigable() (Port) and set Is Applied to true , and Direction to Source To Target . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2022-1-4_17-42-6.png']
16. Select B > Contextual Variable . Set the Value to THIS . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2022-1-4_17-42-51.png']
17. Click OK .

1361199136

## PAGE 00232: (2026x Refresh1) Case 15. Requirements Coverage by Design Elements

- page_id: `303996027`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996027/2026x+Refresh1+Case+15.+Requirements+Coverage+by+Design+Elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:26:19.998+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Specifying criteria for querying model > (2026x Refresh1) Case Studies for Querying the Model
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: JavaScript Rhino

1361225459

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: JavaScript Rhino

1361225479

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: JavaScript Rhino

1361225469

Prerequisites:

To create a Requirements Coverage by Design Elements Legend

1. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Legends' space=''] .
2. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating and managing Legend Items' space=''] named '0%-25%'.
3. Right-click the Legend Item and open its Specification window .
4. C lick three dots [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the Elements by Condition property to edit it.
5. In the **Elements by Condition**dialog, set the **Element Type** to **Requirement**.
6. Rename **Custom** to **Query**.
7. Select Create Operation > Operation from Model > All Leaf Requirements Owned Recursively . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_10-1-24.png']
8. Select Input > Reset . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_10-1-45.png']
9. Select Input > Contextual Variable . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_10-2-20.png']
10. Select All Leaf Requirements Owned Recursively > Edit > Copy . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_10-4-49.png']
11. Select the root node Query > Edit > Paste . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_10-7-45.png']
12. Select the copied operation > Use as... > Input of a New Filter . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_10-10-10.png']
13. Select **Predicate** > **Nested Operation**. [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_10-11-40.png']
14. Select **Body** > **Operation from Model** > **Not**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
15. Select **A** > **Reset**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
16. Select **A** > **Operation from Model** > **isEmpty**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
17. Select Input > Simple Navigation . Select Satisfied By and set Is Applied to true , and Direction to Source To Target . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_10-30-59.png']
18. Select All Leaf Requirements Owned Recursively > Use as... > arg of a new Script .
19. From the Language drop-down list, select Javascript Rhino .
20. Insert the following script as the **B****ody**: jsJavaScript RhinolowerBound && arg2.size()/arg1.size() <= upperBound]]> [IMAGE alt='' src='']
21. Drag-and-drop Filter onto Create parameter to create a second parameter for the Script . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_12-6-19.png']
22. Select Filter > Remove . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_12-7-26.png']
23. Select **Create parameter** > **Element** > select the **Legend Item** for which the expression is defined. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''][IMAGE alt='' src=''] If you cannot see the **Legend Item**, make sure the **Search for**option is set to**Any Element**.
24. Clone the created Legend Item and rename the new items accordingly:
  1. 25%-50%
  2. 50%-75%
  3. 75%-100%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1361225457

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Case Studies for Querying the Model' space='']true

````html
var upperBound = arg3.name.slice(arg3.name.indexOf("-")+1,arg3.name.lastIndexOf("%"))/100 

arg2.size()/arg1.size() > lowerBound && arg2.size()/arg1.size() <= upperBound]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="366" ac:width="944"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2021-12-31_12-1-59.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Case 15. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997859 space=MT version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 00233: (2026x Refresh1) Case 15.1. All Leaf Requirements Owned Recursively

- page_id: `303997859`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997859/2026x+Refresh1+Case+15.1.+All+Leaf+Requirements+Owned+Recursively
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:28:39.755+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Specifying criteria for querying model > (2026x Refresh1) Case Studies for Querying the Model > (2026x Refresh1) Case 15. Requirements Coverage by Design Elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1361231412

1361231432

1361231422

To collect all Leaf Requirements Owned Recursively

1. Create an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opaque Behavior' space='MED'] named 'All Leaf Requirements Owned Recursively'.
2. Right-click the Opaque Behavior element/symbol and open its Specification window . Do one of the following:
  1. Click the plus sign next to the **Owned Parameter** property to edit it. If you cannot see the****Owned Parameter****property, make sure the **Expert** mode is enabled.
  2. On the left side menu, select Parameters > Create .
3. In the Specification of Parameter window , set Name to input , Type to Class , and Direction to in . Click Back .
4. In the Opaque Behavior Specification window , click three dots [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the **Body and Language** property to edit it.
5. In the Body and Language dialog, select Language > StructuredExpression .
6. Click **Create Operation** > **Filter**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. Select Input > Implied Relation . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_9-24-4.png']
8. Select Element and set the Value to input . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_9-24-25.png']
9. Select Criteria > Body > Reset . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_9-25-33.png']
10. Select Criteria > Body > Contextual Variable . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_9-26-13.png']
11. Select Source > Body > Simple Navigation > Clear All . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_9-28-17.png']
12. Select Source > Body > Simple Navigation . Select Owned Element and set Is Applied to true , Direction to Source To Target , and Result Type to Requirement (select Include Subtypes check box). [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_9-29-7.png']
13. Select Target > Reset . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_9-29-57.png']
14. Select Predicate > Nested Operation . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_9-31-13.png']
15. Select **Body** > **Operation from Model** > **isEmpty**. If you cannot see the **isEmpty**operation, make sure the **Apply Filter** box is not checked. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
16. Select Input > Simple Navigation . Select Owned Element and set Is Applied to true , Direction to Source To Target , and Result Type to Requirement . [ATTACHMENT filename='image2021-12-31_9-32-28.png']
17. Click OK .

1361231410

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Case Studies for Querying the Model' space='']true

All Leaf Requirements Owned Recursively" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Select <strong>Target</strong> &gt; <strong>Reset</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="294" ac:width="938"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2021-12-31_9-29-57.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Case 15.1. All Leaf Requirements Owned Recursively" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Select <strong>Predicate</strong> &gt; <strong>Nested Operation</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="302" ac:width="579"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2021-12-31_9-31-13.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Case 15.1. All Leaf Requirements Owned Recursively" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select <strong>Body</strong> &gt; <strong>Operation from Model</strong> &gt; <strong>isEmpty</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="484b1b3b-85cb-4c67-9e41-31d82642e74a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you cannot see the <strong>isEmpty </strong>operation, make sure the <strong>Apply Filter</strong> box is not checked.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="414" ac:width="672"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2021-12-31_9-31-41.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Case 15.1. All Leaf Requirements Owned Recursively" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li>Select <strong>Input</strong> &gt; <strong>Simple Navigation</strong>. Select <strong>Owned Element</strong> and set <strong>Is Applied</strong> to <strong>true</strong>, <strong>Direction</strong> to <strong>Source To Target</strong>, and <strong>Result Type</strong> to <strong>Requirement</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="196" ac:width="944"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2021-12-31_9-32-28.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Case 15.1. All Leaf Requirements Owned Recursively" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="89f952c1-72f9-47cf-bf5d-ee0e4f9651de"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1361231410</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt-include" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a3d03dae-2748-4379-b30e-36ee41881f02"><ac:parameter ac:name=""><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Case Studies for Querying the Model" /></ac:link></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="nopanel">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303996021 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00234: (2026x Refresh1) Case 16. Using the Opposite Reference to find element usages as Tag Values

- page_id: `303996021`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996021/2026x+Refresh1+Case+16.+Using+the+Opposite+Reference+to+find+element+usages+as+Tag+Values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:26:18.893+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Specifying criteria for querying model > (2026x Refresh1) Case Studies for Querying the Model
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

33975345

33975365

33975355

To use the Opposite Reference to find element usages as Tag Values

1. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Generic table' space=''] .
2. Set the Element Type to the element types whose usages you want to find (e.g., Block, Part Property, etc.).
3. Set the Scope where the element usages will be searched for.
4. Click the Columns button and select New Custom Column . The Expression dialog opens.
5. Set the Name for the Custom Column (e.g., usages as Tagged Values ).
6. Select Metachain Navigation .
7. Click **Insert**.
8. Under Metaclass or Stereotype , select Element , and under Property , select _elementTaggedValue .
9. Click Insert again.
10. Under Metaclass or Stereotype , select ElementTaggedValue , and under Property , select Tagged Value Owner .
11. Click **OK**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

33975344

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Case Studies for Querying the Model' space='']true
