<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999192 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00255: (2026x Refresh1) Changes in properties and metaclasses UAF 1.2

- page_id: `303999192`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999192/2026x+Refresh1+Changes+in+properties+and+metaclasses+UAF+1.2
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:30:40.223+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Migration User Guides > (2026x Refresh1) UPDM projects migration manual > (2026x Refresh1) UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.2 > (2026x Refresh1) UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping issues and solutions
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Enduring Task removed

Enduring Task element was removed and after the migration it become Strategic Phase.

##### Desired Effect changes

Desired Effect renamed to Desires. After the migration relationship is removed because the supplier has changed (from Desired State to Actual State).

##### Changes for properties of Actual Enterprise Phase

- Vision property was removed, so after the migration Phases relationship was created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Enterprise Vision
- Goal property was removed, so after the migration Phases relationship was created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Enterprise Goal

##### Changes for property of *Enterprise Goal*

- Enterprise Phase property was removed, so after the migration Phases relationship was created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Enterprise Goal

##### Changes for *property of* *Enterprise Vision*

- Enterprise Phase property was removed, so after the migration Phases relationship was created between Actual Enterprise Phase and Enterprise Vision

##### Changed Property Types

In earlier versions, the Benefit property (owned by Enterprise Goal) was String. In UAF 1.3 Benefit property has Value Item for its value.After migration, Value Item element will be created based on Benefit value.

##### Changed for Whole Life Enterprise

In earlier version, WholeLieEneterprise was Class. In UAF 1.3 WholeLieEneterprise will be Instance Specification. After migration, Whole Life Enterprise becomes Strategic Phase.

**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Enduring Task removed</h3><p>Enduring Task element was removed and after the migration it become Strategic Phase. </p><h3>Desired Effect changes</h3><p>Desired Effect renamed to Desires. After the migration relationship is removed because the supplier has changed (from Desired State to Actual State).</p><h3><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="bac8e30d-4e38-434c-8fb0-f4002fd53aba">Changes for properties of  Actual Enterprise Phase</ac:inline-comment-marker></h3><ul><li><strong>Vision</strong> property was removed, so after the migration <em>Phases</em> relationship was created between <em>Actual Enterprise Phase</em> and <em>Enterprise Vision</em></li><li><strong>Goal</strong> property was removed, so after the migration <em>Phases</em> relationship was created between <em>Actual Enterprise Phase</em> and <em>Enterprise Goal</em></li></ul><h3>Changes for property of <em><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Enterprise Goal</span></em></h3><ul><li><strong>Enterprise Phase </strong>property was removed, so after the migration <em>Phases</em> relationship was created between <em>Actual Enterprise Phase</em> and <em>Enterprise Goal</em></li></ul><h3>Changes for <em style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">property of </span></em> <em style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Enterprise Vision </span></em></h3><ul><li><strong>Enterprise Phase </strong>property was removed, so after the migration <em>Phases</em> relationship was created between <em>Actual Enterprise Phase</em> and <em>Enterprise Vision</em></li></ul><h3><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="230554ab-0e82-49cc-86d0-15b6d571d8e6">Changed Property Types</ac:inline-comment-marker></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In earlier versions, the Benefit property (owned by Enterprise Goal) was String. In UAF 1.3  Benefit property <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">has Value Item for its value. </span>After migration, Value Item element will be created based on Benefit value. </span></p><h3><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c23d762c-2384-4095-9a5f-a424f761adb7">Changed for Whole Life Enterprise</ac:inline-comment-marker></h3><p>In earlier version, WholeLieEneterprise was Class. In UAF 1.3 WholeLieEneterprise  will be Instance Specification. After migration, Whole Life Enterprise becomes Strategic Phase. </p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><em><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><br /></span></em></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999025 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00256: (2026x Refresh1) Changes in Security Traceability matrices

- page_id: `303999025`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999025/2026x+Refresh1+Changes+in+Security+Traceability+matrices
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:30:23.484+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Migration User Guides > (2026x Refresh1) UAF projects migration manual > (2026x Refresh1) Migration from UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.1 > (2026x Refresh1) Migration from UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.0
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Together with the UAF 1.0 profile specification release, the following changes were implemented:

| UAF 1.0 beta | UAF 1.0 |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Old matrix | Client / Supplier | New matrix | Client / Supplier |
| Security Affect Traceability [Deprecated] | Risk / Asset Role | Risks to Assets Mapping | Risk / Asset |
| Security Mitigation Traceability [Deprecated] | Asset Role / Risk | Security Controls to Risks Mapping | Security Control / Risk |

The old matrices are not migrated, although they are left in the model. To continue modeling the Security Traceability matrices, please create the new ones.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target">Together with the UAF 1.0 profile specification release, the following changes were implemented:</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th style="text-align: center;" colspan="2">UAF 1.0 beta</th><th style="text-align: center;" colspan="2">UAF 1.0</th></tr><tr><th style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">Old matrix</th><th style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">Client / Supplier</th><th style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">New matrix</th><th style="text-align: center;" colspan="1">Client / Supplier</th></tr><tr><td>Security Affect Traceability [Deprecated]</td><td colspan="1">Risk / Asset Role</td><td>Risks to Assets Mapping</td><td colspan="1">Risk / Asset</td></tr><tr><td>Security Mitigation Traceability [Deprecated]</td><td colspan="1">Asset Role / Risk</td><td>Security Controls to Risks Mapping</td><td colspan="1">Security Control / Risk</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a032a32f-34b8-467d-9c29-d11916a4940a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The old matrices are not migrated, although they are left in the model. To continue modeling the Security Traceability matrices, please create the new ones.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 00257: (2026x Refresh1) Changes of element metaclass and type

- page_id: `303996806`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996806/2026x+Refresh1+Changes+of+element+metaclass+and+type

- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following table describes element metaclasses mapping between UAF 1.0 and UAF 1.1.

| Element | UAF 1.0 [Metaclass] | UAF 1.1 [Metaclass] |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AssetRole | Element | Property |
| Desirer | Class | Element |

###### Changes types of Elements

The following table describes element metaclasses mapping between UAF 1.0 and UAF 1.1.

| Element/Property | UAF 1.0 [Type] | UAF 1.1 [Type] |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Activity::activityPerformableUnderCondition | Condition | ActualCondition |
| Measurement::environmentalContext | Condition | ActualCondition |
| LocationHolder::requiredEnvironment | Environment | ActualEnvironment |
| CapabilityConfiguration::doctrine | Constraint | StandardOperationalActivity |

For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Mapping solutions and issues' space=''].

## PAGE 00258: (2026x Refresh1) Changes of element types and metaclasses

- page_id: `303999073`
- space_key: `MT`

- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following table describes element types mapping between UPDM 2.1 and UAF 1.1.

| Element/Property | UPDM 2.1 [Type] | UAF 1.1 [Type] |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Activity::activityPerformableUnderCondition | Condition | ActualCondition |
| LocationHolder::requiredEnvironment | Environment | ActualEnvironment |
| CapabilityConfiguration::doctrine | Constraint | StandardOperationalActivity |

The following table describes element metaclasses mapping between UPDM 2.1 and UAF 1.1.

| Element | UPDM 2.1 [Metaclass] | UAF 1.1 [Metaclass] |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Capable Element | Class | Element |
| Desirer | Class | Element |
| Enduring Task | Activity | Class |
| ExhibitsMaps To Capability | Dependency | Abstraction |
| Known Resource | Property | Class |
| Project | Instance Specification | Class |
| View | Package | Class |
| Viewpoint | Package | Class |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994410 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00259: (2026x Refresh1) Changing a path style

- page_id: `303994410`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994410/2026x+Refresh1+Changing+a+path+style
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:12.895+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagramming > (2026x Refresh1) Working with paths and relationships
- labels: ['path-style', 'rectilinear', 'oblique', 'bezier', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To change a path style, do either

- Right-click a path(s) on the diagram pane and select Reset Labels Positions or Remove Break Points .
- Select a path(s) on the diagram pane and on the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_break_points.png'] , [ATTACHMENT filename='reset_labels_positions.png'] , or [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
- Select a path(s) on the diagram pane and on the Edit menu click Paths , and then choose one of the following:
  - Path Style to select one of the path styles - rectilinear, oblique, or bezier.
  - Change Path Style (shortcut keys Ctrl+L) to change the path style to rectilinear, oblique, or bezier.
  - Reset Labels Positions to reset the changed path labels position to default.
  - Remove Break Points to remove all angles of the path.
- Right-click a path(s) on the diagram pane and select Symbol Properties . In the Symbol Properties dialog, change the Path Style property value by selecting rectilinear, oblique, or bezier.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To change a path style, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Right-click a path(s) on the diagram pane and select <strong>Reset Labels Positions</strong> or <strong>Remove Break Points</strong>.</li><li>Select a path(s) on the diagram pane and on the diagram toolbar, click <ac:image ac:title="Remove Break Points" ac:alt="Remove Break Points"><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_break_points.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>, <ac:image ac:title="Reset Labels Positions" ac:alt="Reset Labels Positions"><ri:attachment ri:filename="reset_labels_positions.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>, or<strong> <ac:image ac:title="Rectilinear" ac:alt="Rectilinear"><ri:attachment ri:filename="rectilinear.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong>.</li><li>Select a path(s) on the diagram pane and on the <strong>Edit</strong> menu click <strong>Paths</strong>, and then choose one of the following:<br /><ul><li><strong>Path Style</strong> to select one of the path styles - rectilinear, oblique, or bezier.</li><li><strong>Change Path Style</strong> (shortcut keys Ctrl+L) to change the path style to rectilinear, oblique, or bezier.</li><li><strong>Reset Labels Positions </strong>to reset the changed path labels position to default.</li><li><strong>Remove Break Points</strong> to remove all angles of the path.</li></ul></li><li>Right-click a path(s) on the diagram pane and select <strong>Symbol Properties</strong>. In the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog, change the <strong>Path Style</strong> property value by selecting rectilinear, oblique, or bezier.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000533 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00260: (2026x Refresh1) Changing between UAF and UPDM 2 plugins

- page_id: `304000533`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000533/2026x+Refresh1+Changing+between+UAF+and+UPDM+2+plugins
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:32:24.117+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Before changing the plugin

Before changing from one plugin to another, please do the following:

1. your projects. We recommend that you back up your projects in case you need to return to your previously used plugin version (UPDM2 or UAF).
2. all [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Configuration files' space=''] in your configuration directory. We recommend making a backup if you intend to switch back.
3. You will need both UPDM 2 and UAF licenses to change between plugins. You will get both licenses during the purchase process.

- If the UAF plugin is installed, the UPDM 2 projects need to be migrated to UAF. We recommend that you make a backup of the project before the migration. Do not forget to migrate used projects before migrating the main project.
- You will not be able to migrate a UAF project back to UPDM 2.
- You will not be able to open UAF projects if the UPDM 2 plugin is installed (only applies to MagicDraw and Magic Software Architect).
- You can install only one plugin in one MagicDraw (Magic Software Architect) modeling tool instance (choose UAF 1.3 or UPDM2).

To learn which plugin is installed/used, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using UAF and UPDM2 plugins in Cameo Enterprise Architecture' space='']

##### Changing between the UAF and UPDM 2 plugins for MagicDraw + plugins users

If you are using MagicDraw with plugins, you will need to install the new plugin via the **Resource/Plugin Manager**.

- For information about how to install the plugin, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing plugins' space='IL'] .
- Please note that only one plugin (UAF 1.3 or UPDM 2) can be installed.

##### Switching between the UAF and UPDM 2 plugins for Cameo Enterprise Architecture users

If you are working with Cameo Enterprise Architecture, the UAF plugin is already installed and the UPDM 2 installation file comes together with the Cameo Enterprise Architecture installation files. You will be able to switch between the UAF and UPDM 2 plugins.

To switch between plugins

1. In the modeling tool's main menu, select Help > Manage UPDM 2 / UAF Plugins .
2. Select the plugin you need.
3. Restart the modeling tool.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### What happens after changing between plugins

When you change the plugin:

1. The projects are not migrated automatically.
2. The configuration files are overwritten. If you have customized the diagram descriptors, perspectives, etc., we recommend making a backup if you intend to switch back.
3. The plugin is overwritten (with profiles, templates, etc.) in case of installation. We recommend that you back up your projects in case you need to return to your previously used plugin version (UPDM2 or UAF).

##### backupHow to back up your configuration files and projects

To back up the configuration files

1. In the modeling tool's main menu, click Help > About
2. In the About dialog, click Environment .
3. In the **Environment** tab, click a link near the Configuration Files . The window with your configuration files opens.
4. Select the folder of your current version and make a copy of it (add to the archive, for example, .zip).
5. Place the copied files in a safe location.

To back up the local projects

1. Gather all the projects you want to back up in one folder.
2. Make a copy of that folder or add it to an archive (for example, .zip).
3. Place the copied files in a safe location.

##### How to restore from the backup

If you need to switch back to working with your previously used plugin (UAF or UPDM2), you can do so by restoring the backups you made before switching. You will need to restore configuration files as well as projects.

To restore the configuration files from backup

1. Close the modeling tool.
2. Extract the archived configuration files.
3. Replace the current configuration files with the extracted ones.

##### Installing two modeling tools on one machine

In case you need to work with UPDM 2 and UAF projects on the same machine, you will need to have two modeling tool installations. One of them should have UPDM 2 Plugin and the second - UAF Plugin installed.

When working with two or more installations of the same modeling toolversion, you must have individual configuration files directory for each installation (by default, all the installations of the same modeling tool version use a single directory, for example, *<...>\.cameoea\19.0*).

In such case, the initial installation of a certain modeling tool version can keep on using the default configuration files directory, that is, *<...>\.cameoea\<version number>*, but you must choose a different configuration files location for each subsequent installation (see the following section).

##### customStoring configuration files in a custom location

#### NOTE: Note

Note

If you have defined to store files in the modeling tool installation directory, files will not be stored in the defined location.

To store modeling tool configuration files in a custom location

Do either:

1. Open the <modeling tool name>.properties file, which is located in <modeling tool installation directory>\bin .
2. In the JAVA_ARGS line, add the following property: -Dlocalconfig.location=<absolute path to a custom location> Sample property value-Dlocalconfig.location\=E\:\\temp\\innerPack

Or:

1. Go to the directory, where [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Configuration files' space=''] .
2. Create a new file named *redirected.location*.
3. In the file, type the absolute path to the directory, wherein modeling configuration files will be saved, e.g., C:/<directory name>.

The path in both options (*localconfig.location* property/*redirected.location* file) can contain system/environment/application related variables.

| Context of the variable | Format of the variable | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Java system property | <sys:variableName> | <sys:user.name> |
| OS environment variable | <env:variableName> | <env:USERNAME> |
| Application related variable | <app:variableName> | <app:version> |

There are only two application related variables:

| Application related variable | Description | Value example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| version | Full version of the application | 2024x Refresh3 |
| default.config.dir.name | Default modeling tool configuration directory name (.<modeling tool name>) | .magicdraw (see ) |

#### TIP: Sample path with variables

Sample path with variables

```text
<env:APPDATA>/configurations/<app:default.config.dir.name>/<sys:user.name>/<app:version>
```

##### The installation workflow

To install two modeling tools with different plugin versions

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing modeling tools' space='IL'] the first modeling tool.
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Configuration files' space=''] for the modeling tool configuration files (create a new folder for that).
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing modeling tools' space='IL'] the second modeling tool to a different location.
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Configuration files' space=''] for the modeling tool configuration files (create another folder for that).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

If you have customized the diagram descriptors, perspectives, etc., we recommend making a backup if you intend to switch back. </li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">The plugin is overwritten (with profiles, templates, etc.) in case of installation.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="971ba737-e913-4ad6-8607-73e747720adc"><ac:rich-text-body><p>We recommend that you back up your projects in case you need to return to your previously used plugin version (UPDM2 or UAF).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0e90b719-98ce-43ce-8d8b-fa934988aca0"><ac:parameter ac:name="">backup</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>How to back up your configuration files and projects</h3><p>To back up the configuration files</p><hr /><ol><li>In the modeling tool's main menu, click <strong>Help </strong>&gt;<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><strong>About</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></li><li>In the <strong>About</strong> dialog, click <strong>Environment</strong>.</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>Environment</strong> tab, click a link near the </span><strong>Configuration Files</strong>. The window with your configuration files opens.</li><li>Select the folder of your current version and make a copy of it (add to the archive, for example, .zip).</li><li>Place the copied files in a safe location.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To back up the local projects</p><hr /><ol><li>Gather all the projects you want to back up in one folder.</li><li>Make a copy of that folder or add it to an archive (for example, .zip).</li><li>Place the copied files in a safe location.</li></ol><h3>How to restore from the backup</h3><p class="auto-cursor-target">If you need to switch back to working with your previously used plugin (UAF or UPDM2), you can do so by restoring the backups you made before switching. You will need to restore configuration files as well as projects.</p><p><br /></p><p>To restore the configuration files from backup</p><hr /><ol><li>Close the modeling tool.</li><li>Extract the archived configuration files.</li><li>Replace the current configuration files with the extracted ones.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Installing two modeling tools on one machine</h3><p>In case you need to work with UPDM 2 and UAF projects on the same machine, you will need to have two modeling tool installations. One of them should have UPDM 2 Plugin and the second - UAF Plugin installed.</p><p>When working with two or more installations of the same <span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">modeling tool</span> </span>version, you must have individual configuration files directory for each installation (by default, all the installations of the same <span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">modeling tool</span> </span> version use a single directory, for example, <em>&lt;...&gt;\.cameoea\19.0</em>).</p><p>In such case, the initial installation of a certain <span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">modeling tool</span> </span> version can keep on using the default configuration files directory, that is, <em>&lt;...&gt;\.cameoea\&lt;version number&gt;</em>, but you must choose a different configuration files location for each subsequent installation (see the following section).</p><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8f1fed61-4306-4eff-abdd-4fe871dedb79"><ac:parameter ac:name="">custom</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Storing configuration files in a custom location<br /><br /></h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a9e64a11-d251-4e2c-a18f-a287f0631189"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you have defined to store files in the modeling tool installation directory, files will not be stored in the defined location.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To store modeling tool configuration files in a custom location</p><hr /><p>Do either:</p><ol><li>Open the <em>&lt;modeling tool name&gt;.properties</em> file, which is located in <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\bin</em>.</li><li>In the JAVA_ARGS line, add the following property:<br /><pre> -Dlocalconfig.location=&lt;absolute path to a custom location&gt;<br /><br /></pre><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c3647347-253c-4233-adbd-db8af1c6d06b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Sample property value</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>-Dlocalconfig.location\=E\:\\temp\\innerPack</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br />Or:</p><ol><li>Go to the directory, where <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Configuration files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[the configuration files are stored]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><a>.</a></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Create a new file named <em>redirected.location</em>.</p></li><li>In the file, type the absolute path to the directory, wherein modeling configuration files will be saved, e.g., C:/&lt;directory name&gt;.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>The path in both options (<em>localconfig.location</em> property/<em>redirected.location</em> file) can contain system/environment/application related variables. </p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Context of the variable</th><th>Format of the variable</th><th>Example</th></tr><tr><td>Java system property</td><td>&lt;sys:variableName&gt;</td><td>&lt;sys:user.name&gt;</td></tr><tr><td>OS environment variable</td><td>&lt;env:variableName&gt;</td><td>&lt;env:USERNAME&gt;</td></tr><tr><td>Application related variable</td><td>&lt;app:variableName&gt;</td><td>&lt;app:version&gt;</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p>There are only two application related variables:</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Application related variable</th><th>Description</th><th>Value example</th></tr><tr><td>version</td><td>Full version of the application</td><td>2024x Refresh3</td></tr><tr><td>default.config.dir.name</td><td>Default modeling tool configuration directory name (.<span>&lt;modeling tool name&gt;</span>)</td><td>.magicdraw (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Configuration files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Modeling tool file naming]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>)</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b0a1eb04-9765-4a56-87b3-4ff3ac2a34fa"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Sample path with variables</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><pre>&lt;env:APPDATA&gt;/configurations/&lt;app:default.config.dir.name&gt;/&lt;sys:user.name&gt;/&lt;app:version&gt;</pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>The installation workflow </h3><p>To install two modeling tools with different plugin versions</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL" ri:content-title="Installing modeling tools" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Install]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the first modeling tool.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Configuration files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Change the predefined location]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for the modeling tool configuration files (create a new folder for that).</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL" ri:content-title="Installing modeling tools" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Install ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>the second modeling tool to a different location.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Configuration files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Change the predefined location]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for the modeling tool configuration files (create another folder for that).</li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993068 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00261: (2026x Refresh1) Changing diagram type

- page_id: `303993068`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993068/2026x+Refresh1+Changing+diagram+type
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:22:45.709+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagramming
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The diagram type can be changed to another type of diagram if both diagram types are compatible.

#### NOTE: Diagram Elements

Diagram Elements

Diagram elements are not converted.

###### **Uses for changing the diagram type:**

- To migrate with the existing project to a diagram type that was not available until then. For example, migrating a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Class diagram' space=''] to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SysML Block Definition Diagram' space=''] .
- To migrate with the existing project from the diagram type, which will be erased from your project. For example, if the user has decided to erase a plugin, they would need to convert that plugin's specific diagram to standard diagrams.

###### **Diagram conversion scenarios:**

- Any static diagram can be converted to another static diagram.
- Any dynamic diagram can be converted to another diagram if and only if both diagrams are based on the same diagram type and both diagrams are compatible.

To change the diagram type

1. Select one or more of the same type diagrams in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Model Browser' space=''] (Containment, Structure, or Diagram tree).
2. From the shortcut menu, choose Refactor > Change Type To . In the open dialog, select the desired diagram type. [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-06 at 5.02.07 PM.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target">The diagram type can be changed to another type of diagram if both diagram types are compatible.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d10c128c-e709-43cf-b861-e3c7eba7699c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Diagram Elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Diagram elements are not converted.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4><strong>Uses for changing the diagram type:</strong></h4><ul><li>To migrate with the existing project to a diagram type that was not available until then. For example, migrating a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Class diagram" /></ac:link> to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SysML Block Definition Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML v1 Block Definition diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>To migrate with the existing project from the diagram type, which will be erased from your project. For example, if the user has decided to erase a plugin, they would need to convert that plugin's specific diagram to standard diagrams. </li></ul><p><br /></p><h4><strong>Diagram conversion scenarios:</strong></h4><ul><li>Any static diagram can be converted to another static diagram. </li><li>Any dynamic diagram can be converted to another diagram if and only if both diagrams are based on the same diagram type and both diagrams are compatible.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To change the diagram type</p><hr /><ol><li>Select one or more of the same type diagrams in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Model Browser" /></ac:link> (Containment, Structure, or Diagram tree).</li><li>From the shortcut menu, choose <strong>Refactor </strong>&gt;<strong> Change Type To</strong>. In the open dialog, select the desired diagram type.<br /><ac:image ac:height="400"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-06-06 at 5.02.07 PM.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Changing diagram type" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999582 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00262: (2026x Refresh1) Changing interface style

- page_id: `303999582`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999582/2026x+Refresh1+Changing+interface+style
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:09.343+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Getting started > (2026x Refresh1) Understanding the user interface
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Look and Feel Themes

1426180851

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Look and Feel Themes

1426180854

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Look and Feel Themes

1426180852

#### EXCERPT: Look and Feel Themes

INLINE

You can change the appearance of your modeling tool windows, dialogs, and menus. The **Look and Feel** menu allows you to personalize the user interface using a number of predefined styles and themes. You can also create a custom theme with your favorite colors and fonts.

To change the interface style

1. In the main menu of a modeling tool, select Options > Look and Feel .
2. Select the desired style from the list of available styles.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Look and Feel menu options.

#### NOTE: Important

Important

- The selected style may not look exactly like the style in related operating systems.
- Depending on your operating system, some style options may be unavailable.

##### Creating a custom theme

Define your own interface style by creating a custom theme. The **Custom** theme option is located in the **Look and Feel Themes** submenu which also offers 8 predefined themes. The **Custom** theme allows you to specify text fonts and sizes of the desired interface elements as well as their colors.

To create a custom theme

1. In the main menu of a modeling tool, select Options > Look and Feel > Metal .
2. In the same menu, select Options > Look and Feel > Look and Feel Themes > Custom .
3. In the open dialog, change the values of the desired style properties.
4. Click OK .

#### NOTE: Look and Feel Themes

Look and Feel Themes

The themes listed in the **Look and Feel Themes** submenu are only available if you select the **Metal** style.

1426180850

**Related pages**

You can also create a custom theme with your favorite colors and fonts.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the interface style</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu of a modeling tool, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Look and Feel</strong>.</li><li>Select the desired style from the list of available styles.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="look_and_feel_menu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Changing interface style" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Look and Feel menu options.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="347f5ee8-ee67-4777-82bc-e7674faed378"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The selected style may not look exactly like the style in related operating systems.</li><li>Depending on your operating system, some style options may be unavailable.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><br />Creating a custom theme</h3><p>Define your own interface style by creating a custom theme. The <strong>Custom</strong> theme option is located in the <strong>Look and Feel Themes</strong> submenu which also offers 8 predefined themes. The <strong>Custom</strong> theme allows you to specify text fonts and sizes of the desired interface elements as well as their colors.<br /><br /></p><p>To create a custom theme</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu of a modeling tool, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Look and Feel</strong> &gt;<strong> Metal</strong>.</li><li>In the same menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Look and Feel</strong> &gt;<strong> Look and Feel Themes</strong> &gt;<strong> Custom</strong>.</li><li>In the open dialog, change the values of the desired style properties.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2c726df5-edb3-47c1-b619-b75abdc6764b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Look and Feel Themes</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The themes listed in the <strong>Look and Feel Themes</strong> submenu are only available if you select the <strong>Metal</strong> style.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="00bc8004-9c35-455b-83a0-3ce093c77b92"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1426180850</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Understanding the user interface" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Main menu" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Toolbars" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Model Browser" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Image Library tab" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990085 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00263: (2026x Refresh1) Changing the context

- page_id: `303990085`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990085/2026x+Refresh1+Changing+the+context
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:15.187+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Analysis diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Relation Map
- labels: ['relation-map-change-context', 'relation-map-context', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Context is the element, from which a relation map is drawn and the analysis is started. You can set any element as the context.

To set an element as the context of the relation map

- Drag the selected element from the Model Browser to the relation map.
- In the Criteria area, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='select_context_element_button.png'] button next to the Context box and then select the element in the Select Context Element dialog.
- If the **Make Element as Context on Selection** option is turned on, click any node to make it the new context of the structure. To turn this option on, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the toolbar and select **Make Element as Context on Selection**.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Context is the element, from which a relation map is drawn and the analysis is started. You can set any element as the context.</p><p>To set an element as the context of the relation map</p><hr /><ul><li>Drag the selected element from the Model Browser to the relation map.</li><li>In the Criteria area, click the <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="21"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_context_element_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_MD buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button next to the Context box and then select the element in the <strong>Select Context Element</strong><span> </span>dialog.<span> </span></li><li><p>If the <strong>Make Element as Context on Selection</strong> option is turned on, click any node to make it the new context of the structure. To turn this option on, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the toolbar and select <strong>Make Element as Context on Selection</strong>.</p></li></ul><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993074 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00264: (2026x Refresh1) Changing the label display mode

- page_id: `303993074`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993074/2026x+Refresh1+Changing+the+label+display+mode
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:22:46.155+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling structure with Blocks > (2026x Refresh1) Connecting Blocks in SysML Internal Block Diagram > (2026x Refresh1) Implied Connectors
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The label of implied relation provides useful information about actual ports and Connectors. You can define how much of the information should be shown in the label by changing the **Label Display Mode**in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Symbol Properties dialog' space='']**Symbol Properties** dialog.The procedure below describes how to change the label display mode and explains the meaning each of display mode.

To change the label display mode

1. Right-click the implied relation.
2. Select **Symbol Properties**.
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Symbol Properties dialog' space='']**Symbol Properties** dialog, find the **Label Display Mode** property and select one of the following value:
  - 
    - **Do Not Display** - to hide the label.
    - **Compact**(default) - to show a short summary in the label of how many actual connectors are underneath.
    - **Detailed** - to show all names or types of ports on both actual connector ends.
4. Click **OK**.

##### Compact Label Display Mode

It allows you to show a short summary in the label of how many actual connectors are underneath. It also displays names of the first level ports at both connector ends, if actual connectors exist only between two ports. Otherwise, the names will not be shown. The figure below demonstrates the situation, when all labels of implied relations provide a different information despite the same **Compact** display mode is selected for all of them.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Three labels of the implied relation with Compact display mode which provides different information.

1. The label of the implied relation between Part Property typed by *TV* Block and diagram frame indicates that only 1 actual connector exists between part and diagram frame because the number is not shown in brackets. The *power in* and socket 2 names in the label shows the names of the first level ports. 
2. The label of the implied relation between Part Properties typed by *TV* and *TV Tuner* Blocks indicates that 18 actual connectors exist between those parts. The *hdmi*name in the label shows the names of the first level ports. 
3. The label of the implied relation between Part Property typed by *TV Tuner* Block and diagram frame indicates that 9 actual connectors exist between part and diagram frame. The names of ports are not displayed in the label because actual connectors (which are underneath of implied relation) have different first level ports at both ends.

##### Detailed Label Display Mode

It allows you to show all names or types of ports on both actual connector ends. If actual Connectors are between two ports, the label is the same as**Compact** mode.The figure below demonstrates the situation, when all labels of implied relations provide a different information despite the same **Detailed** display mode is selected for all of them.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Three labels of the implied relation with Detailed display mode which provides different information.

In the figure above, the labels numbered 1. and 2. in the illustration, provide the same information as **Compact** display mode because actual connectors exist only between two ports. The label between the *TV Tuner* Block and diagram frame (3.) shows that 8 connectors exist between*internet* and *LAN socket* ports and 1 connector between *power in* and *socket 2* ports. It is useful to set the**Detailed**mode when you want to see all first level port names in the implied relation label (see figure below).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The implied relation with the Detailed label display mode.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SysML Internal Block Diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Connecting parts through interface' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Hiding direction arrow on port shape' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Displaying parts and ports in SysML v1' space='']

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The label of implied relation provides useful information about actual ports and Connectors. You can define how much of the information should be shown in the label by changing <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">the <strong>Label Display Mode </strong>in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</span><span class="confluence-link"> </span>The procedure below describes how to change the label display mode and explains the meaning each of display mode.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To change the label display mode</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="140d6462-a2a2-495a-b5c2-696dfd96e519"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Right-click the implied relation.</span></li><li data-uuid="650e6d7e-307d-4e51-b48e-26661c12f79d"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select <strong>Symbol Properties</strong>.<br /></span></li><li data-uuid="a92a275a-1779-4653-933f-2926b47f637e"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>, f</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">ind the <strong>Label Display Mode</strong> property and select one of the following value:</span></p><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Do Not Display</strong> - to hide the label.</span></p></li><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Compact </strong>(default) - to show a short summary in the label of how many actual connectors are underneath.<br /></span></p></li><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Detailed</strong> - to show all names or types of ports on both actual connector ends.</span></p></li></ul></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="26827236-af20-4d46-b521-862bebf9979b"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click <strong>OK</strong>.</span></li></ol><h3><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Compact Label Display Mode</span></h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">It allows you to show a short summary in the label of how many actual connectors are underneath. It also displays names of the first level ports at both connector ends, if actual connectors exist only between two ports. Otherwise, the names will not be shown.</span> The figure below demonstrates the situation, when all labels of implied relations provide a different information despite the same <strong>Compact</strong> display mode is selected for all of them.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="compact_label_display_mode.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Changing the label display mode" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Three labels of the implied relation with Compact display mode which provides different information.</h6><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p>1. The label of the implied relation between Part Property typed by <em>TV</em> Block and diagram frame indicates that only 1 actual connector exists between part and diagram frame because the number is not shown in brackets. The <em>power in</em> and socket 2 names in the label shows the names of the first level ports.<br />2. The label of the implied relation between Part Properties typed by <em>TV</em> and <em>TV Tuner</em> Blocks indicates that 18 actual connectors exist between those parts. The <em>hdmi </em>name in the label shows the names of the first level ports.<br />3. The label of the implied relation between Part Property typed by <em>TV Tuner</em> Block and diagram frame indicates that 9 actual connectors exist between part and diagram frame. The names of ports are not displayed in the label because actual connectors (which are underneath of implied relation) have different first level ports at both ends.</p><h3><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Detailed Label Display Mode </span></h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">It allows you to show all names or types of ports on both actual connector ends. If actual Connectors are between two ports, the label is the same as<strong> Compact</strong> mode.<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> The figure below demonstrates the situation, when all labels of implied relations provide a different information despite the same <strong>Detailed</strong> display mode is selected for all of them.</span><br /></span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="detailed_label_display_mode.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Changing the label display mode" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Three labels of the implied relation with Detailed display mode which provides different information.</h6><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the figure above, the labels numbered 1. and 2. in the illustration, provide the same information as <strong>Compact</strong> display mode because actual connectors exist only between two ports. The label between the <em>TV Tuner</em> Block and diagram frame (3.) shows that 8 connectors exist between<em> internet</em> and <em>LAN socket</em> ports and 1 connector between <em>power in</em> and <em>socket 2</em> ports. It is useful to set the<strong> Detailed </strong>mode when you want to see all first level port names in the implied relation label (see figure below).<br /></span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Detailed label mode.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Changing the label display mode" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The implied relation with the Detailed label display mode.</h6></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995352 space=MT version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 00265: (2026x Refresh1) Changing the tooltips style

- page_id: `303995352`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995352/2026x+Refresh1+Changing+the+tooltips+style
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:28.292+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can specify the tooltips display mode of the symbols on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Understanding the user interface' space=''] by selecting to hide it, to show only the element name, or its documentation text. The element tooltip in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Model Browser' space=''] displays only documentation text, if it is specified for an element. Otherwise, the tooltip is not shown in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Model Browser' space=''].

#### INFO: Writing documentation for an element

Writing documentation for an element

You can write a documentation for an element by using the:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Documentation tab' space=''] .
- Element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification window' space=''] > Documentation/Hyperlinks property group.
- Element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification window' space=''] > Documentation property.

To change the tooltips style of the symbol on the diagram pane

1. On the main menu, select Options > Project .
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project options' space='']**Project Options** dialog find the Tooltips Style option.
3. Select one of the following values:
  - Do Not Show (default) - the tooltip is not shown when hovering a mouse on any element.
  - Show Element Name - The tooltip displays the name of the element when hovering a mouse on it.
  - Show Element Documentation - The tooltip displays the documentation text of the element when hovering a mouse on it.
4. Click **OK**. 
The symbol tooltips display mode is changed in the entire project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can specify the tooltips display mode of the symbols on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> by selecting to hide it, to show only the element name, or its documentation text. The element tooltip in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Model Browser" /></ac:link> displays only documentation text, if it is specified for an element. Otherwise, the tooltip is not shown in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Model Browser" /></ac:link>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="71741dd5-c1fa-46c6-9794-ac42b18dacf7"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Writing documentation for an element</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can write a documentation for an element by using the:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Documentation tab" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Element <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Specification window" /></ac:link> &gt; <strong>Documentation/Hyperlinks</strong> property group.</li><li>Element <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Specification window" /></ac:link> &gt; <strong>Documentation</strong> property.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To change the tooltips style of the symbol on the diagram pane</p><hr /><ol><li>On the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Project options" /><ac:link-body><strong>Project Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> find the <strong>Tooltips Style</strong> option.</li><li>Select one of the following values:<br /><ul><li><strong>Do Not Show</strong> (default) -  the tooltip is not shown when hovering a mouse on any element.</li><li><strong>Show Element Name</strong> - The tooltip displays the name of the element when hovering a mouse on it.</li><li><strong>Show Element Documentation </strong>- The tooltip displays the documentation text of the element when hovering a mouse on it.</li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />The symbol tooltips display mode is changed in the entire project.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol>
````

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1348176924

1348176935

1348176934

The Chart tool provides functions to prepare tag values from models in matrix tabular form to be mapped into a variety of chart types and styles. This tool is supported in the DOCX template. Like other Custom tools, the Chart tool (chart.JAR) must be installed in the **extensions** folder of the Report Wizard plugin. For further information about Custom tool and installation, see [Custom tool](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Custom+tool).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Chart tool using tag values from models in matrix tabular form to be mapped into chart types and styles.

To use the Chart tool in the modeling tool

1. Place the JAR file in the extensions folder of the Report Wizard plugin folder.
2. Start the modeling tool.
3. Use the following line of code to import the Chart tool to your report: text

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991272 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00268: (2026x Refresh1) Choreography Activities

- page_id: `303991272`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991272/2026x+Refresh1+Choreography+Activities
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:47:41.193+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN Choreography Diagram
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1486594888

1486594889

1486594889

A Choreography Activity is an abstract element. It represents a point on a choreography flow where an interaction occurs between two or more participants.

There are three types of Choreography activities defined in business process modeling:

The shape of a Choreography Task, SubChoreography, or Call Choreography consists of two or more participant compartments and one name compartment. One of the participants can be selected as an initiating participant. The color of the initiating participant compartment is the same as the color of the name compartment. The other participants compartments are gray.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Compartments on Choreography Activity shape

The looping properties of a Choreography Activity can be specified. They can be repeated sequentially, essentially behaving like a loop. The presence of loop characteristics signifies that the Choreography Activity has looping behavior. There are two types of Looping characteristics defined in BPMN:

**Standard Loop**

A Standard Loop marker displayed on a name compartment of a Choreography Activity shape shows that the looping behavior based on a boolean condition is defined for this Activity. Additional looping characteristics can also be defined: the Activity will loop as long as the boolean condition is true. The condition is evaluated for every loop iteration and can be evaluated at the beginning or end of the iteration. In addition, a numeric cap can be optionally specified, but the number of iterations cannot exceed this cap.

- Choreography Activity with Standard Loop marker [ATTACHMENT filename='choreography_task_with_standard_loop.png']
- SubChoreography with Standard Loop marker [ATTACHMENT filename='choreography_subprocess_with_standard_loop.png']

**Multi-instance Loop**

A Multi-instance Loop marker shows that a desired number of Choreography Activity instances can be created. The instances can be executed either in parallel or sequentially and each will be identified using a different marker.

- Choreography Task with Parallel MultiInstance Loop marker [ATTACHMENT filename='choreography_task_parallel.png']
- SubChoreography with Parallel MultiInstance Loop marker [ATTACHMENT filename='choreography_subprocess_parallel.png']
- Choreography Task with sequential MultiInstance Loop marker [ATTACHMENT filename='choreography_task_parallel_is_sequential.png']
- SubChoreography with sequential MultiInstance Loop marker [ATTACHMENT filename='choreography_subprocess_parallel_is_sequential.png']

Multi-instance Loop and Standard Loop markers can be added to all types of Choreography activities.

There are circumstances when a Choreography Activity references a multi-instance participant (the minimum multiplicity property value for a participant is 2 or greater). A multi-instance participant represents a situation where there is more than one possible related participant involved in a Choreography. If this is the case, a MultiInstance marker will be displayed in the participant compartment of a Choreography Activity shape.

1486594887

**Related diagram**

- BPMN Process Diagram

**Related procedure**

- Using Choreography Activity

It represents a point on a choreography flow where an interaction occurs between two or more participants.</p><p>There are three types of Choreography activities defined in business process modeling:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="b3e4d655-dd0d-49cc-8824-972698b39055" /></p><p>The shape of a Choreography Task, SubChoreography, or Call Choreography consists of two or more participant compartments and one name compartment. One of the participants can be selected as an initiating participant. The color of the initiating participant compartment is the same as the color of the name compartment. The other participants compartments are gray.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="choreography_task.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Choreography Activities" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Compartments on Choreography Activity shape</h6><p>The looping properties of a Choreography Activity can be specified. They can be repeated sequentially, essentially behaving like a loop. The presence of loop characteristics signifies that the Choreography Activity has looping behavior. There are two types of Looping characteristics defined in BPMN:</p><p><strong>Standard Loop</strong></p><p>A Standard Loop marker displayed on a name compartment of a Choreography Activity shape shows that the looping behavior based on a boolean condition is defined for this Activity. Additional looping characteristics can also be defined: the Activity will loop as long as the boolean condition is true. The condition is evaluated for every loop iteration and can be evaluated at the beginning or end of the iteration. In addition, a numeric cap can be optionally specified, but the number of iterations cannot exceed this cap.</p><ul><li>Choreography Activity with Standard Loop marker<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="choreography_task_with_standard_loop.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Choreography Activities" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>SubChoreography with Standard Loop marker<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="choreography_subprocess_with_standard_loop.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Choreography Activities" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><p><strong>Multi-instance Loop</strong></p><p>A Multi-instance Loop marker shows that a desired number of Choreography Activity instances can be created. The instances can be executed either in parallel or sequentially and each will be identified using a different marker.</p><ul><li>Choreography Task with Parallel MultiInstance Loop marker<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="choreography_task_parallel.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Choreography Activities" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>SubChoreography with Parallel MultiInstance Loop marker<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="choreography_subprocess_parallel.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Choreography Activities" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Choreography Task with sequential MultiInstance Loop marker<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="choreography_task_parallel_is_sequential.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Choreography Activities" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>SubChoreography with sequential MultiInstance Loop marker<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="choreography_subprocess_parallel_is_sequential.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Choreography Activities" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="11aafcb5-2c6a-42d7-b66b-404ecfd578f0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Multi-instance Loop and Standard Loop markers can be added to all types of Choreography activities.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>There are circumstances when a Choreography Activity references a multi-instance participant (the minimum multiplicity property value for a participant is 2 or greater). A multi-instance participant represents a situation where there is more than one possible related participant involved in a Choreography. If this is the case, a MultiInstance marker will be displayed in the participant compartment of a Choreography Activity shape.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="23358ac3-1722-4330-8761-d53ed91a9414"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1486594887</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related diagram</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) BPMN+Process+Diagram">BPMN Process Diagram</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedure</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Using+Choreography+Activity">Using Choreography Activity</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

1486626112

1486626112

**Description**

A Choreography Task is an atomic Activity in a choreography process. It represents one or more messages exchanged between two Participants.A Choreography Task can display messages that are defined by the referenced Message Flows. The messages connected to an Initiating Participant compartment are white and those connected to a Non-Initiating Participant compartment are gray.

**Notation**

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Choreography task corresponding to BPMN Collaboration diagram

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993966 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00271: (2026x Refresh1) Class diagram

- page_id: `303993966`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993966/2026x+Refresh1+Class+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:23:45.732+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) UML diagrams
- labels: ['class', 'diagram', 'uml', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

306054507

306054509

306054508

**Overview**

A Class diagram falls under the structural diagramming family. It is a graphic representation of the static structural model. It shows [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MED'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface' space='MED'], along with their internal structure and relationships. The classes represent types of objects that are handled in a system. The instances of those types (objects) are instantiated only on the runtime and are represented by an object and the interaction diagrams. A Class diagram does not show temporal information, it describes only the classification.

Classes can be related to each other in a number of ways: associated (connected to each other), dependent (one class depends/uses another class), specialized (one class is a subtype of another class), or packaged (grouped together as a unit – package). A Class diagram does not express anything specific about the relationships of a given object, but it does abstractly describe the potential relationships of one object with other objects.

A system typically has a number of Class diagrams – not all the classes are inserted into a single Class diagram. A class can have multiple levels of meaning and participate in several Class diagrams. A Class diagram is the logical map of an existing or future source code.

**Purpose**

A class diagram describes the types of objects (e.g., people, materials, information, and behaviors) in the system and various kinds of static relationships that exist among them. Class diagrams also show the attributes and operations of a class and the constraints that apply to the way objects are connected.

**Usage**

A class diagram can be used to:

- illustrate both simple and complex models for information systems
- understand the general overview of the schematics of an application
- visually express the specific needs of a system
- highlight the specific code that needs to be programmed and implemented to the structure
- provide an implementation-independent description of types used in a system
- share relevant information with stakeholders
- detect errors early in the lifecycle.

**Summary:**

Class diagrams are valuable because they:

- define the essential resources of the system
- define relationships between resources
- generate code
- model code (reverse engineering)
- provide a focus for all the other diagrams.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of a Class diagram

306054506

It shows <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Class" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[classes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[interfaces]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, along with their internal structure and relationships. The classes represent types of objects that are handled in a system. The instances of those types (objects) are instantiated only on the runtime and are represented by an object and the interaction diagrams. A Class diagram does not show temporal information, it describes only the classification. </p><p>Classes can be related to each other in a number of ways: associated (connected to each other), dependent (one class depends/uses another class), specialized (one class is a subtype of another class), or packaged (grouped together as a unit – package). A Class diagram does not express anything specific about the relationships of a given object, but it does abstractly describe the potential relationships of one object with other objects.</p><p>A system typically has a number of Class diagrams – not all the classes are inserted into a single Class diagram. A class can have multiple levels of meaning and participate in several Class diagrams. A Class diagram is the logical map of an existing or future source code.</p><p><strong>Purpose</strong></p><p>A class diagram describes the types of objects (e.g., people, materials, information, and behaviors) in the system and various kinds of static relationships that exist among them. Class diagrams also show the attributes and operations of a class and the constraints that apply to the way objects are connected. </p><p><strong>Usage</strong></p><p>A class diagram can be used to:</p><ul><li>illustrate both simple and complex models for information systems </li><li>understand the general overview of the schematics of an application</li><li>visually express the specific needs of a system</li><li>highlight the specific code that needs to be programmed and implemented to the structure</li><li>provide an implementation-independent description of types used in a system</li><li>share relevant information with stakeholders </li><li>detect errors early in the lifecycle. </li></ul><p><strong>Summary:</strong></p><p>Class diagrams are valuable because they:</p><ul><li>define the essential resources of the system</li><li>define relationships between resources </li><li>generate code</li><li>model code (reverse engineering)</li><li>provide a focus for all the other diagrams. </li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="class_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Class diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of a Class diagram</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bdbc5c26-9baa-4aab-8ebe-f1760f1a8623"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">306054506</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating diagrams" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Class" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997888 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00272: (2026x Refresh1) Class Diagram Wizard

- page_id: `303997888`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997888/2026x+Refresh1+Class+Diagram+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:28:41.531+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Model Visualizer
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

561698190

561698194

561698193

This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions only.

The **Class Diagram Wizard** helps you create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Class diagram' space=''] when all classes and their relationships are already created and specified. You can select which classes, packages, and relationships will be included in a new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Class diagram' space=''], as well as the details of the class representation to be configured (attributes, operations, accessibility). The **Class Diagram Wizard** then guides you through several steps and collects information along the way. It will automatically create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Class diagram' space=''] with your selected elements, freeing you from creating the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Class diagram' space=''] manually.

To create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Class diagram' space=''] using the **Class Diagram Wizard**

1. Open the Class Diagram Wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the Diagrams menu, select Diagram Wizards > Class Diagram Wizard .
  - From the Analyze menu, select Model Visualizer . In the open Model Visualizer dialog, select to open the Class Diagram Wizard and click Start.
2. Follow the steps as directed by the wizard.

561698189

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Class diagram' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997599 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00273: (2026x Refresh1) Clearing values from rows

- page_id: `303997599`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997599/2026x+Refresh1+Clearing+values+from+rows
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:28:29.194+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Tables > (2026x Refresh1) Basic tasks in tables > (2026x Refresh1) Working with rows
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To clear values from an entire row

1. Right-click the number cell to select the entire row.
2. From the shortcut menu, click Clear . Alternatively, you can press Delete on your keyboard after selecting the entire row.

#### WARNING: Data types

Data types

Only String, Boolean, Number, or Enumeration data types can be cleared from a cell.

To clear a value from a cell

1. Right-click the specific cell of the row.
2. From the shortcut menu, click Clear . Alternatively, you can press Delete on your keyboard after selecting the cell.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target">To clear values from an entire row</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f5205aa9-b573-4983-984b-8ec9e03d6c2c">Right-click the number cell to select the entire row. </ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>From the shortcut menu, click <strong>Clear</strong>.<br />Alternatively, you can press<strong> Delete</strong> on your keyboard after selecting the entire row. </li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="63035fe7-faf5-41da-b952-aaf332340a76"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Data types</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Only String, Boolean, Number, or Enumeration data types can be cleared from a cell.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p>To clear a value from a cell</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the specific cell of the row.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, click<strong> Clear</strong>. <br />Alternatively, you can press <strong>Delete</strong> on your keyboard after selecting the cell. </li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995232 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00274: (2026x Refresh1) Cloning selected element

- page_id: `303995232`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995232/2026x+Refresh1+Cloning+selected+element
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:23.871+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements > (2026x Refresh1) Selecting elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

402269317

402269319

402269318

In the element selection dialog, you can clone an existing element. It means you can create a new element on the basis of the selected element having the same data as the selected element.

To clone a selected element

1. In the Tree tab, click the Creation Mode button.
2. In the model tree, select the element which you want to clone and do one of the following:
  - Click the Clone button.
  - From the selected element's shortcut menu, select Clone .
3. The Specification window of a new element opens with all properties derived from the cloned element. By default, the number is added to the element name.
4. Change or define desired properties and click Close when you are done. The new element is included to the model tree.

402269316

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Selecting elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements' space='']
