<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998817 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00295: (2026x Refresh1) Complete diagrams

- page_id: `303998817`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998817/2026x+Refresh1+Complete+diagrams
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:30:09.697+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagramming
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Updating multiple paths

31649184

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Updating multiple paths

31649186

31649182

**On this page**

4

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Updating multiple paths

31649185

##### Marking diagrams as complete

When you finish working on a diagram, you expect that the diagram layout will not change. However, if the content of your model is changed, the diagram layout changes as well; shapes are automatically resized to fit new content and labels are automatically displayed on paths. This can cause symbols to overlap and paths to be left hanging in a diagram, as illustrated below. So you must go back to the finished diagram and fix its layout.To save time, you can mark diagrams as complete andprevent their layout from being automatically updated.

700500

To mark a diagram as complete

- Right-click the diagram on the diagram pane or in the model browser and from the menu select Complete .

When a diagram is marked as complete, the following happens:

- The «complete» stereotype is applied to a diagram. This way you can easily indicate complete diagrams in the model browser without opening them.
- Auto-resizing of shapes and auto-displaying of labels is suspended. This means that the diagram layout is not automatically changed when the model content changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The figure displays an example of a complete diagram.

#### NOTE: Alternative

Alternative

You can suspend auto-resizing of shapes and auto-displaying of labels in a diagram without marking it as complete in one of the following ways:

- To suspend auto-resizing of shapes and auto-displaying of labels for individual symbols, o pen the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Symbol Properties dialog' space='']**Symbol Properties** dialog of a symbol or a group of symbols, and set the Suspend Auto-Resizing of Shapes and/or Suspend Auto-Displaying of Labels properties to true .
- To suspend auto-resizing of shapes and auto-displaying of labels for all the diagram symbols, o pen the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying diagram style properties' space='']**Diagram Properties** dialog of a diagram, and set the Suspend Auto-Resizing of Shapes and/or Suspend Auto-Displaying of Labels properties to true.

##### Updating complete diagram layout

When a diagram is marked as complete, the symbols with suspended content related to model changes are highlighted in blue (see the figure above). You can update these symbols manually, as described below.

To update shapes

1. In a complete diagram, select the highlighted shape you want to update.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Drag one of the selection handles on the corner of the shape to resize it. The suspended content is dynamically displayed after resizing the shape.
  - Click the smart manipulator on the bottom of the shape, as shown below. [ATTACHMENT filename='updating_shape.png']
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='solver.png'] on the shape toolbar and select Resize to Fit Content .

#### TIP: Updating multiple shapes

Updating multiple shapes

To update multiple shapes in a complete diagram, select the highlighted shapes you want to update and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the diagram toolbar.

To update paths

1. In a complete diagram, select the highlighted path you want to update.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Click the smart manipulator on the path, as shown below. [ATTACHMENT filename='updating_path.png']
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='solver.png'] on the path toolbar and select Show Suspended Labels .

#### TIP: Updating multiple paths

Updating multiple paths

To update multiple paths in a complete diagram, select the highlighted paths you want to update and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the diagram toolbar.

##### Marking diagrams as incomplete

If you need to resume normal work on a diagram, mark it as incomplete, as described below.

To mark a diagram as incomplete

1. Right-click a diagram on the diagram pane or in the model browser and select the Complete option to clear its check box.
2. When the question dialog is displayed, do one of the following:
  - Select **Yes**, to continue suspending auto-resizing of shapes and/or auto-displaying of labels.
  - Select No , to resume auto-resizing of shapes and/or auto-displaying of labels.

If you select**No**in step 2, all diagram symbols are automatically resized to fit suspended content, andyou can continue working on the diagram as usual. If you select **Yes**, auto-resizing of shapes and auto-displaying of labels in the diagram are still suspended, even though the diagram is not complete.

##### Customizing complete diagram behavior

By default, auto-resizing of shapes and auto-displaying of labels in a complete diagram are suspended. Also, the symbols with suspended content due to model changes are highlighted in blue. You can change this default behavior in the **Project Options** dialog.

To customize complete diagram behavior

1. In the main menu of your modeling tool, go to Options > Project .
2. On the left side of the Project Options dialog, expand the General node and select Diagrams .
3. On the right side of the dialog, specify the desired options in the Complete Diagrams group:
  - Suspend Auto-Resizing of Shapes - set to true to keep the original size of shapes while editing the model.
  - Suspend Auto-Displaying of Labels - set to true if you do not want to automatically show labels in a diagram while editing the model.
  - Highlight Suspended Symbols - set to true to highlight symbols that have suspended content related to model changes.

When you specify project options listed in step 3, they are applied to all diagrams that are marked as complete after specification.

##### **Check complete diagrams**

To check complete diagrams

- In the main menu of your modeling tool, select Analyze > Check Complete Diagrams .

The **Check Complete Diagrams**action checks for complete diagrams without completely visible content by running the following three validation rules:

| Validation rule | Error message | Severity | Profile |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Suspended Autodisplaying | The symbol content is not completely visible. Auto-displaying of labels is suspended. | warning | UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile |
| Suspended Autoresizing | The symbol content is not completely visible. Auto-resizing of shapes is suspended. | warning | UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile |
| Suspended Autochanges | The diagram contains symbols whose content is not completely visible. | warning | UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fe6478ea-38ed-4100-a010-476f2f82af3c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">31649184</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="65c27722-ae16-4182-ad68-70e6a9d58995"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">31649186</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f60a082b-0878-442a-988c-aacb5c806494"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">31649182</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1a3d3a0d-87b9-4be2-a1e6-fff6474fc74c"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e7bde8e3-a27c-4d12-bd7b-ce56f19ad8ad"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">31649185</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3>Marking diagrams as complete</h3><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="834fe518-37ef-4695-8dc1-9d724225be7d">When you finish working on a diagram, you expect that the diagram layout <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8699c10a-64e7-443e-97d2-bf23bbe489f4">will not change</ac:inline-comment-marker>. However, if the content of your model is changed, the diagram layout changes as well; <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">shapes are automatically resized to fit new content and labels are automatically displayed on paths. This can cause symbols to overlap and paths to be left hanging in a diagram, as illustrated below. So you must go back to the finished diagram and fix its layout. </span>To save time, you can mark diagrams as complete and </ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d5ee668f-8ddb-4894-b55e-7caa5b86134e"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="834fe518-37ef-4695-8dc1-9d724225be7d">prevent their layout from being automatically updated.</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="171704bf-9248-4ad2-8488-15ce80247af2"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">700</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lPj4knb0JfY&amp;feature=youtu.be" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">500</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);"><br /></span></p><p>To mark a diagram as complete</p><hr /><ul><li>Right-click the diagram on the diagram pane or in the model browser and from the menu select <strong>Complete</strong>.</li></ul><p><br /><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d31772ee-38ea-4cc3-b158-7937f5d04955">When a diagram is marked as complete</ac:inline-comment-marker>, the following happens:</p><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="4dee7dec-5fe0-4f29-b7ed-1446985121db">The «complete» stereotype is applied to a diagram. This way you can easily indicate complete diagrams in the model browser without opening them.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="4dee7dec-5fe0-4f29-b7ed-1446985121db">Auto-resizing of shapes and auto-displaying of labels is suspended. This means that the diagram layout is not automatically changed when the model content changes.<br /><br /></ac:inline-comment-marker></p></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="complete_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Complete diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The figure displays an example of a complete diagram.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="40bbb078-4d3c-4f42-af4a-076adb667cad"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Alternative</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can suspend auto-resizing of shapes and auto-displaying of labels in a diagram without marking it as complete in one of the following ways:</p><ul><li>To suspend <span>auto-resizing of shapes and auto-displaying of labels for individual symbols, o</span>pen the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> of a symbol or a group of symbols, and set the <strong>Suspend Auto-Resizing of Shapes</strong> and/or <strong>Suspend Auto-Displaying of Labels</strong> properties to <em>true</em>.</li><li>To suspend <span>auto-resizing of shapes and auto-displaying of labels for all the diagram symbols, o</span>pen the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Specifying diagram style properties" /><ac:link-body><strong>Diagram Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> of a diagram, and set the <strong>Suspend Auto-Resizing of Shapes</strong><span> and/or </span><strong>Suspend Auto-Displaying of Labels</strong><span> properties to </span><em>true.</em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><br />Updating complete diagram layout</h3><p>When a diagram is marked as complete, the symbols with suspended content related to model changes are highlighted in blue (see the figure above). You can update these symbols manually, as described below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="86efe6dd-ebdb-47b2-917c-0eac9ad4bc1b">update</ac:inline-comment-marker> shapes</p><hr /><ol><li>In a complete diagram, select the highlighted shape you want to update.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Drag one of the selection handles on the corner of the shape to resize it. <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="31395402-df94-4a3c-bdce-e2cbbbf321e9">The suspended content is <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8c9350f9-dd0d-4d84-a1fc-cfc36665fee5">dynamically</ac:inline-comment-marker> displayed after resizing the shape.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>Click the smart manipulator on the bottom of the shape, as shown below.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="updating_shape.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Complete diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="solver.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Complete diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the shape toolbar and select <strong>Resize to Fit Content</strong>.</li></ul></li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5a6d417b-8437-4169-904e-12c917ebf25a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Updating multiple shapes</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d9afad5e-1a0c-4f99-814d-0c31b200c81d">update multiple shapes</ac:inline-comment-marker> in a complete diagram, select the highlighted shapes you want to update and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resize_to_fit_content.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Complete diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the diagram toolbar.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To update paths</p><hr /><ol><li>In a complete diagram, select the highlighted path you want to update.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Do one of the following:</span><br /><ul><li>Click the smart manipulator on the path, as shown below.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="updating_path.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Complete diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="solver.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Complete diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the path toolbar and select <strong>Show Suspended Labels</strong>.</li></ul></li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ebb5b2fd-3e1d-43b1-9dea-cd4b4e4b35b2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Updating multiple paths</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>To update multiple paths in a complete diagram, select the highlighted paths you want to update and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_suspended_labels.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Complete diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the diagram toolbar.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><br />Marking diagrams as incomplete</h3><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e19ed52e-ea35-4234-8846-966198a91fa1"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d2f2d779-d6ba-46a3-ae59-1c0f2ca3b3f7">If</ac:inline-comment-marker> you need to resume normal work on a diagram, mark it as incomplete, as described below.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="27f48bad-1eea-4d44-96a8-a8312f1f29a3">To mark a diagram as incomplete</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><hr /><ol><li><span>Right-click a diagram on the diagram pane or in the model browser and select the </span><strong>Complete</strong><span> option to clear its check box.</span></li><li><span>When the question dialog is displayed, do one of the following:</span><ul><li><span>Select <strong>Yes</strong>, to continue suspending auto-resizing of shapes and/or auto-displaying of labels.</span></li><li>Select <strong>No</strong>, to resume auto-resizing of shapes and/or auto-displaying of labels.</li></ul></li></ol><p><br /><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="de16dfcb-8c59-4e2a-8e1c-eb4f72b31c85">If you select </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="de16dfcb-8c59-4e2a-8e1c-eb4f72b31c85">No</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="de16dfcb-8c59-4e2a-8e1c-eb4f72b31c85"> in step 2, <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">all diagram symbols are automatically resized to fit suspended content, and </span>you can continue working on the diagram as usual.</ac:inline-comment-marker> If you select <strong>Yes</strong>, auto-resizing of shapes and auto-displaying of labels in the diagram are still suspended, even though the diagram is not complete.</p><h3><br />Customizing complete diagram behavior</h3><p>By default, auto-resizing of shapes and auto-displaying of labels in a complete diagram are suspended. Also, the symbols with suspended content due to model changes are highlighted in blue. You can change this default behavior in the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog.</p><p><br /></p><p>To customize complete diagram behavior</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu of your modeling tool, go to <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li>On the left side of the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, expand the <strong>General</strong> node and select <strong>Diagrams</strong>.</li><li>On the right side of the dialog, specify the desired options in the <strong>Complete Diagrams</strong> group:<ul><li><strong>Suspend Auto-Resizing of Shapes</strong> - set to <em>true</em> to keep the original size of shapes while editing the model.</li><li><strong>Suspend Auto-Displaying of Labels</strong> - set to <em>true</em> if you do not want to automatically show labels in a diagram while editing the model.</li><li><strong>Highlight Suspended Symbols</strong> - set to <em>true</em> to highlight symbols that have suspended content related to model changes.</li></ul></li></ol><p><br /><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="900c833b-d566-4ac3-9fa8-00af21ec26d9">When you specify project options listed in step 3, they are applied to all diagrams that are marked as complete after specification.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><h3><strong>Check complete diagrams</strong></h3><p>To check complete diagrams</p><hr /><ul><li>In the main menu of your modeling tool, select <strong>Analyze</strong> &gt; <strong>Check Complete Diagrams</strong>.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="900c833b-d566-4ac3-9fa8-00af21ec26d9">The <strong>Check Complete Diagrams </strong>action checks for complete diagrams without completely visible content by running the following three validation rules:</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 78.296%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 20.2714%;" /><col style="width: 30.6063%;" /><col style="width: 9.77742%;" /><col style="width: 24.2493%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Validation rule</th><th colspan="1">Error message</th><th colspan="1">Severity</th><th>Profile</th></tr><tr><td>Suspended Autodisplaying</td><td colspan="1">The symbol content is not completely visible. Auto-displaying of labels is suspended.</td><td colspan="1">warning</td><td>UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile</td></tr><tr><td>Suspended Autoresizing</td><td colspan="1">The symbol content is not completely visible. Auto-resizing of shapes is suspended.</td><td colspan="1">warning</td><td>UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile</td></tr><tr><td>Suspended Autochanges</td><td colspan="1">The diagram contains symbols whose content is not completely visible.</td><td colspan="1">warning</td><td>UML Standard Profile::Validation Profile</td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

## PAGE 00296: (2026x Refresh1) Complex Gateway

- page_id: `303991896`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991896/2026x+Refresh1+Complex+Gateway
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:47:31.470+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN Process Diagram > (2026x Refresh1) Gateways
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1485849877

1485849879

1485849879

**Description**

A Complex Gateway can be used to model complex synchronization behavior. An Activation Condition is the Complex Gateway’s property, which is used to describe precise behavior.

**Notation**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Example**

The activation condition specifies that tokens on three out of five incoming sequence flows are needed to activate the gateway. Which token the Gateway will produce is determined by the conditions on the outgoing sequence flow as in the split behavior of an Inclusive Gateway

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Complex Gateway

1485849876

**Related elements**

- Gateways
- Sequence Flow

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram
- BPMN Choreography Diagram

**Related procedure**

- Creating and Using a Sequence Flow

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999448 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00297: (2026x Refresh1) ComplexType

- page_id: `303999448`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999448/2026x+Refresh1+ComplexType
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:30:57.856+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Data-related modeling > (2026x Refresh1) XML schemas > (2026x Refresh1) XML schema mapping to UML elements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Complex type maps to UML Class with the stereotype XSDcomplexType.

- abstract - to UML Class abstract value(true | false).
- annotation - to UML Class documentation.
- attribute – to inner UML Class Attribute or UML Association End.
- attributeGroup – to UML AssociationEnd or UML Attribute with type XSDattributeGroup.
- name – to UML Class name.

This class also can have the stereotypes XSDsimpleContent, XSDcomplexContent, XSDall, XSDchoice, and XSDsequence.

No stereotype – the same as “XSDsequence”.

A generalization between a complex type and another type has the stereotype XSDrestriction or XSDextension. We assume the stereotype XSDextension if the generalizations do not have stereotypes.

Some complex mapping:

- complexType with simpleContent – to UML Class. This class must be derived from another class and must have stereotype XSDsimpleContent.
- complexType with complexContent – to UML Class. This class must be derived from another class and must have stereotype XSDcomplexContent.

#### PANEL: complexType with group, all, choice or sequence – to UML class with appropriate stereotype

complexType with group, all, choice or sequence – to UML class with appropriate stereotype

```text
<complexType
```

When you choose the <simpleContent> alternative, the following elements are relevant, and the remaining property mappings are as below. Note that you must either choose <restriction> or <extension> as the content of <simpleContent>.

When the <complexContent> alternative is chosen, the following elements are relevant (as are the<attributeGroup> and <anyAttribute> elements, not repeated here), and the additional property mappings appear below. Note that you must choose either <restriction> or <extension> as the content of <complexContent>, but their content models differ in this case from the case above when they occur as children of<simpleContent>.

The property mappings below are also used in the case where the third alternative (neither <simpleContent> nor <complexContent>) is chosen. This case is understood as shorthand for complex content restricting the **ur type definition**. The details of the mappings should be modified as necessary.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Complex type maps to UML Class with the stereotype XSDcomplexType.</p><ul><li>abstract -  to UML Class abstract value(true | false).</li><li>annotation - to UML Class documentation.</li><li>attribute – to inner UML Class Attribute or UML Association End.</li><li>attributeGroup – to UML AssociationEnd or UML Attribute with type XSDattributeGroup.</li><li>name – to UML Class name.</li></ul><p>This class also can have the stereotypes XSDsimpleContent, XSDcomplexContent, XSDall, XSDchoice, and XSDsequence.</p><p>No stereotype – the same as “XSDsequence”.</p><p>A generalization between a complex type and another type has the stereotype XSDrestriction or XSDextension. We assume the stereotype XSDextension if the generalizations do not have stereotypes.</p><p>Some complex mapping:</p><ul><li>complexType with simpleContent – to UML Class. This class must be derived from another class and must have stereotype XSDsimpleContent.</li><li>complexType with complexContent – to UML Class. This class must be derived from another class and must have stereotype XSDcomplexContent.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f92f6153-0e31-448a-bb79-2e4348d3dd17"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">complexType with group, all, choice or sequence – to UML class with appropriate stereotype</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><pre>&lt;complexType</pre><pre>	abstract = boolean : false</pre><pre>	block = (#all | List of (extension | restriction))</pre><pre>	final = (#all | List of (extension | restriction)) <br />	id = ID</pre><pre>	mixed = boolean : false</pre><pre>	name = NCName</pre><pre>	{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}&gt;</pre><pre>	Content: (annotation?,(simpleContent | complexContent | ((group | al <br />((attribute | attributeGroup)*, anyAttribute?))))</pre><pre>&lt;/complexType&gt;</pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>When you choose the &lt;simpleContent&gt; alternative, the following elements are relevant, and the remaining property mappings are as below. Note that you must either choose &lt;restriction&gt; or &lt;extension&gt; as the content of &lt;simpleContent&gt;.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="009944e0-e6b7-4f4f-bccc-2516e12bba3c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">&lt;restriction&gt; chosen as the content of &lt;simpleContent&gt;</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><pre>&lt;simpleContent</pre><pre>	id = ID</pre><pre>	{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}&gt; Content: (annotation?, (restriction | extension))<br />	&lt;/simpleContent&gt;</pre><pre>&lt;restriction</pre><pre>	base = OName</pre><pre>	id = ID</pre><pre>	{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}&gt;</pre><pre>	Content: (annotation?, (simpleType?, (minExclusive | minInclusive  | maxExclusive |</pre><pre>maxlnclusive | totalDigits | fractionDigits | length | minLength | maxLength</pre><pre>whitespace | pattern) *)?, ((attribute | attributeGroup)*, anyAttribute?))</pre><pre>&lt;/restriction&gt;<br />&lt;extension</pre><pre>	base = OName</pre><pre>	id = ID</pre><pre>	{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}&gt;</pre><pre>	Content: (annotation?, ((attribute | attributeGroup)*, anyAttribute?))</pre><pre>&lt;/extension&gt;</pre><pre>&lt;attributeGroup</pre><pre>	id = ID</pre><pre>	ref = OName</pre><pre>	{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}&gt;</pre><pre>	Content: (annotation?)</pre><pre>&lt;/attributeGroup&gt;</pre><pre>&lt;anyAttribute</pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>When the <u>&lt;complexContent&gt;</u> alternative is chosen, the following elements are relevant (as are the<u> &lt;attributeGroup&gt;</u> and <u>&lt;anyAttribute&gt;</u> elements, not repeated here), and the additional property mappings appear below. Note that you must choose either <u>&lt;restriction&gt;</u> or <u>&lt;extension&gt;</u> as the content of <u>&lt;complexContent&gt;,</u> but their content models differ in this case from the case above when they occur as children of<u> &lt;simpleContent&gt;.</u></p><p>The property mappings below are also used in the case where the third alternative (neither <u>&lt;simpleContent&gt;</u> nor <u>&lt;complexContent&gt;)</u> is chosen. This case is understood as shorthand for complex content restricting the <strong>ur type definition</strong>. The details of the mappings should be modified as necessary.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ef0542e8-9728-4810-a738-c090574a1549"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Sample of either &lt;restriction&gt; or &lt;extension&gt; must be chosen as the content of &lt;complexContent&gt;</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><pre>&lt;complexContent</pre><pre>	id = ID</pre><pre>	mixed = boolean</pre><pre>	{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}&gt;<br />	Content: (annotation?, (restriction | extension))</pre><pre>&lt;/complexContent&gt;</pre><pre>&lt;restriction</pre><pre>	base = OName</pre><pre>	id = ID</pre><pre>	{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}&gt;</pre><pre>	Content: (annotation?, (group | all | choice | sequence)?, ((attribute | attributeGroup)*, anyAttribute?))</pre><pre>&lt;/restriction&gt;</pre><pre>&lt;extension</pre><pre>	base = OName</pre><pre>	id = ID</pre><pre>	{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}&gt;</pre><pre>	Content: (annotation?, ((group | all | choice | sequence)?, ((attribute | attributeGroup)*, anyAttribute?)))</pre><pre>&lt;/extension&gt;</pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 58 complexType UML Model Example.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) ComplexType" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>Example of complexType UML model.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1b502a09-1a14-42cb-bc44-e3052fedd33f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example of complexType UML model with associated XML</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><pre>&lt;?xml version=’1.0’ encoding = ‘Cp1252’?›</pre><pre>&lt;xs:schema xmlns:nm = &quot;<a href="http://nomagic.com">http://nomagic.com</a>&quot; xmlns:xs = &quot;<a href="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema">http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema</a>&quot; targetNamespace = &quot;<a href="http://nomagic.com">http://nomagic.com</a>&quot; &gt;</pre><pre>	&lt;xs:complexType name = &quot;my_Type2&quot; block = &quot;extension&quot; final = &quot;extension&quot; mixed = &quot;true&quot; &gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;xs:annotation &gt;</pre><pre>			&lt;xs:documentation &gt;my_type2</pre><pre>Documentation&lt;/xs:documentation&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;/xs:annotation&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;xs:complexContent id = &quot;contentID&quot; mixed = &quot;false&quot; &gt;<br />			&lt;xs:extension base = &quot;nm:my_Type&quot;&gt;</pre><pre>				&lt;xs:attribute name = &quot;surname&quot; type = &quot;xs:string&quot; /&gt;</pre><pre>			&lt;/xs:extension&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;/xs:complexContent&gt; <br />	&lt;/xs:complexType&gt;</pre><pre>	&lt;xs:complexType name = &quot;my_Type3&quot; &gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;xs:complexContent &gt;</pre><pre>			&lt;xs:restriction base = &quot;nm:my_Type&quot;&gt;</pre><pre>				&lt;xs:all &gt;</pre><pre>					&lt;xs:element name = &quot;order&quot; type = &quot;xs:string&quot;/&gt;</pre><pre>					&lt;xs:element name = &quot;order1&quot; type = &quot;xs:string&quot;/&gt;</pre><pre>				&lt;/xs:all&gt; <br />			&lt;/xs:restriction&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;/xs:complexContent&gt;<br />	&lt;/xs:complexType&gt;</pre><pre>	&lt;xs:complexType name = &quot;my_Type4&quot;&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;xs:simpleContent &gt;</pre><pre>			&lt;xs:restriction base = &quot;xs:string&quot; &gt;</pre><pre>				&lt;xs:minLength value = &quot;2&quot; /&gt;</pre><pre>			&lt;/xs:restriction&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;/xs:simpleContent&gt; &lt;/xs:complexType&gt;</pre><pre>	&lt;xs:complexType name = &quot;my_Type5&quot;&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;xs:simpleContent &gt;</pre><pre>			&lt;xs:extension base = &quot;xs:string&quot;&gt;</pre><pre>				&lt;xs:attribute name = &quot;attri&quot; type = &quot;xs:string&quot;/&gt;</pre><pre>			&lt;/xs:extension&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;/xs:simpleContent&gt;<br />	&lt;/xs:complexType&gt;</pre><pre>	&lt;xs:complexType name = &quot;my_Type&quot; abstract = &quot;true&quot; block = &quot;extension&quot; final = &quot;extension&quot; id = &quot;myTypeID&quot; mixed = &quot;true&quot; &gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;xs:annotation &gt;</pre><pre>			&lt;xs:documentation &gt;my_type documentation&lt;/xs:documentation&gt;<br />		&lt;/xs:annotation&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;xs:attribute name = &quot;name&quot; <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992397 space=MT version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 00298: (2026x Refresh1) Component Behavior

- page_id: `303992397`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992397/2026x+Refresh1+Component+Behavior
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:55.592+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Exporting to External Simulation Models > (2026x Refresh1) Sample models > (2026x Refresh1) Creating a simple model
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1553808215

1553808216

1553808216

Now that the variables and parameters are defined, the behavior of each block can be specified. To do so, equations are used to establish the relationships among different properties.

In SysML v1, these equations are represented as constraints within *ConstraintBlocks*. Their parameters correspond to the *PhSVariables* and *PhSConstant*of the blocks (“i”, “v”, “r”) as well as the*PhSVariables*present in the type of the ports (“pv”, “pi”, “nv”, “ni”). Refer to the image below to see how these constraints are represented in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SysML Block Definition Diagram' space=''].

An abstract ConstraintBlock *TwoPinComponentConstraint* is created to gather parameters and equations common to the source and the resistor. These equations state that the voltage of the component is the difference between the voltage in the positive pin and the voltage in the negative pin. The current of the component is the current coming from the positive pin, and the current in the two pins must add up to zero. As for the ground, the constraint states that the voltage in the pin is zero.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Constraint Blocks with specified parameters and equations in BDD.

1553808214

**Related page:**

****

Their parameters correspond to the <em>PhSVariables</em> and <em>PhSConstant  </em>of the blocks (“i”, “v”, “r”) as well as the<em> PhSVariables </em>present in the type of the ports (“pv”, “pi”, “nv”, “ni”). Refer to the image below to see how these constraints are represented in a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SysML Block Definition Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Block Definition Diagram (BDD)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>An abstract ConstraintBlock <em>TwoPinComponentConstraint</em> is created to gather parameters and equations common to the source and the resistor. These equations state that the voltage of the component is the difference between the voltage in the positive pin and the voltage in the negative pin. The current of the component is the current coming from the positive pin, and the current in the two pins must add up to zero. As for the ground, the constraint states that the voltage in the pin is zero.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="constraints.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Component Behavior" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Constraint Blocks with specified parameters and equations in BDD.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="22bcc84a-0ee4-41c2-8c44-097a819f60f0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1553808214</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related page:</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="d092f157-fedc-46f0-ab33-04ec2fc4b642" /><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994057 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00299: (2026x Refresh1) Component diagram

- page_id: `303994057`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994057/2026x+Refresh1+Component+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:23:52.886+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) UML diagrams
- labels: ['implementation', 'implementation-diagram', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

308582438

308582440

308582439

**Overview**

A Component diagram falls under the structural diagramming family.A Component diagram represents pieces of software in the implementation environment in terms of code components.

A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Component' space='MED'] contains information about the logical [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MED'] or Classes that it implements, thus creating a mapping from a logical view to a component view. Dependencies between the Components make it easy to analyze how a change in one component affects the others. The Components may also be shown with any of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface' space='MED'] that they expose. They, as with almost any other model elements, can be grouped into [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space='MED'], much like Classes or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='MED'].

**Purpose**

A Component diagram models the implementation view. It is used in the later phases of the software development when there is a need to divide up Classes among different Components. When working with the CASE facilities, the Components are used for file-class mapping during code generation, reverse engineering, and round-trip engineering operations.****

**Usage**

A Component diagram can be used to:

- analyze system components and how they relate
- analyze system's physical structure
- understand the exact service behavior provided by software.

**Summary**

Component diagrams are valuable because they:

- reveal possible software configuration issues
- provide an accurate picture of existing systems prior to making changes/enhancements
- model the real software in the implementation environment
- help to reveal bottlenecks in an implementation.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of a Component diagram

308582437

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating diagrams' space='']

They, as with almost any other model elements, can be grouped into <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Package" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Packages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, much like Classes or <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Use Case" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Use Cases]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><strong>Purpose</strong></p><p>A Component diagram models the implementation view. It is used in the later phases of the software development when there is a need to divide up Classes among different Components. When working with the CASE facilities, the Components are used for file-class mapping during code generation, reverse engineering, and round-trip engineering operations.<strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Usage</strong></p><p>A Component diagram can be used to:</p><ul><li>analyze system components and how they relate</li><li>analyze system's physical structure</li><li>understand the exact service behavior provided by software. </li></ul><p><strong>Summary</strong></p><p>Component diagrams are valuable because they:</p><ul><li>reveal possible software configuration issues</li><li>provide an accurate picture of existing systems prior to making changes/enhancements</li><li>model the real software in the implementation environment</li><li>help to reveal bottlenecks in an implementation. <br /><br /></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="component_diagram_example.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Component diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of a Component diagram</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="08d36afe-7d48-42c1-b72e-10091cb4c272"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">308582437</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating diagrams" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992400 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00300: (2026x Refresh1) Component variables and parameters

- page_id: `303992400`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992400/2026x+Refresh1+Component+variables+and+parameters
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:55.707+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Exporting to External Simulation Models > (2026x Refresh1) Sample models > (2026x Refresh1) Creating a simple model
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Each of the electrical component has variables corresponding to the domain being modeled. Electrical components have *voltage* and *current*. The voltage corresponds to the difference of potential between the positive and negative pin (across the component), whereas the current corresponds to the amount of charge going through the component.

Two properties *v* and*i*are created in*TwoPinComponent*. During the simulation, the voltage and the current will vary over time. As a result, the properties need to be stereotyped by *PhSVariable*. Their type must be a *ValueType* that has a unit with a symbol. Voltage and Current are already provided by the simulation library, so they can be selected (remove filters if necessary). The resistor has an additional property - the resistance. A property *r* is created in the resistor. Since the resistance will not change during the simulation, the property needs to be stereotyped by *PhSConstant.* It is necessary to create a new*ValueType* called *Resistance*that will serve as a type for this property. It must have a corresponding SysML *Unit* with a symbol set to *Ω*. Additionally, it must be a specialization of SysML*Real*.

The image below shows how [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SysML Block Definition Diagram' space=''] should look like at this point.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The *i* and *v* Value properties typed by *Current* and *Voltage* value types.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Each of the electrical component has variables corresponding to the domain being modeled. Electrical components have <em>voltage</em> and <em>current</em>. The voltage corresponds to the difference of potential between the positive and negative pin (across the component), whereas the current corresponds to the amount of charge going through the component.</p><p>Two properties <em>v</em> and<em> i  </em>are created in<em> TwoPinComponent</em>. During the simulation, the voltage and the current will vary over time. As a result, the properties need to be stereotyped by <em>PhSVariable</em>. Their type must be a <em>ValueType</em> that has a unit with a symbol. Voltage and Current are already provided by the simulation library, so they can be selected (remove filters if necessary). The resistor has an additional property - the resistance. A property <em>r</em> is created in the resistor. Since the resistance will not change during the simulation, the property needs to be stereotyped by <em>PhSConstant.</em> It is necessary to create a new<em> ValueType</em> called <em>Resistance </em>that will serve as a type for this property. It must have a corresponding SysML <em>Unit</em> with a symbol set to <em>Ω</em>. Additionally, it must be a specialization of SysML<em> Real</em>.</p><p>The image below shows how <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SysML Block Definition Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Block Definition Diagram (BDD)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> should look like at this point.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="component_variables_and_parameters.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Component variables and parameters" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(94,108,132);">The <em>i</em> and <em>v</em> Value properties typed by <em>Current</em> and <em>Voltage</em> value types.</span></h6><p />
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992169 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00301: (2026x Refresh1) Composing model

- page_id: `303992169`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992169/2026x+Refresh1+Composing+model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:39.033+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects > (2026x Refresh1) Project partitioning
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can compose your model by using parts of models stored in other projects:

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990044 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00302: (2026x Refresh1) Composite Structure diagram

- page_id: `303990044`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990044/2026x+Refresh1+Composite+Structure+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:04.315+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) UML diagrams
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

308768822

308768824

308768823

**Overview**

A Composite Structure diagram falls under the structural diagramming family.The Composite Structure diagram allows a decomposition and modeling of the internal structure of the classifiers, such as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MED'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Component' space='MED'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Node' space='MED'], and other elements that are able to own other elements.

**Purpose**

A Composite Structure diagram can be used to visually represent the parts of a class, component, or collaboration, including the interaction points (called ports) that are used to access features of the structure. Additionally, Composite Structure diagrams model collaborations that describe behavior, resources used to perform the behavior, and the roles the participating resources take during the behavior.

**Usage**

Composite Structure diagram can be used to:

- analyze an object to see exactly what it is composed of
- detail internal actions of a class, including the relationships of nested classes
- show objects that need to be defined as a composition of other classified objects.

**Summary**

Composite Structure diagrams are valuable because they:

- show complex components design
- show components interfaces separately from their structures
- describe the roles played by elements in the structure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of a Composite Structure diagram

308768820

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating diagrams' space='']

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ab92cd2a-a401-4844-9c5f-0ff217a1862b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">308768822</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="78822c8e-9a3a-404d-ba10-fa0174493722"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">308768824</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e9c673fb-cb43-494f-9b74-9ad733a6128b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">308768823</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Overview</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">A Composite Structure diagram falls under the structural diagramming family. </span>The Composite Structure diagram allows a decomposition and modeling of the internal structure of the classifiers, such as <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Class" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[classes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Component" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[components]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Node" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[nodes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and other elements that are able to own other elements.</p><p><strong>Purpose</strong></p><p>A Composite Structure diagram can be used to visually represent the parts of a class, component, or collaboration, including the interaction points (called ports) that are used to access features of the structure. Additionally, Composite Structure diagrams model collaborations that describe behavior, resources used to perform the behavior, and the roles the participating resources take during the behavior. </p><p><strong>Usage</strong></p><p>Composite Structure diagram can be used to:</p><ul><li>analyze an object to see exactly what it is composed of</li><li>detail internal actions of a class, including the relationships of nested classes</li><li>show objects that need to be defined as a composition of other classified objects.</li></ul><p><strong>Summary</strong></p><p>Composite Structure diagrams are valuable because they:</p><ul><li>show complex components design</li><li>show components interfaces separately from their structures</li><li>describe the roles played by elements in the structure.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="composite_structure_diagram_example.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Composite Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of a Composite Structure diagram</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e03f3fe1-a2a8-47af-9d4e-3024c4e6339a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">308768820</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating diagrams" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="42f4595c-1db3-4b0e-a549-bdd8f01b65b3" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992760 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00303: (2026x Refresh1) Composite Structure Diagram context

- page_id: `303992760`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992760/2026x+Refresh1+Composite+Structure+Diagram+context
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:48:30.050+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) UML diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Composite Structure diagram
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When creating a new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Composite Structure diagram' space=''] for the element that cannot be the context of this diagram (e.g. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space='MED']), the new context element (which is a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MED']) is created automatically.

INLINE

##### Turning off automatic creation of a diagram context element

To turn off automatic creation of a diagram context element

1. On the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Understanding the user interface' space=''] , select Project > Options .
2. In the Project Options dialog, find the Diagram Context option.
3. Select the **Do Not Create** value.
4. Click OK . The automatic creation of a diagram context element is turned off.

This means that when youcreate a newdiagramforan element which cannot be the context of this diagram (e.g. [Package](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Package)),the**Select Diagram Context**dialog will open automatically.It allows you to select an existing element as a context, create a new context element by enabling the**Creation Mode**, or click the**Cancel**button if you do not want to specify the diagram context. The diagram will be created in the Package.

If you want to enable the automatic creation of a diagram context element, select the **Create Automatically** value for the **Diagram Context** option.

##### Changing the diagram context element

After the diagram is created, you can change its context element.

To change the diagram context element

1. Right-click the diagram and select Specification .
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification window' space='']Specification window , find the Context property.
3. Click on its value cell, and select [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button_in_specification_window.png'] .
4. In the **Select Diagram Context** dialog, do one of the following: 
 - Select an existing element as a context in the **Tree** or **List** tab 
 - Create a new context element by enabling the **Creation Mode**>**Create**and select it.
5. Click **OK**. The diagram context element is changed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
