<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992829 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00329: (2026x Refresh1) Controlling dependency creation between used projects

- page_id: `303992829`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992829/2026x+Refresh1+Controlling+dependency+creation+between+used+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:22:24.527+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects > (2026x Refresh1) Project partitioning > (2026x Refresh1) Automated project usages
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In earlier versions of our modeling tool, you could accidentally create unneeded or incorrect (from the user standpoint) dependencies between used projects in the read/write mode. Careless editing often caused cyclic module dependencies or inadvertent expansion of the scope of the other projects when the same used project was used in several other projects.

Automated project usages fix this problem.

Every automatically created usage must be backed by an existing user-defined used project usage. If it is not backed, the automated used project usage must be explicitly confirmed by the user. The usage cannot cause undesired effects in other projects while it is unconfirmed.

Automated used project usage *A**→**B* is called**unconfirmed**when there is no user-defined usage path from A to B (neither direct user-defined usage*A**→**B* nor user-defined usage path of arbitrary length *A**→ ... → X →**B*).

User-defined usages are always confirmed.

In ordinary circumstances, automated usages are not visible to the user. When the user modifies the main project’s model and adds dependencies to used projects or sub-used projects, these usages are proper. They are backed by the fact that the used project or sub-used project is attached to the project in some way; thus, the user-defined usage path refers from the main project to the used project or sub-used project.

However, there are situations when automated used project usages are not backed and thus unconfirmed.

An unconfirmed automated used project usage is considered to be invalid.

This is just one of the project composition errors. Other project composition errors include similarity to recovered element errors, conflicting user-defined usage parameters, and cyclic dependencies.

Unconfirmed automated used project usages are caught by special [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Active Validation Rules for Links' space='MED']. They are displayed in validation results as warnings or errors depending on the project composition. The validation results prompt the user to remedy the situation by either confirming or rejecting the usage.

Unconfirmed usages can occur in several cases. They are described as follows.

#### INFO: Case #1

Case #1

When you edit a model part belonging to a used project with read/write permissions, an additional model-level reference can be created either back to the project itself (cycle) or to another used project.

These model-level usages may or may not be valid from the user point of view. Since the creation of usages is very easy, you can inadvertently create undesired usages. On the other hand, usages can be entirely benign. Our modeling tool cannot decide whether the usage is good or bad. For example, creating references from the used project *Implementation* to the used project *Requirements* is valid, while creating references in the opposite direction may be invalid since requirements usually are standalone, and implementation refers to them.

#### INFO: Case #2

Case #2

Reorganization or removal of sub-used projects may affect the projects or other used projects using the used project. This happens only in cases when a new refactored used project does not carry all of the contents that the old used project contained.

Consider a used project *ElectricComponents* with three sub-used projects: *Capacitors, Inductances,* and *Resistances.* Also consider a project *RadioSet,* which uses the used project *ElectricComponents.*Now let’s use one model element from each sub-used project in the project *RadioSet.* As a result, the three automated usages

*RadioSet* → *Capacitors,*

*RadioSet* → *Inductances,*and**

*RadioSet* → *Resistances*

will be created.

If the used project *ElectricComponents* was refactored by re-importing the sub-used project *Capacitors* and *Inductances* and completely separating the sub-used project *Resistances,* then when loading the project *RadioSet,* the automated usages *RadioSet* → *Capacitors* and *RadioSet* → *Inductances* will be automatically updated. However, the usage *RadioSet* → *Resistors* will remain unaddressed. It will appear as an unconfirmed used project usage, and you will have to confirm it, i.e., explicitly attach the used project *Resistances* to the project by creating the user-defined usage.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span>In earlier versions of our modeling tool, you could accidentally create unneeded or incorrect (from the user standpoint) dependencies between used projects in the read/write mode. Careless editing often caused cyclic module dependencies or inadvertent expansion of the scope of the other projects when the same used project was used in several other projects.</span></p><p><span style="text-align: center;">Automated project usages fix this problem.</span></p><p><span>Every automatically created usage must be backed by an existing user-defined used project usage. If it is not backed, the automated used project usage must be explicitly confirmed by the user. The usage cannot cause undesired effects in other projects while it is unconfirmed. </span></p><p><span>Automated used project usage <em>A </em></span><em>→ </em><span><em>B</em> is called </span><span><strong>unconfirmed</strong> </span><span>when there is no user-defined usage path from A to B (neither direct user-defined usage<em> A </em></span><em>→ </em><span><em>B</em> nor user-defined usage path of arbitrary length <em>A </em></span><em>→ ... → X → </em><span><em>B</em>). </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="87a6ff2e-53c4-4353-beee-84af03df8344"><ac:rich-text-body>User-defined usages are always confirmed.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>In ordinary circumstances, automated usages are not visible to the user. When the user modifies the main project’s model and adds dependencies to used projects or sub-used projects, these usages are proper. They are backed by the fact that the used project or sub-used project is attached to the project in some way; thus, the user-defined usage path refers from the main project to the used project or sub-used project.</p><p>However, there are situations when automated used project usages are not backed and thus unconfirmed.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="808f52e0-54e8-48ad-97c6-d303a33d4766"><ac:rich-text-body>An unconfirmed automated used project usage is considered to be invalid.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>This is just one of the project composition errors. Other project composition errors include similarity to recovered element errors, conflicting user-defined usage parameters, and cyclic dependencies.</p><p>Unconfirmed automated used project usages are caught by special <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Active Validation Rules for Links" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[automatic validation rules]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. They are displayed in validation results as warnings or errors depending on the project composition. The validation results prompt the user to remedy the situation by either confirming or rejecting the usage.</p><p>Unconfirmed usages can occur in several cases. They are described as follows.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2f54675e-1ffa-442e-9bb7-c0df1efb9642"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Case #1</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When you edit a model part belonging to a used project with read/write permissions, an additional model-level reference can be created either back to the project itself (cycle) or to another used project.</p><p>These model-level usages may or may not be valid from the user point of view. Since the creation of usages is very easy, you can inadvertently create undesired usages. On the other hand, usages can be entirely benign. Our modeling tool cannot decide whether the usage is good or bad. For example, creating references from the used project <em>Implementation</em> to the used project <em>Requirements</em> is valid, while creating references in the opposite direction may be invalid since requirements usually are standalone, and implementation refers to them.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2a59384f-809c-41ea-b15f-791023c1e3f8"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Case #2</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Reorganization or removal of sub-used projects may affect the projects or other used projects using the used project. This happens only in cases when a new refactored used project does not carry all of the contents that the old used project contained.</p><p>Consider a used project <em>ElectricComponents</em> with three sub-used projects: <em>Capacitors, Inductances,</em> and <em>Resistances.</em> Also consider a project <em>RadioSet,</em> which uses the used project <em>ElectricComponents. </em>Now let’s use one model element from each sub-used project in the project <em>RadioSet.</em> As a result, the three automated usages</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>RadioSet</em> → <em>Capacitors,</em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>RadioSet</em> → <em>Inductances, </em>and<em> </em></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>RadioSet</em> → <em>Resistances</em></p><p>will be created.</p><p>If the used project <em>ElectricComponents</em> was refactored by re-importing the sub-used project <em>Capacitors</em> and <em>Inductances</em> and completely separating the sub-used project <em>Resistances,</em> then when loading the project <em>RadioSet,</em> the automated usages <em>RadioSet</em> → <em>Capacitors</em> and <em>RadioSet</em> → <em>Inductances</em> will be automatically updated. However, the usage <em>RadioSet</em> → <em>Resistors</em> will remain unaddressed. It will appear as an unconfirmed used project usage, and you will have to confirm it, i.e., explicitly attach the used project <em>Resistances</em> to the project by creating the user-defined usage.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995669 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00330: (2026x Refresh1) Controlling direction

- page_id: `303995669`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995669/2026x+Refresh1+Controlling+direction
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:57.497+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Tools > (2026x Refresh1) Model Transformation Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Controlling Type Mapping Rule Behavior
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

By default, the same type map can be applied in two directions: forward and backward. The backward direction can be set by selecting the **Run type mapping in reverse order** check box in the third step of the Model Transformation Wizard. This is useful, when there are two related opposite transformations for some domain; for example, the same type map is used for both UML to XML schema and XML schema to UML transformations.

If you want to limit the directions, in which type map can be used, you can set the**defaultDirection** tag for your type map package. Possible values are **forward**, **reverse**, and **both** (default).

The direction can also be limited on a per-rule basis. This is controlled by setting the **direction** tag on the type map dependency. Then the mapping rule is excluded from the rule set when the type map is run in a different direction than specified for this rule.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>By default, the same type map can be applied in two directions: forward and backward. The backward direction can be set by selecting the <strong>Run type mapping in reverse order</strong> check box in the third step of the Model Transformation Wizard. This is useful, when there are two related opposite transformations for some domain; for example, the same type map is used for both UML to XML schema and XML schema to UML transformations.</p><p>If you want to limit the directions, in which type map can be used, you can set the<strong> defaultDirection</strong> tag for your type map package. Possible values are <strong>forward</strong>, <strong>reverse</strong>, and <strong>both</strong> (default).</p><p>The direction can also be limited on a per-rule basis. This is controlled by setting the <strong>direction</strong> tag on the type map dependency. Then the mapping rule is excluded from the rule set when the type map is run in a different direction than specified for this rule.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995574 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00331: (2026x Refresh1) Controlling type inheritance, any, and empty types

- page_id: `303995574`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995574/2026x+Refresh1+Controlling+type+inheritance+any+and+empty+types
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:48.344+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Tools > (2026x Refresh1) Model Transformation Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Controlling Type Mapping Rule Behavior
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can also control mapping behavior for the type inheritance. By default, derived subtypes are also mapped by the rule governing the parent type (unless, of course, they have their own rules for mapping). If the **blockInheritedSourceTypes** tagged value is set, derived types are not affected by this rule. Let’s review the following example:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Here T1, T2, and T5 are types in the source domain, while T2 and T4 are types in the destination domain. Given these two mappings (T1 > T2 and T3 > T4), the following statement is true: T1, and all types derived from it (such as T5), are mapped to the T2 type, except T3 and any of the types derived from it. These types are 
mapped to T4.

Now consider an example where blockInheritedSourceTypes is set:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In this case, T3, along with the types derived from it, are still mapped to T4. T1 is still mapped to T2. However, unlike the previous example, T5 and all the types inherited from T1 are NOT mapped to T2.

You can also control the mapping behavior of the type inheritance in the destination model. This is only effective on the transformation updates, the second (and successive) reapplications of the transformation. By default, derived subtypes in the destination model are not overwritten, since they are considered suitable substitutes of their parent. Let’s review the following example:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Here T1 is a type in the source domain, while T2 and T4 are types in the destination domain. Given this mapping (T1 > T2), on the first application of the transformation, type T1 residing in the source model will be mapped to type T2 in the destination model.

Now let us look at a case, where the user refines the destination model by changing the type on the destination model attribute from T2 to T4. This situation is quite common, for example, the user refines an attribute type from string to basic URI in the XML schema, or from Integer to *nonNegativeInteger*, and so forth. The essence is that the mapping for inherited types of T2 is performed as if there was a mapping T1 > T2 (default), T1 > T4, T1 > <any_other_type_inherited_from_T2>.

Now consider what happens, when we apply the **blockInheritedDestinationTypes** tagged value:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In this case, type T4 has no special treatment. If the user applies the transformation, T1 is mapped to T2. Afterwards the user refines the destination model, changing the attribute type from T2 to T4. If the user now updates the transformation, the attribute type is overwritten: T4 is reset back to T2.

When the user loads the type map in the reverse direction, the roles of the **blockInheritedSourceTypes** and **blockInheritedDestinationTypes** are transposed (unless of course the **direction** tag mandates that this mapping is not used in the reverse direction).

The special type **EmptySourceType** (residing in the *Model_Transformation_Profile.xml.zip*) is used in type maps to indicate that the attributes with no type should be mapped with this dependency.

The special type **EmptyDestinationType** (residing in the *Model_Transformation_Profile.xml.zip*) is used to indicate that the attributes in the destination classes should have no type after remapping (type removal).

The special type **AnySourceType** is a template that matches any type in the source model (see mapping rules for type inheritance). By using this type, together with the inheritance mapping rules, the user can specify that any other types not defined by the mapping should be interpreted by this mapping.

The special type **AnyDestinationType** is a template that matches any type in the destination model (see mapping rules for type inheritance).

Here is an example of template type usage:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

According to this rule, any types in the source model for which there are no other mapping rules should be stripped in the destination model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can also control mapping behavior for the type inheritance. By default, derived subtypes are also mapped by the rule governing the parent type (unless, of course, they have their own rules for mapping). If the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">blockInheritedSourceTypes</strong> tagged value is set, derived types are not affected by this rule. Let’s review the following example:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Transf_BlockInherited.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Controlling type inheritance, any, and empty types" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>Here T1, T2, and T5 are types in the source domain, while T2 and T4 are types in the destination domain. Given these two mappings (T1 &gt; T2 and T3 &gt; T4), the following statement is true: T1, and all types derived from it (such as T5), are mapped to the T2 type, except T3 and any of the types derived from it. These types are <br />mapped to T4.</p><p>Now consider an example where blockInheritedSourceTypes is set:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Transf_BlockInherited1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Controlling type inheritance, any, and empty types" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>In this case, T3, along with the types derived from it, are still mapped to T4. T1 is still mapped to T2. However, unlike the previous example, T5 and all the types inherited from T1 are NOT mapped to T2.</p><p>You can also control the mapping behavior of the type inheritance in the destination model. This is only effective on the transformation updates, the second (and successive) reapplications of the transformation. By default, derived subtypes in the destination model are not overwritten, since they are considered suitable substitutes of their parent. Let’s review the following example:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Transf_BlockInheritedDest.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Controlling type inheritance, any, and empty types" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>Here T1 is a type in the source domain, while T2 and T4 are types in the destination domain. Given this mapping (T1 &gt; T2), on the first application of the transformation, type T1 residing in the source model will be mapped to type T2 in the destination model.</p><p>Now let us look at a case, where the user refines the destination model by changing the type on the destination model attribute from T2 to T4. This situation is quite common, for example, the user refines an attribute type from string to basic URI in the XML schema, or from Integer to <em>nonNegativeInteger</em>, and so forth. The essence is that the mapping for inherited types of T2 is performed as if there was a mapping T1 &gt; T2 (default), T1 &gt; T4, T1 &gt; &lt;any_other_type_inherited_from_T2&gt;.</p><p>Now consider what happens, when we apply the <strong>blockInheritedDestinationTypes</strong> tagged value:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Transf_BlockInheritedDest1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Controlling type inheritance, any, and empty types" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>In this case, type T4 has no special treatment. If the user applies the transformation, T1 is mapped to T2. Afterwards the user refines the destination model, changing the attribute type from T2 to T4. If the user now updates the transformation, the attribute type is overwritten: T4 is reset back to T2.</p><p>When the user loads the type map in the reverse direction, the roles of the <strong>blockInheritedSourceTypes</strong> and <strong>blockInheritedDestinationTypes</strong> are transposed (unless of course the <strong>direction</strong> tag mandates that this mapping is not used in the reverse direction).</p><p>The special type <strong>EmptySourceType</strong> (residing in the <em>Model_Transformation_Profile.xml.zip</em>) is used in type maps to indicate that the attributes with no type should be mapped with this dependency.</p><p>The special type <strong>EmptyDestinationType</strong> (residing in the <em>Model_Transformation_Profile.xml.zip</em>) is used to indicate that the attributes in the destination classes should have no type after remapping (type removal).</p><p>The special type <strong>AnySourceType</strong> is a template that matches any type in the source model (see mapping rules for type inheritance). By using this type, together with the inheritance mapping rules, the user can specify that any other types not defined by the mapping should be interpreted by this mapping.</p><p>The special type <strong>AnyDestinationType</strong> is a template that matches any type in the destination model (see mapping rules for type inheritance).</p><p>Here is an example of template type usage:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Transf_AnySourceType.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Controlling type inheritance, any, and empty types" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>According to this rule, any types in the source model for which there are no other mapping rules should be stripped in the destination model.</p><p> </p>
````

- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1486330209

1486330210

1486330210

**Description**

A Conversation is an atomic element for a BPMN Collaboration diagram. It represents a set of message flows that is grouped together.

A Conversation can involve two or more Participants. A Conversation Link path will be from a Conversation to the involved Participants (Pools).

**Notation**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1486330209

**Related elements**

- Pool and Lane
- Conversation Link

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram

**Related procedure**

- Creating and Using Conversation Nodes

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994911 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00335: (2026x Refresh1) Converting Elements

- page_id: `303994911`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994911/2026x+Refresh1+Converting+Elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:53.442+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements > (2026x Refresh1) Refactoring
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### Original element

The original element is the element that will be converted. In other words, it is the conversion source.

###### Conversion target

The conversion target is the element type, to which the original element is going to be converted.

###### Converted element

The converted element is the element that type has been changed during the conversion.

###### Related elements

The related elements are elements having relations to the original element. Related elements can be the following:

- If the original element is a relationship, elements connected by this relationship are considered as related element to relationship.
- If the original element is not a relationship, relationships going from or to the original element are the related elements (such as an association, dependency, generalization, and others).
- Other related elements associated to the original element. They are properties of the original element, such as inner elements, types, and others.

###### Compatible properties

The compatible properties are original element specification that are compatible with the conversion target. The term “properties” includes related elements, inner elements, and all other elements listed in the element Specification window.

Compatible means that the converted element can have or own the particular property or properties of the original element.

###### Incompatible properties

Incompatible properties are original element specification that are incompatible with the conversion target. Incompatible means that the converted element can NOT have or can NOT own the particular property(-ies) of the original element.

Element conversion allows converting one element type to another. To be more specific, the element conversion functionality allows the UML model element conversion from one metaclass to another. For example, a class element can be converted to a use case, component, interface, or other. All compatible properties and all compatible related elements are moved from the source element to the converted element during the conversion.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- Relationships conversion is available.
- Multiple elements can be selected on the diagram or in the Model Browser to convert them to other types.

INLINE

To convert elements

1. From the selected element's shortcut menu, select Refactor > Convert To . The list with available conversion target types opens.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Select the element type from the commonly used element type list. The element is converted.
  - Select More Elements to see the whole list of available conversion targets. In the opened More Elements dialog, select the element type you need and click OK . The element is converted and selected element type is included as commonly used element type.

As of the modeling tool version 17.0.2, the following commands have been moved under the **Refactor** command in the shortcut menu of the particular element:

- Convert To > Output Pin for the selected Object Node or Input Pin.
- Convert To > Input Pin for the selected Object Node or Output Pin.
- Convert To > Object Node for the selected Input Pin or Output Pin.
- Convert To > Attribute(s) for the selected Association that has defined roles, that is, roles have names.
- Convert To > Association(s) for the selected Attribute. NOTE : The Association(s) command is available only if the attribute(s) type is specified.

- If the Refactor command is unavailable, make sure you have permissions to edit the selected element or related element(s).
- Use Undo to restore the original element and its data.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<strong>Object Node</strong> for the selected Input Pin or Output Pin.</li><li><strong>Convert To</strong> &gt; <strong>Attribute(s)</strong> for the selected Association that has defined roles, that is, roles have names.</li><li><strong>Convert To</strong> &gt; <strong>Association(s)</strong> for the selected Attribute. <strong>NOTE</strong>: The Association(s) command is available only if the attribute(s) type is specified.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="104de9ad-feea-4320-8cd5-d8310cb200a9"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If the <strong>Refactor</strong> command is unavailable, make sure you have permissions to edit the selected element or related element(s).</li><li>Use <strong>Undo</strong> to restore the original element and its data.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4810fea3-739c-4de7-90a8-6fa7ea57512d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="089ecc4f-400a-472c-916b-c7607d8b8764" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000121 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00336: (2026x Refresh1) Converting model between Enterprise Architecture Frameworks

- page_id: `304000121`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000121/2026x+Refresh1+Converting+model+between+Enterprise+Architecture+Frameworks
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:49.642+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Procedures
- labels: ['convert-to', 'convert-model-between-enterprise-architecture-frameworks', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can change the selected environment and its mode via the **Select Perspective** dialog. Just keep in mind that the project will not be converted automatically. When you select another perspective, only the GUI will change, but the project will not be migrated to the new framework.

To open the **Select Perspective** dialog

- Click Options > Perspectives > Perspectives . The Select Perspective dialog opens.

However, if you need to change the project's framework, you must convert it to the required framework.

Before converting a model from one framework to another, make sure there are no elements locked by other users.

To convert an enterprise model to another Enterprise Architecture Framework (EAF)

1. In the modeling tool's main menu, click **File** > **Convert****To** and select the needed perspective from the following:
  - DoDAF
  - DoDAF 2.0
  - MODAF
  - NAF
  - NAF 4.0
  - UAF
  - EA-BPMN
2. The **Question** dialog opens. Click Yes .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In the following example, you can see how UAF 1.3 viewpoints and view specifications align with NAF 4.0 packages.

- When the packages of one framework correlate with those of another, they are mapped during conversion, and the package names are adjusted to fit the framework. [ATTACHMENT filename='converting_frameworks.png']

- When the packages of one framework do not correlate with another framework, they remain unchanged after the conversion. [ATTACHMENT filename='after_conversion_package_unchanged.png']

- When you have packages with custom names, they do not change after the conversion. [ATTACHMENT filename='after_conversion_custom_name.png']

- When you have several packages with the same name, after the conversion, those packages are merged into one. [ATTACHMENT filename='after_conversion_merged.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can change the selected environment and its mode via the <strong>Select Perspective</strong> dialog. Just keep in mind that the project will not be converted automatically. When you select another perspective, only the GUI will change, but the project will not be migrated to the new framework.</p><p><br /></p><p>To open the <strong>Select Perspective</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ul><li>Click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Perspectives</strong> &gt; <strong>Perspectives</strong>.<br />The <strong>Select Perspective</strong> dialog opens.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>However, if you need to change the project's framework, you must convert it to the required framework.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="702e49fc-de2e-4409-ac4c-aa9cab08d5f4"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172B4D);">Before converting a model from one framework to another, make sure there are no elements locked by other users.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To convert an enterprise model to another Enterprise Architecture Framework (EAF)</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the modeling tool's main menu, click <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Convert</strong><strong> To</strong> and select <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">the needed perspective from the following:</span></span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">DoDAF</span></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">DoDAF 2.0</span></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">MODAF</span></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">NAF</span></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">NAF 4.0</span></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">UAF</span></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172B4D);">EA-BPMN</span></span></span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-yellow-bolder,#533F04);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The</span> <strong>Question</strong> dialog opens<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. </span></span>Click <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Yes</strong>.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="question_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Converting model between Enterprise Architecture Frameworks" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>In the following example, you can see how UAF 1.3 viewpoints and view specifications align with NAF 4.0 packages.</p><ul><li>When the packages of one framework correlate with those of another, they are mapped during conversion, and the package names are adjusted to fit the framework.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="converting_frameworks.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Converting model between Enterprise Architecture Frameworks" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><p><br /></p><ul><li>When the packages of one framework do not correlate with another framework, they remain unchanged after the conversion.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="after_conversion_package_unchanged.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Converting model between Enterprise Architecture Frameworks" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><p><br /></p><ul><li class="auto-cursor-target">When you have packages with custom names, they do not change after the conversion.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="after_conversion_custom_name.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Converting model between Enterprise Architecture Frameworks" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ul><li class="auto-cursor-target">When you have several packages with the same name, after the conversion, those packages are merged into one.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="after_conversion_merged.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Converting model between Enterprise Architecture Frameworks" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995248 space=MT version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 00338: (2026x Refresh1) Copy and paste symbol style

- page_id: `303995248`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995248/2026x+Refresh1+Copy+and+paste+symbol+style
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:24.327+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements > (2026x Refresh1) Formatting symbols
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A symbol style defined in symbol properties can be copied and pasted on another symbol. Copy a symbol and then select one or more other symbols of the same type where you need to paste copied properties.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To copy and paste symbol style

1. On the diagram pane, select symbol(s) which style properties you want to copy.
2. Copy style properties by doing one of the following:
  - From the Edit menu, select Copy .
  - Press Ctrl+C.
  - Click the [ATTACHMENT filename='copy.png'] Copy button on the diagram toolbar.
3. On the diagram pane, select the symbol(s) on which you want to paste style properties.
4. Paste style properties on the selected symbols by doing one of the following:
  - From the Edit menu, select Paste Style .
  - Press Ctrl+Shift+V.
  - Click the [ATTACHMENT filename='paste_style.png'] Paste Style button on the diagram toolbar.

You can copy and paste symbol properties of the more than one symbol at the time. Symbol properties will be pasted according to the element type.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Formatting symbols' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995318 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00339: (2026x Refresh1) Copying and opening element URLs

- page_id: `303995318`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995318/2026x+Refresh1+Copying+and+opening+element+URLs
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:26.901+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1213345435

1213345437

1213345436

You can copy a project element URL to a clipboard and share it with others as a quick reference to model elements.

Copying and opening element URLs do not work with the no install version (.zip) of a modeling tool.

To copy a project element URL, do one of the following

- Select Copy URL from the element shortcut menu in the Containment tree to copy the URL to a model element.
- Select the element symbol in a diagram and click Edit > Copy URL on the main menu to copy the URL to element symbol.

You can open any element through its URL by clicking the **Open Element from URL** command. The element will then be highlighted in the Containment tree or in the diagram. A custom URL "mdel://" is registered into windows registry. Activating the URL in other applications will allow you to start the modeling tool, open the project (if possible), and select one or more elements. You can paste URLs from the clipboard to any modeling tool diagram. Hyperlinks also can hold URLs of any model elements.

You cannot open elements using their URLs on an internet browser to start a modeling tool.

1213345434

**Related page**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994781 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00340: (2026x Refresh1) Copying and pasting data

- page_id: `303994781`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994781/2026x+Refresh1+Copying+and+pasting+data
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:48.451+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Tables > (2026x Refresh1) Basic tasks in tables
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can transfer information by copying and pasting data between tables and other resources, such as Excel spreadsheets, documents, web pages, email messages, and presentations. You are able to copy and paste data:

33

##### From other resources to modeling tool tables

You can copy information from other resources (e.g. Excel spreadsheets, documents, web pages, email messages, presentations) and paste it into a modeling tool table. After pasting the data to the modeling tool table, the new elements are created in the model. If elements already exist in the model, the information updates. The ID prefixes are set automatically after pasting data into modeling tool tables.

The following rules should be satisfied for a successful copy-and-paste operation to modeling tool tables:

- The cells must be editable.
- The number of columns in the other resource should be the same as the number of columns in the modeling tool table.
- The data types of columns in the modeling tool table must be compatible with the copied information from the other resource.

How to copy data from another resource and paste it into a modeling tool:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Instance table' space='']]]>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Generic table' space='']]]>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Glossary table' space='']]]>

To copy data from another resource and paste it to the modeling tool table

1. Copy the table from another resource. - The number of columns in that table should be the same as the number of columns in the table.- The data types of columns in the table must be compatible with the copied information.
2. Open the table where you want to paste the copied data.
3. In the Criteria area, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to specify the Element Type (in Generic table), Classifier (in Instance table), or Metric Suite (in Metric table).
4. In the table toolbar, click the **Show Columns/Columns** button and choose the columns that you want to fill with the copied information. The cells must be editable.
5. Do one of the following: 
 - Press Ctrl+V (Cmd+V on OS X). 
 - In the main toolbar, select **Edit** > **Paste.**The new elements are created in the model. If the element already exists in the model, the information updates. If elements have an ID prefix in another resource, they are set automatically after the copy-and-paste command only for pasted elements in the table. Manage ID prefixes in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Element Numbering dialog' space='']**Element Numbering** dialog.

##### From modeling tool tables to other resources

You can transfer information by copying and pasting data from modeling tool tables to other resources (e.g. Excel spreadsheets, documents, web pages, email messages, presentations).

To copy data from modeling tool tables and paste it to other resources

1. Select the required cells in the modeling tool table.
2. Do one of the following: - Press Ctrl+C (Cmd+C on OS X). - In the main toolbar, select Edit > Copy . The data is stored in the clipboard.
3. Open the other resource where you want to paste the data and select the first cell in the area you want to paste what you copied (e.g. Excel spreadsheet).
4. Press Ctrl+V. The contents of the cells are transferred to another resource.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Between cells in the modeling tool table

You can fill the table cells by copying the content from the modeling tool table cell(s) and pasting it to another cell(s) in the same or another modeling tool table(s). After pasting the data to a modeling tool table the new elements are created in the model. If elements already exist in the model, the information updates. The ID prefixes are set automatically after pasting data into modeling tool tables.

The following rules should be satisfied for a successful copy-and-paste operation to modeling tool tables:

- The cells must be editable.
- The data types of columns in the modeling tool table must be compatible with copied information.
- The cells contain the following data types: String, Boolean, Number, or Enumeration.

Copy and paste cell content into a modeling tool table

1. Select the cells that contain the data you want to copy.
2. Do one of the following: - Press Ctrl+C (Cmd+C on OS X). - In the main toolbar, select Edit > Copy . The data is stored in the clipboard.
3. Select single or multiple cells where you want to paste what you copied.
4. Do one of the following: - Press Ctrl+V (Cmd+V on OS X). - In the main toolbar, select Edit > Paste. The content of the cell(s) is duplicated in another cell(s).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Manage ID prefixes in the<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Element Numbering dialog" /><ac:link-body> <strong>Element Numbering</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h3>From modeling tool tables to other resources</h3><p>You can transfer information by copying and pasting data from modeling tool tables to other resources (e.g. Excel spreadsheets, documents, web pages, email messages, presentations).</p><p><br /></p><p>To copy data from modeling tool tables and paste it to other resources</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the required cells in the modeling tool table.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /> - Press Ctrl+C (Cmd+C on OS X).<br /> - In the main toolbar, select <strong>Edit</strong> &gt; <strong>Copy</strong>.<br />The data is stored in the clipboard.</li><li>Open the other resource where you want to paste the data and select the first cell in the area you want to paste what you copied (e.g. Excel spreadsheet).</li><li>Press Ctrl+V.<br />The contents of the cells are transferred to another resource.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="copy_paste_operation_from MD_table_to_excel.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Copying and pasting data" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Between cells in the modeling tool table</h3><p>You can fill the table cells by copying the content from the modeling tool table cell(s) and pasting it to another cell(s) in the same or another modeling tool table(s). After pasting the data to a modeling tool table the new elements are created in the model. If elements already exist in the model, the information updates. The ID prefixes are set automatically after pasting data into modeling tool tables.</p><p>The following rules should be satisfied for a successful copy-and-paste operation to modeling tool tables:</p><ul><li>The cells must be editable.</li><li><p>The data types of columns in the modeling tool table must be compatible with copied information.</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">The cells contain the following data types: String, Boolean, Number, or Enumeration.</p></li></ul><p>Copy and paste cell content into a modeling tool table</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the cells that contain the data you want to copy.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /> - Press Ctrl+C (Cmd+C on OS X).<br /> - In the main toolbar, select <strong>Edit</strong> &gt; <strong>Copy</strong>.<br />The data is stored in the clipboard.</li><li>Select single or multiple cells where you want to paste what you copied.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /> - Press Ctrl+V (Cmd+V on OS X).<br /> - In the main toolbar, select <strong>Edit</strong> &gt; <strong>Paste.</strong><br />The content of the cell(s) is duplicated in another cell(s).</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="copy_paste_operation_between_cells_in_MD_table.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Copying and pasting data" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to Top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992965 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00341: (2026x Refresh1) Copying and pasting objects

- page_id: `303992965`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992965/2026x+Refresh1+Copying+and+pasting+objects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:22:40.999+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagramming
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1422299706

1422299708

1422299707

Modeling tools support all common copying/cutting and pasting functions, as well as advanced copying and pasting functions. You can copy and paste objects from one project to another. You can find these options in the **Edit** menu.

Advanced commands for copying:

| Command | Shortcut | Description |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | Windows | Mac OS |  |
| Copy URL |  |  | Copies the URL of the selected project element or element symbol to the clipboard (the project element and the element symbol have different URLs). To copy the URL of an element, select Copy URL from the element shortcut menu in the Containment tree. To copy the URL of an element symbol, select the the symbol and click Edit > Copy URL.You can use URLs to open any elements by clicking the Open Element from URL command. The element is then highlighted in the Containment tree or in a diagram. Activating the URL in another application allows you to start the modeling tool, open a project (if possible), and select any elements. You can paste URLs from the clipboard to any diagram. Hyperlinks can also hold URLs of any model elements. |
| Copy as BMP Image | Ctrl+Shift+B | ⌘⇧B | Copies the selected shapes to the clipboard. If no shapes are selected, the active diagram is copied. The .bmp image format provides the best image quality. The image files of this format are typically not compressed, resulting in a large file. |
| Copy as EMF Image | Ctrl+Shift+E | ⌘⇧E | Copies the selected shapes to the clipboard. If no shapes are selected, the active diagram is copied. The .emf format image files are smaller, so using this format reduces network traffic. Additionally, the .emf format supports language specific symbols.EMF does not support gradient fill. Because icons of the modeling tool are stored in the SVG format, it may be exported incorrectly.To render SVG icons as raster images in EMF, on the main menu, click Options > Environment > General > Image Export and set the Render SVG Icons as Raster Images in EMF option to true. |
| Copy as JPG Image | Ctrl+Shift+J | ⌘⇧J | Copies the selected shapes to the clipboard. If no shapes are selected, the active diagram is copied. The .jpg format produces relatively small file sizes, but when compressing, the .jpg image file loses data, which results in lower quality. Thus, this format is suitable for final distribution of images, but is not recommended if images are to be edited. |
| Copy as PNG Image | Ctrl+Shift+P | ⌘⇧P | Copies the selected shapes to the clipboard. If no shapes are selected, the active diagram is copied. The .png format is best suited for storing images during the editing process because of its lossless compression. Compared to the .jpg format, the .png excels when the image has large areas of uniform color. It also works well in online viewing applications, such as web browsers. |

Advanced commands for pasting:

| Command | Shortcut | Description |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | Windows | Mac OS |  |
| Paste with New Element | Ctrl+E | ⌘E | Pastes one or more copied model elements by creating new model elements. Use this command when copying or cutting owned symbols from one shape to another in a diagram. |
| Paste Style | Ctrl+Shift+V | ⌘⇧V | Pastes symbol properties to the selected symbol. You can copy and paste symbol properties of more than one symbol at the same time. Symbol properties are pasted according to the element type. For example, on a class symbol, the class symbol properties are pasted, and on a package symbol, the package symbol properties are pasted, etc. |
| Paste as Diagram Overview |  |  | Creates a new diagram overview representing selected elements. A diagram overview shape provides the ability to overview other diagrams on a diagram pane. Moreover, using diagram overview shape you can overview dependency matrices, tables, and relation maps. |

In addition to the described commands, there you have the ability to copy the whole diagram or the selected shapes and paste them to Microsoft Office or other applications.

Not all symbols can be copied/cut and pasted.

1422299705

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993158 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00342: (2026x Refresh1) Copying text or images to diagrams

- page_id: `303993158`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993158/2026x+Refresh1+Copying+text+or+images+to+diagrams
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:22:53.196+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagramming
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

570108383

570108385

570108384

You can copy the text or images to a diagram. The copied text or image is pasted into a text box or an image shape. The modeling tool supports the HTML and plain text, and images of gif, jpg, svg, and png image file types. This copy and paste functionality allows you to drag and drop data from other applications, for example, web browsers.

To copy the text or image to a diagram

1. Select the text or image.
2. Press Ctrl+C.
3. Open the diagram.
4. Press Ctrl+V. The Paste Special dialog opens.
5. Select Image , Plain Text , or HTML Text according to what you want to paste on the diagram.

570108382

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Diagramming' space='']

The copied text or image is pasted into a text box or an image shape. The modeling tool supports the HTML and plain text, and images of gif, jpg, svg, and png image file types. This copy and paste functionality allows you to drag and drop data from other applications, for example, web browsers.</p><p><br /></p><p>To copy the text or image to a diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the text or image.</li><li>Press Ctrl+C.</li><li>Open the diagram.</li><li>Press Ctrl+V. The <strong>Paste Special</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select <strong>Image</strong>, <strong>Plain Text</strong>, or <strong>HTML Text</strong> according to what you want to paste on the diagram.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="54003f95-1740-4eec-bac5-3ee0534d93e4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">570108382</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Diagramming" /></ac:link><strong><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></strong></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00343: (2026x Refresh1) CORBA IDL diagram

- page_id: `303994172`
- space_key: `MT`

- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:03.146+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Extension diagrams
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Functionalities

135158942084

INLINE

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Functionalities

345982345

INLINE

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Functionalities

23458729345

INLINE

#### NOTE: Functionalities

Functionalities

This feature is available in Architect and Enterprise editions.

The CORBA IDL diagram facilitates the creation of CORBA IDL elements. The following patterns are also available for CORBA IDL: Interface, Value Type, Type Definition, Sequence, Array, Fixed, Union, Enumeration, Struct, and Exception.

You can select either the UML Interface or the UML Class as a base element for the CORBA Interface. For more information about the CORBA IDL Interface implementation, see CORBA Interface Implementation.

Reference: UMLTMProfile for CORBATMSpecification, Version 1.0, April 2002.[http://www.omg.org/technology/documents/formal/profile_corba.htm](http://www.omg.org/technology/documents/formal/profile_corba.htm)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of the CORBA IDL diagram *Tickets Purchasing*

43578293475

INLINE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000138 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00344: (2026x Refresh1) Coverage analysis in UAF

- page_id: `304000138`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000138/2026x+Refresh1+Coverage+analysis+in+UAF
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:51.883+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Procedures
- labels: ['coverage-analysis', 'metrics', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Coverage analysis is based on the calculation of your model parameters. For this purpose, metrics are introduced. You can calculate metrics for the selected elements using the predefined metric suites. Also, you can create your own metrics suites from scratch or based on the predefined metric suites.

Metric is an item of information about some specific aspect of the model. The collection of various metrics can be used to evaluate the current state of your model. Calculated at regular intervals they can help track the evolution of the model development. The results of metric calculation are stored in the metric tables.

**Related procedures**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>Coverage analysis is based on the calculation of your model parameters. For this purpose, metrics are introduced. You can calculate metrics for the selected elements using the predefined metric suites. Also, you can create your own metrics suites from scratch or based on the predefined metric suites.</p><p>Metric is an item of information about some specific aspect of the model. The collection of various metrics can be used to evaluate the current state of your model. Calculated at regular intervals they can help track the evolution of the model development. The results of metric calculation are stored in the metric tables.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="7ea2a4ba-394c-438b-ab17-9ed0521a093f" /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999589 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00345: (2026x Refresh1) Covering use cases of Excel Import Plugin

- page_id: `303999589`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999589/2026x+Refresh1+Covering+use+cases+of+Excel+Import+Plugin
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:49:29.801+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements > (2026x Refresh1) Importing data from Excel and CSV files
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page is dedicated for those who used the Excel Import Plugin for data import to the model. As of 2021x version this plugin is deprecated and import functionality is covered by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Sync with Excel or CSV files' space=''] (import to table) and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Importing data from Excel and CSV files' space=''] (import to model) features. To explain the mapping of deprecated Excel Import Plugin, read the following sections:

5

##### Removing auto usages

Before importing data from Excel or CSV files by using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Importing data from Excel and CSV files' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Sync with Excel or CSV files' space=''] features, you must remove auto usages of Excel Import Plugin, since 20.0 version its no longer available to use. [Learn more about automated project usages >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Automated+project+usages)

To remove the Excel Import Plugin usage from your project

1. Open the project.
2. In the Containment tree, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select Show Auxiliary Resources .
3. Right-click an Excel Import Profile package in the Containment tree and, from its shortcut menu, select Project Usages > Remove. After removal, all the elements of the used project disappear from the Containment tree, and all the references to these elements are cleared from other model places.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Preparing the Excel spreadsheet

Before importing data from Excel file by using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Importing data from Excel and CSV files' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Sync with Excel or CSV files' space=''] features, we recommend to make sure the data in your Excel file is in line. As shown in the following figure, the merged cells are eliminated by showing nested structure in line. [Learn more about preparing Excel file to import >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Importing+data+from+Excel+or+CSV+files#ImportingdatafromExcelorCSVfiles-PreparingExcelorCSVfiletoimport)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The comparison of Excel tables available to import by using the Excel Import Plugin and Excel/CSV Sync or Excel/CSV Import features.

##### User interface comparison

To import data from Excel or CSV file by using the Excel Import Plugin, three dialogs were used to import table heading, create mapping and import data. The same cases are covered with one dialog depending where you want to import data:

- To the table use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Excel and CSV Sync Options dialog' space='']**Excel/CSV Sync Options** dialog .
- To the model use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='']**Excel/CSV Import** dialog .

You cannot create a diagram when importing data by using the Excel/CSV Sync or the Excel/CSV Import features.

The figure below illustrates the differences between dialogs of Excel Import Plugin and **Excel/CSV Sync Options** or **Excel/CSV Import**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The UI differences between Excel Import Plugin and Excel/CSV Sync and Excel/CSV Import.

##### Mapping comparison

The Excel Import Plugin allows you to create mapping in the **Create Mapping** dialog that generates the class diagram. In the **Excel/CSV Sync Options** dialog you create mapping directly in the dialog. The comparison of the Excel Import Plugin and Excel/CSV Sync mappings are provided in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The mapping differences between Excel Import Plugin and Excel/CSV Sync.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>This page is dedicated for those who used the Excel Import Plugin for data import to the model. As of 2021x version this plugin is deprecated and import functionality is covered by <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Sync with Excel or CSV files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Excel/CSV Sync]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (import to table) and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Importing data from Excel and CSV files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Excel/CSV Import]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (import to model) features. To explain the mapping of deprecated Excel Import Plugin, read the following sections:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" In the <strong>Excel/CSV Sync Options</strong> dialog you create mapping directly in the dialog. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990077 space=MT version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 00346: (2026x Refresh1) Cr Capability Roadmap

- page_id: `303990077`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990077/2026x+Refresh1+Cr+Capability+Roadmap
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:15.049+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF 4.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Concepts viewpoint
- labels: ['cr-capability-roadmap', 'cr', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

62002807

62002809

62002808

**Description**

The Cr Capability Roadmap view provides a representation of the actual or estimated availability of capabilities over a period of time (derived from capability delivery milestones in acquisition projects). The Cr view support the Capability Audit process and similar processes used across different communities of interest by providing a method to identify gaps or duplication in capability provision. The Cr view indicates capability increments, which are derived from delivery milestones within acquisition projects.

The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) C2 Enterprise Vision' space=''] view specifies the requirement for capability at each Enterprise Phase, whereas the Cr view reflects the output of capability programmes (e.g. acquisition, training, etc.) over time. The requirement specified in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) C2 Enterprise Vision' space='']may or may not be met by those programmes.**

The Cr view may be used to:

- Plan Capability (capability phasing).
- Plan Capability integration.
- Analyze Capability gap.
- Manage acquisition with High-level dashboard.

**Implementation**

The Cr view can be represented using a Cr Capability Roadmap diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

62002806

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Configuration' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exhibits' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Project Milestone' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Project' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The Cr view support the Capability Audit process and similar processes used across different communities of interest by providing a method to identify gaps or duplication in capability provision. The Cr view indicates capability increments, which are derived from delivery milestones within acquisition projects.</p><p>The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) C2 Enterprise Vision" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[C2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view specifies the requirement for capability at each Enterprise Phase, whereas the Cr view reflects the output of capability programmes (e.g. acquisition, training, etc.) over time. The requirement specified in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) C2 Enterprise Vision" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[C2 ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>may or may not be met by those programmes.<em> <br /></em></p><p>The Cr view may be used to:</p><ul><li>Plan Capability (capability phasing).</li><li>Plan Capability integration.</li><li>Analyze Capability gap.</li><li>Manage acquisition with High-level dashboard.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Cr view can be represented using a Cr Capability Roadmap diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Cr Capability Roadmap" ac:alt="Cr Capability Roadmap"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cr.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Cr Capability Roadmap" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="110b6ad5-d88b-4404-9b11-b617f6018fef"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">62002806</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Capability" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Capability Configuration" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Exhibits" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Actual Project Milestone" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Actual Project" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="e12ef789-057a-4d97-8746-1faa256e24ac" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
