<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994361 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00348: (2026x Refresh1) Creating a diagram overview shape

- page_id: `303994361`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994361/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+a+diagram+overview+shape
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:10.057+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagramming > (2026x Refresh1) Overviewing other diagrams
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can use one of the following ways to create a diagram overview shape:

- Drag the diagram shape from the Model Browser and change it into the diagram overview shape afterward . This way created diagram overview shape shows the full content of the corresponding diagram and is updated automatically according to all changes made in this diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='dgr_and_dgr_overview_shape_full.png']
- **Use the [IMAGE alt='' src='']Diagram Overview button that is located in the diagram palette**. This way created diagram overview shape shows the full content of the corresponding diagram and is updated automatically according to all changes made in this diagram.
- **Paste a copied part of the diagram’s content using a special command from the main menu**. This way created diagram overview shape shows any copied part of the corresponding diagram’s content and is updated automatically according only to *the changes made in this copied part*. Note that this way cannot be used for dependency matrices, tables, and relation maps, as it is not allowed to copy and paste different parts of them. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create a diagram overview shape by dragging it to the diagram pane

1. Open a diagram wherein you want to create the diagram overview shape.
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Model Browser' space=''] select a diagram you want to overview.
3. Drag the diagram shape to the open diagram pane. If you cannot drag the diagram shape to the open diagram pane when working with a Teamwork Cloud project, make sure you have the right to edit the model of this project and then try to lock for edit this diagram.
4. Make the diagram shape show the full content of the corresponding diagram by doing one of the following:
  - Right-click the diagram shape, and from the shortcut menu, select [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Symbol Properties dialog' space=''] . In the open dialog, set the Show Diagram Overview Content property value to true . [ATTACHMENT filename='show_dgr_overview_content_symbol_properties.png']
  - Click the diagram shape, and on the smart manipulator toolbar, select the [IMAGE alt='' src='']button. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The diagram shape will be changed to the diagram overview shape that shows the full content of the corresponding diagram. The content of the diagram overview shape will be updated automatically according to*all changes* made in the corresponding diagram. Double-click the diagram overview shape to open the corresponding diagram.

To create a diagram overview shape by using the diagram palette

1. Open a diagram wherein you want to create the diagram overview shape.
2. On the diagram palette, click the **Diagram Overview** button that is located within the **Common** buttons group. The **Select Diagram** dialog is open. If the diagram palette is inactive within a Teamwork Cloud project, make sure you have the right to edit the model of this project and then try to lock for edit this diagram.
3. Select a diagram for which you want to create a diagram overview shape and click OK .
4. Click a free space of the diagram pane.

To create a diagram overview shape by pasting any part of the diagram’s content

1. Select the element shapes that you want to appear in the diagram overview shape and then copy them.
2. Open the diagram wherein you want to create the diagram overview shape.
3. From the **Edit** menu, select **Paste as Diagram Overview**. The content will be updated automatically according to the changes made in the copied part of the corresponding diagram only.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Modifying the content of a diagram overview shape' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can use one of the following ways to create a diagram overview shape:</p><ul><li data-uuid="6d91f0f3-7179-4312-ab23-3db65e75fa8c"><strong>Drag the diagram shape from the Model Browser and change it into the diagram overview shape afterward</strong>. This way created diagram overview shape shows the full content of the corresponding diagram and is updated automatically according to all changes made in this diagram.<br /><ac:image ac:title="An example of diagram and its diagram overview shape showing the full content of the diagram" ac:alt="An example of diagram and its diagram overview shape showing the full content of the diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="dgr_and_dgr_overview_shape_full.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating a diagram overview shape" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </li><li data-uuid="969c636f-a274-4eb0-b1ef-f3de190661ea"><p><strong>Use the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="diagram_overview.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating a diagram overview shape" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>Diagram Overview button that is located in the diagram palette</strong>. This way created diagram overview shape shows the full content of the corresponding diagram and is updated automatically according to all changes made in this diagram. </p></li><li data-uuid="24a1798a-a05b-4a46-81f7-c89a4c89057f"><p><strong>Paste a copied part of the diagram’s content using a special command from the main menu</strong>. This way created diagram overview shape shows any copied part of the corresponding diagram’s content and is updated automatically according only to <em>the changes made in this copied part</em>. Note that this way cannot be used for dependency matrices, tables, and relation maps, as it is not allowed to copy and paste different parts of them.<br /><ac:image ac:title="An example of diagram and its diagram overview shape showing only the part of the diagram content" ac:alt="An example of diagram and its diagram overview shape showing only the part of the diagram content"><ri:attachment ri:filename="dgr_and_dgr_overview_shape_part.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating a diagram overview shape" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </p></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To create a diagram overview shape by dragging it to the diagram pane</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="8b64b138-a2a4-4c45-a3bd-e88bdbaafaf7">Open a diagram wherein you want to create the diagram overview shape.</li><li data-uuid="e0e8936b-e738-4e55-aa17-79b42db1a1d2">In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Model Browser" /></ac:link></strong> select a diagram you want to overview.</li><li data-uuid="926b1c5b-49f3-430f-8174-27e55391eb62"><p>Drag the diagram shape to the open diagram pane.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="07f8d55a-b7b3-45bb-bd6e-70cfde831e0c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you cannot drag the diagram shape to the open diagram pane when working with a Teamwork Cloud project, make sure you have the right to edit the model of this project and then try to lock for edit this diagram.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="75fb0218-e647-4513-9df7-46c6cca5d1d3">Make the diagram shape show the full content of the corresponding diagram by doing one of the following:<ul><li>Right-click the diagram shape, and from the shortcut menu, select <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>. In the open dialog, set the <strong>Show Diagram Overview Content</strong> property value to <em>true</em>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_dgr_overview_content_symbol_properties.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating a diagram overview shape" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p>Click the diagram shape, and on the smart manipulator toolbar, select the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_dgr_overview_content_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating a diagram overview shape" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>button.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_dgr_overview_content.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating a diagram overview shape" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />The diagram shape will be changed to the diagram overview shape that shows the full content of the corresponding diagram.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a268ba55-34d5-483c-aefe-67bb081ec3ff"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The content of the diagram overview shape will be updated automatically according to<em> all changes</em> made in the corresponding diagram.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="61cce830-fc71-418e-8c98-b09ad3fd38ee"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Double-click the diagram overview shape to open the corresponding diagram.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To create a diagram overview shape by using the diagram palette</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="2e091368-811b-43bb-b685-c012c7828479">Open a diagram wherein you want to create the diagram overview shape.</li><li data-uuid="07c4e97a-171a-4fcd-9bc2-ca2ff54dbff6"><p>On the diagram palette, click the <strong>Diagram Overview</strong> button that is located within the <strong>Common</strong> buttons group. The <strong>Select Diagram</strong> dialog is open.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4403312c-45d3-4539-b476-bfe94f4e2d99"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the diagram palette is inactive within a Teamwork Cloud project, make sure you have the right to edit the model of this project and then try to lock for edit this diagram.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="03ff8d2f-5fb5-4757-af51-989a1c100678">Select a diagram for which you want to create a diagram overview shape and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="db4e5a41-d54f-434a-b42f-f5a9923d1568">Click a free space of the diagram pane.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To create a diagram overview shape by pasting any part of the diagram’s content</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="aadb2da2-d8b4-4f69-bb41-554e288c8c47">Select the element shapes that you want to appear in the diagram overview shape and then copy them.</li><li data-uuid="b15271a3-2ac5-472b-b030-cbdc872b65fa">Open the diagram wherein you want to create the diagram overview shape.</li><li data-uuid="07bf24ae-8225-4e84-953a-4c5fbcd17347"><p>From the <strong>Edit</strong> menu, select <strong>Paste as Diagram Overview</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a8b3cfe0-8867-4dc9-a342-097ce8c7f82c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The content will be updated automatically according to the changes made in the copied part of the corresponding diagram only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="44856a02-e9c9-47bf-acde-bfd3eb94767e"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Modifying the content of a diagram overview shape" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998710 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00349: (2026x Refresh1) Creating a Macro

- page_id: `303998710`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998710/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+a+Macro
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:29:54.560+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Macro engine
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Once you have selected a default macro language (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Selecting a default macro language' space='']), you can create a new macro by using the **Create Macro** dialog, see figure below. The dialog allows you to specify a macro language, enter source code, and save it.

To create a new macro

1. Click Tools > Macros > Create Macro on the main menu. The Create Macro dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='create-macro.png']
2. Select a macro language in the **Macro Language** box. To ensure the best performance experience, it is strongly suggested that you use the recommended language (Groovy). The icon color next to the language selection reflects performance, (i.e., green for fast, yellow for medium, and red for slow). If the performance is unknown, an info icon appears.
3. Enter the source code in the text box.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to test and make sure that the source code works properly.
5. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] . The Macro Information dialog opens. Follow the steps described on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Adding a Macro and editing Macro information' space=''] page.
6. After clicking OK in the Macro Information dialog, the new macro will be saved in the location you have specified in the File text box.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Once you have selected a default macro language (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Selecting a default macro language" /></ac:link>), you can create a new macro by using the <strong>Create Macro</strong> dialog, see figure below. The dialog allows you to specify a macro language, enter source code, and save it.</p><p>To create a new macro</p><hr /><ol><li>Click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Macros</strong> &gt; <strong>Create Macro</strong> on the main menu. The<strong> Create Macro</strong> dialog opens.<br /><ac:image ac:title="The Create Macro Dialog" ac:alt="The Create Macro Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create-macro.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating a Macro" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"> Select a macro language in the <strong>Macro Language</strong> box.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="08c8f1bf-cf76-4a9e-a576-3bba18f3c988"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To ensure the best performance experience, it is strongly suggested that you use the recommended language (Groovy). The icon color next to the language selection reflects performance, (i.e., green for fast, yellow for medium, and red for slow). If the performance is unknown, an info icon appears.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li> Enter the source code in the text box.</li><li> Click <strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="run-btn.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating a Macro" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong> to test and make sure that the source code works properly.</li><li> Click <strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="save-btn.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating a Macro" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong>. The <strong>Macro Information</strong> dialog opens. Follow the steps described on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding a Macro and editing Macro information" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Adding a Macro and Its Information]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</li><li> After clicking <strong>OK</strong> in the <strong>Macro Information</strong> dialog, the new macro will be saved in the location you have specified in the <strong>File</strong> text box.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995032 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00350: (2026x Refresh1) Creating a Metric Table

- page_id: `303995032`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995032/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+a+Metric+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:01.089+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Tables > (2026x Refresh1) Metric table
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Although you can create other types of tables in various ways, there is only one correct way to create a Metric Table. This method of creating Metric Tables saves much time compared to the more common ways of table (and even diagram) creation, as these methods for a Metric Table creation start with an empty table. This requires further effort on your part to fill it (specify the metric suite, create an empty row, define parameters, and then calculate the metrics).

To create a Metric Table

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Containment tab' space=''], right-click thePackage which contains e.g. requirements, system structure, system behavior, which will be used as a scope for the metrics calculation.
2. From the shortcut menu of the element, select **Tools** > **Metrics** > **New Metric Table**.
3. In the **Create Metric Table** dialog, specify the following: 
 - Type the Metric Table name; 
 - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to specify the Metric Table owner.By default, the selected Package in the Containment tree is chosen. 
 - From the list of available metric suites, select the check boxes next to the Metric Suites you want to use.There are three groups of predefined Metric Suites: If predefined Metric Suites do not allow to evaluate the current state of your model, you can create a custom Metric Suite according to your needs.[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Metric Suites' space='']>]]> [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Metric Suite descriptionsSelect a Metric Suite and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to read its description in the description area of the **Create Metric Table** dialog. Carefully read descriptions of each metric suite. This helps you decide which one best suits your needs.
  - Requirement Metric Suites (Obsolete)
  - Requirement Coverage (Treat Owner As Grouping Element)
  - Requirement Coverage (Treat Owner As Requirement)
4. Click **OK**.

The table of a particular Metric Suite is created for the selected Package. It is already filled with data because metrics calculated at the time you create the table. These metrics are actually stored in the Package owned by the element for which they have been calculated. As example, see the figure below. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If one or more metrics are not calculated, make sure the Metric Suite is specified correctly:

- Check the metric definition – the formula could be specified incorrectly.
- Check the parameter definition – the metric definition probably cannot access parameter values.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991837 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00351: (2026x Refresh1) Creating a new project

- page_id: `303991837`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991837/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+a+new+project
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:17.766+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects > (2026x Refresh1) Creating projects
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1428335651

1428335654

1428335653

INLINE

All project information is stored in a single file that has the same name as the project itself.

A newly created project consists of the following packages:

- The empty root package Model, storing all model elements.
- File View package, containing components that are created during code engineering and representing source files. Adding a resident element to a particular component causes that element to be generated within the source file.
- UML Standard Profile, containing stereotypes that are necessary in order to work with the modeling tool, primitive data types and constraints (which are UML standard), and UML 2 metamodel elements. The following data types are specified in the modeling tool: boolean, byte, char, date, double, float, int, Integer, real long, short, void, and string.

You can create your own packages for keeping model elements as well. By default, packages cannot be deleted or renamed in a project (except for the *File View* package).

To start a new project, first, you must create a new workspace for it. You can select a project type from the following domains:

- General-Purpose Modeling (UML, Use Case, Guide to UML Diagrams projects)
- System Engineering (SysML v1 project)
- Enterprise Modeling (DoDAF, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF projects)
- Software Engineering (Scrum project, Project from Existing Source Code)
- Business Process Modeling (BPMN 2.0 project)
- Service-Oriented Modeling (Cameo SOA+ project)
- Other (Project from Template, Process Guide project)

Upon creation of a new project, the **General-Purpose Modeling** domain opens by default.

To create a new workspace for a blank project

1. Do one of the following:

- 
  - From the File menu, select New Project .
  - On the main toolbar, click the New Project button.
  - Press Ctrl + Shift + N.

Any of the above actions opens the **New Project** dialog.

2. Select the **UML Project** in the **General-Purpose Modeling** domain.

3. Specify the file name in the **Name** box.

4. Click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button to select the location to store the created project.

5. Click **OK**.

1428335648

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating projects' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Project Properties dialog' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with multiple projects' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Adding a resident element to a particular component causes that element to be generated within the source file.</li><li class="atl-forced-newline"><em>UML Standard Profile,</em> containing stereotypes that are necessary in order to work with the modeling tool, primitive data types and constraints (which are UML standard), and UML 2 metamodel elements. The following data types are specified in the modeling tool: boolean, byte, char, date, double, float, int, Integer, real long, short, void, and string.</li></ul><p class="atl-forced-newline">You can create your own packages for keeping model elements as well. By default, packages cannot be deleted or renamed in a project (except for the <em>File View</em> package).</p><p class="atl-forced-newline">To start a new project, first, you must create a new workspace for it. You can select a project type from the following domains:</p><ul><li class="atl-forced-newline"><strong>General-Purpose Modeling</strong> (UML, Use Case, Guide to UML Diagrams projects)</li><li class="atl-forced-newline"><strong>System Engineering</strong> (SysML v1 project)</li><li class="atl-forced-newline"><strong>Enterprise Modeling</strong> (DoDAF, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF projects)</li><li class="atl-forced-newline"><strong>Software Engineering</strong> (Scrum project, Project from Existing Source Code)</li><li class="atl-forced-newline"><strong>Business Process Modeling</strong> (BPMN 2.0 project)</li><li class="atl-forced-newline"><strong>Service-Oriented Modeling</strong> (Cameo SOA+ project)</li><li class="atl-forced-newline"><strong>Other</strong> (Project from Template, Process Guide project)</li></ul><p class="atl-forced-newline">Upon creation of a new project, the <strong>General-Purpose Modeling</strong> domain opens by default.</p><p class="atl-forced-newline"><br /></p><p class="atl-forced-newline">To create a new workspace for a blank project</p><hr /><ol><li class="atl-forced-newline">Do one of the following:</li></ol><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li class="atl-forced-newline">From the <strong>File</strong> menu, select <strong>New Project</strong>.</li><li class="atl-forced-newline">On the main toolbar, click the <strong>New Project</strong> button.</li><li class="atl-forced-newline">Press Ctrl + Shift + N.</li></ul></li></ul><p class="atl-forced-newline" style="margin-left: 60.0px;">Any of the above actions opens the <strong>New Project</strong> dialog. </p><p class="atl-forced-newline" style="margin-left: 30.0px;">2. Select the <strong>UML Project</strong> in the <strong>General-Purpose Modeling</strong> domain.</p><p class="atl-forced-newline" style="margin-left: 30.0px;">3. Specify the file name in the <strong>Name</strong> box.</p><p class="atl-forced-newline" style="margin-left: 30.0px;">4. Click the <ac:image ac:height="250"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_project_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating a new project" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button to select the location to store the created project.</p><p class="atl-forced-newline" style="margin-left: 30.0px;">5. Click <strong>OK</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2476cc15-2c7a-46a2-938a-95f5957aa355"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1428335648</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating projects" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Project Properties dialog" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with multiple projects" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000455 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00352: (2026x Refresh1) Creating a new slide for a presentation

- page_id: `304000455`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000455/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+a+new+slide+for+a+presentation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:32:13.090+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Template Creation Tutorial > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard Directives > (2026x Refresh1) #forpage directive
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a new slide for a presentation

1. Open OpenOffice.org Impress or Microsoft PowerPoint.
2. Create two text boxes.
3. Type in: 
(i) one of the text boxes: text (ii) and type in the other text box: text The result will appear as shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The result of the generated report will appear shown in the figure below. 
 
 [IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a new slide for a presentation</p><hr /><ol><li>Open <a href="http://OpenOffice.org">OpenOffice.org</a> Impress or Microsoft PowerPoint.</li><li>Create two text boxes.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Type in:<br />(i) one of the text boxes:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="94c10f12-41cb-4d4d-a942-9a270ff64ad8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#forpage($dia in $Diagram) 
$dia.name]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">(ii) and type in the other text box:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a112c55c-f1ea-4b2b-9b37-d8c22d0489ae"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$dia.image 
#endpage]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The result will appear as shown in the figure below.</p></li></ol><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddavbf2e5609b8cf026cfb71c63ca946bfca.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating a new slide for a presentation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The result of the generated report will appear shown in the figure below.<br /> <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <ac:image ac:title="A Generated Report" ac:alt="A Generated Report"><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddav8dfe469fffe291fde883d6b38b0573a7.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating a new slide for a presentation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994480 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00353: (2026x Refresh1) Creating a relationship

- page_id: `303994480`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994480/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+a+relationship
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:16.886+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagramming > (2026x Refresh1) Working with paths and relationships
- labels: ['create-relationship', 'create-path', 'relationship-direction', 'relate-source-and-target', 'path-direction', 'path-target', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

You can create relationships between elements in one of the following ways:

- 
- 
- 

diagram_paneTo draw a path on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Understanding the user interface' space='']

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Understanding the user interface' space=''] , click the needed relationship.
2. Select the source shape of the path in the diagram pane.
3. Drop the path to the target shape. When drawing a new path, the optimal route is chosen automatically (the path avoids other shapes).When drawing a path between two elements, those shapes are bordered with a red or blue rectangle. The red highlight indicates that the path can not be drawn between these shapes. The blue highlight allows a path to be drawn. You can disable this highlighting in the **Environment Options** dialog under the Diagram node by setting the **Show Manipulation Highlighting** property value to *false*. For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Customizing environment options' space=''].To draw a path faster, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Smart manipulator toolbar' space=''].

model_browserTo create relationships between selected elements

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Model Browser' space=''] , diagram pane, or table, right-click an element.
2. Choose Create Relation > Outgoing / Incoming and select the desired relationship type. The Create New <relation name> To / From dialog opens.
3. Do one or more of the following:
  - In the Model Browser, click the element to / from which you want to create a relationship.
  - On a diagram, click an element to / from which you want to create a relationship.
  - In the **Create New <relation name> To** /**From** dialog, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. In the opened **Create New <relation name> To**/**From**dialog, choose the elementto / from which you want to create a relationship. If you do not know the exact location of the needed element, you can search for it in this step.
4. Click OK .

The **Active Selection** area shows the elements currently selected in the active diagram, table, matrix, map, or Model Browser. It changes with each click of the different elements when you switch to another diagram.

Tochoose one or several elements at a time,do one of the following

- Press Shift and click as many elements as you need.
- Click and drag the mouse pointer to select as many elements as you need.

The multiple selection works in one area at a time. For example, diagram pane, table, matrix, relation map, or Model Browser. If you need to select elements from several locations, you need to pin your current selection.

The **Pinned Selection** area shows the elements you choose to pin from the **Active Selection**. This is needed when you want to select elements from different locations (for example, you need to select elements from different diagrams, or from the diagram and Model Browser). In this case, you should pin each selection.

specificationTo create a relationship in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification window' space='']

1. Open the element's Specification window and select the Relations property group.
2. Click the Create Outgoing button or Create Incoming button and then select the desired relation type from the list.
3. In the Create New <relation name> To/ From dialog select an element to/ from which you want to create a relation and click OK . The relation appears in the Relations property group.

When the relationship is created, you can visually identify its direction (source and target elements) under the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Containment tab' space=''] >**Relations**.

The arrow between source and target elements appears if the path is created using only directed relationships.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

It changes with each click of the different elements when you switch to another diagram.</p><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7c3f0f3b-09de-4337-8fa6-4e7e973667d7"><br /></ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7c3f0f3b-09de-4337-8fa6-4e7e973667d7">To </ac:inline-comment-marker>choose one or several elements at a time<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7c3f0f3b-09de-4337-8fa6-4e7e973667d7">, </ac:inline-comment-marker><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7c3f0f3b-09de-4337-8fa6-4e7e973667d7">do one of the following</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></p><hr /><ul><li>Press Shift and click as many elements as you need.</li><li>Click and drag the mouse pointer to select as many elements as you need.</li></ul><p>The multiple selection works in one area at a time. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000466 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00354: (2026x Refresh1) Creating a report including the Actor, Use Cases, and diagram image names

- page_id: `304000466`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000466/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+a+report+including+the+Actor+Use+Cases+and+diagram+image+names
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:32:14.560+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Template Creation Tutorial > (2026x Refresh1) Linking report templates to the modeling tool > (2026x Refresh1) How modeling tool adds its elements to velocity context
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

135588550

135588551

135588549

**On this page**

3

135588551

##### Creating a report including the names of actors, use cases, and diagram images

This section will use part of the Inventory Control System.mdzip file as an example to illustrate how to reference elements in a report. This example contains several packages. For instance, if you want to use the 'Use Case View' package, you need to know the elements inside the package before using it. To do this, follow the instructions in the previous section, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Your First Template' space=''].

The 'Use Case View' package contains three element types: Actor, UseCase, and Diagram that you can name in a template by typing **"$Actor**," **"$UseCase**," and **"$Diagram**" respectively. The following example shows you how to create a report that will print the names of the Actors, UseCases, and diagram images.

To create a report that includes the names of Actor, Use Cases, and diagram images

1. Open Microsoft Word or OpenOffice.org Writer.
2. Use the **#foreach** directive to iterate through **$Actor**, **$UseCase**, and **$Diagram**. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Using #foreach directive to iterate through $Actor, $UseCase, and $Diagram in a Rich Text Format template.
3. Use Report Wizard to add the template to the modeling tool.
4. With the " Inventory Control System.mdzip " project open, open the Report Wizard dialog and select your template.
5. Select the element scope so it covers only the "Use Case View" package. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Selecting the Use Case View package from the model as the element scope to be included in the report.
6. Complete all steps in the **Report Wizard** dialog, and generate the report. The output will be shown as in the following figure. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Shows a partial report output. A full report will print all the names of the Actors, Use Cases, and the Use Case diagrams.

##### Glossary

- Actor An actor models a type of role played by an entity that interacts with the subjects of its associated use cases, e.g., by exchanging signal and data. Actors may represent roles played by human users, external hardware, or other systems.
- Element T he abstract root UML metaclass. It has no superclass in the hierarchy of UML elements. It is the superclass for all metaclasses in the UML infrastructure library. There is no general notation for an element. The specific subclasses of an element define their own notation. Each UMLelementhas acompositionrelationship to itself to support the capability for elements toownother elements.
- Use case A specification of behavior. A use case is a kind of BehavioredClassifier that represents a declaration of a set of offered Behaviors. Each use case specifies some behavior that a subject can perform in collaboration with one or more actors. A UseCase may be owned either by a Package or by a Classifier. Use cases are a means to capture the requirements of systems,( i.e. what systems are supposed to do).

##### Sample model

[ATTACHMENT filename='Inventory Control System.mdzip']250

##### More information about creating report templates

To learn more about working with report templates, visit the following pages:

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Your First Template' space='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Linking report templates to the modeling tool' space='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Exercise: Building a simple class diagram report' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8e89264c-43fa-4307-a8df-4d2fd2dd7cef"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">135588550</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="82788425-137f-45d7-9da1-0c543ac80a31"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">135588551</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f8df02be-c7aa-413a-bd61-587fc7843ff1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">135588549</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="25b94b5c-c705-4d08-a3a9-c7ef18077981"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48d1684b-b720-4d61-8446-9b204c2e3645"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">135588551</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3>Creating a report including the names of actors, use cases, and diagram images</h3><p>This section will use part of the Inventory Control System.mdzip file as an example to illustrate how to reference elements in a report. This example contains several packages. For instance, if you want to use the 'Use Case View' package, you need to know the elements inside the package before using it. To do this, follow the instructions in the previous section, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating Your First Template" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating your first template]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>The 'Use Case View' package contains three element types: Actor, UseCase, and Diagram that you can name in a template by typing <strong>&quot;$Actor</strong>,&quot; <strong>&quot;$UseCase</strong>,&quot; and <strong>&quot;$Diagram</strong>&quot; respectively. The following example shows you how to create a report that will print the names of the Actors, UseCases, and diagram images.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a report that includes the names of Actor, Use Cases, and diagram images</p><hr /><ol><li>Open Microsoft Word or <a href="http://OpenOffice.org">OpenOffice.org</a> Writer.</li><li><p>Use the <strong>#foreach</strong> directive to iterate through <strong>$Actor</strong>, <strong>$UseCase</strong>, and <strong>$Diagram</strong>.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="A Rich Text Format Template" ac:alt="A Rich Text Format Template"><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddavc7e294e99af69f1030b81c42610dd0c7.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating a report including the Actor, Use Cases, and diagram image names" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Using #foreach directive to iterate through $Actor, $UseCase, and $Diagram in a Rich Text Format template.</h6></li><li>Use Report Wizard to add the template to the modeling tool.</li><li>With the &quot;<em>Inventory Control System.mdzip</em>&quot; project open, open the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog and select your template.</li><li><p>Select the element scope so it covers only the &quot;Use Case View&quot; package.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Select_elementScope.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating a report including the Actor, Use Cases, and diagram image names" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Selecting the Use Case View package from the model as the element scope to be included in the report.</h6></li><li><p>Complete all steps in the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog, and generate the report. The output will be shown as in the following figure.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="partialOutput.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating a report including the Actor, Use Cases, and diagram image names" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6><span>Shows a partial report output. A full report will print all the names of the Actors, Use Cases, and the Use Case diagrams.</span></h6></li></ol><h3>Glossary</h3><ul><li><strong>Actor</strong><br />An actor models a type of role played by an entity that interacts with the subjects of its associated use cases, e.g., by exchanging signal and data. Actors may represent roles played by human users, external hardware, or other systems.</li><li><strong>Element</strong><br />T<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">he abstract </span>root<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> UML metaclass. It has no superclass in the hierarchy of UML elements. It is the superclass for all metaclasses in the </span>UML<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">infrastructure library. <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">There is no general notation for an element. The specific subclasses of an element define their own notation. <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Each UML </span>element<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> has a </span>composition<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> relationship to itself to support the capability for elements to </span>own<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> other elements.</span></span></span></li><li><strong>Use case</strong><br />A specification of behavior. A use case is a kind of BehavioredClassifier that represents a declaration of a set of offered Behaviors. Each use case specifies some behavior that a subject can perform in collaboration with one or more actors. A UseCase may be owned either by a Package or by a Classifier. <span>Use cases are a means to capture the requirements of systems,( i.e. what systems are supposed to do). </span></li></ul><h3>Sample model</h3><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="view-file" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b697956-c268-47c6-a6c6-87b8403e366e"><ac:parameter ac:name="name"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Inventory Control System.mdzip"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating a report including the Actor, Use Cases, and diagram image names" /></ri:attachment></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">250</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3>More information about creating report templates</h3><p>To learn more about working with report templates, visit the following pages:</p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating Your First Template" /></ac:link></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Linking report templates to the modeling tool" /></ac:link></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Exercise: Building a simple class diagram report" /></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This section explains the creation of a basic electrical circuit model using the SysML extension. The model can be exported into Modelica® and Simulink®. The same process can be used to create more complex models.

Read the following topics:

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991197 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00356: (2026x Refresh1) Creating a SysML Internal Block diagram for a Block

- page_id: `303991197`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991197/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+a+SysML+Internal+Block+diagram+for+a+Block
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:20:28.306+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling structure with Blocks > (2026x Refresh1) Decomposing Blocks
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''] for a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED']

1. Select the Block shape on the diagram pane. The smart manipulator menu appears.
2. Click the SysML Internal Block diagram button [ATTACHMENT filename='ibd_icon.png'] . The SysML Internal Block diagram is created and owned by the selected Block. The SysML Internal Block diagram and the owner Block have the same name. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining hyperlinks1' space=''] to the created diagram is added to the Block.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SysML Internal Block Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Internal Block diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Block" /></ac:link></p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Block shape on the diagram pane. The smart manipulator menu appears.</li><li>Click the SysML Internal Block diagram button <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="SysML Internal Block Diagram" ac:width="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ibd_icon.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating a SysML Internal Block diagram for a Block" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.<br />The SysML Internal Block diagram is created and owned by the selected Block. The SysML Internal Block diagram and the owner Block have the same name. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Defining hyperlinks1" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[hyperlink ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>to the created diagram is added to the Block.</li></ol>
````

## PAGE 00357: (2026x Refresh1) Creating a template for a Rich Text Format or OpenDocument Text document, or Microsoft Word Document (DOCX)

- page_id: `303999101`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999101/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+a+template+for+a+Rich+Text+Format+or+OpenDocument+Text+document+or+Microsoft+Word+Document+DOCX
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:30:30.271+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Template Creation Tutorial > (2026x Refresh1) Linking report templates to the modeling tool > (2026x Refresh1) Supported file types
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1358508562

1358508564

1358530037

**On this page**

3

1358508564

#### Creating a template for a Rich Text Format or OpenDocument Text document, or Microsoft Word Document (DOCX)

You can create a document in .rtf, .odt, or .docx file format and add it to the modeling tool as a report template.

To create a template for a Rich Text Format or OpenDocument Text document, or Microsoft Word Document (*DOCX*)

1. Open OpenOffice.org Writer or Microsoft Word for an *.odt , *.rtf , or *.docx file. (To save a file as an *.odt file in Microsoft Word, an add-on is required. You can get the add-on at [http://odf-converter.sourceforge.net/](http://odf-converter.sourceforge.net/) .)
2. Add the Velocity Template Language codes to the template file.
3. Save the template file.
4. Add the template to the modeling tool.

When generating a document, Report Wizard will apply the text format used in writing the Velocity code.

For instance, when typing:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

the generated output will be:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Glossary

- Velocity A Java-based template engine. It permits anyone to use a simple, yet powerful, template language to reference objects defined in Java code . Velocity is a project of the[Apache Software Foundation](http://www.apache.org/), charged with the creation and maintenance of open-source software related to the[Apache Velocity Engine](http://velocity.apache.org/engine/index.html). All software created at the Velocity project is available under the[Apache Software License](http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0.txt)and free of charge for the public.

#### More information about working with report templates in Report Wizard

To learn more about working with report templates, visit the following pages:

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating a template for an HTML, a text, an XML, or a DocBook document' space='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating a template for an OpenDocument Presentation (ODP) or Microsoft PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)' space='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating a template for an OpenDocument spreadsheet or Microsoft Excel spreadsheet' space='']

1358508560

It permits anyone to use a simple, yet powerful, template language to reference objects defined in Java </span><span style="color: rgb(106,106,106);">code</span><span style="color: rgb(84,84,84);">. <span>Velocity is a project of the </span><a href="http://www.apache.org/">Apache Software Foundation</a><span>, charged with the creation and maintenance of open-source software related to the </span><a href="http://velocity.apache.org/engine/index.html">Apache Velocity Engine</a><span>. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000464 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00358: (2026x Refresh1) Creating a template for an HTML, a text, an XML, or a DocBook document

- page_id: `304000464`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000464/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+a+template+for+an+HTML+a+text+an+XML+or+a+DocBook+document
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:32:14.090+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Template Creation Tutorial > (2026x Refresh1) Linking report templates to the modeling tool > (2026x Refresh1) Supported file types
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a template for an *HTML*, a *text*, an *XML*, or a *DocBook* document

1. Open a text editor.
2. Add the code to the text file.
3. Save the template file (with a correct filename extension).
4. Add the template to Report Wizard.

- When you generate a report from a particular template, Report Wizard will automatically create an HTML , text , an XML , or a DocBook document depending on a given template filename extension.
- The #forrow , #forcol , and #forpage directives do not support HTML , text, XML , and DocBook format files.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a template for an <em>HTML</em>, a <em>text</em>, an <em>XML</em>, or a <em>DocBook</em> document</p><hr /><ol><li>Open a text editor.</li><li>Add the code to the text file.</li><li>Save the template file (with a correct filename extension).</li><li>Add the template to Report Wizard.<br /> </li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6853e0b9-8381-4096-9c74-cccd903d3c91"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>When you generate a report from a particular template, Report Wizard will automatically create an <em>HTML</em>, <em>text</em>, an <em>XML</em>, or a <em>DocBook</em> document depending on a given template filename extension.</li><li>The <strong>#forrow</strong>,<strong> #forcol</strong>, and <strong>#forpage</strong> directives do not support <em>HTML</em>, <em>text, XML</em>, and <em>DocBook</em> format files.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999108 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00359: (2026x Refresh1) Creating a template for an OpenDocument Presentation (ODP) or Microsoft PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)

- page_id: `303999108`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999108/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+a+template+for+an+OpenDocument+Presentation+ODP+or+Microsoft+PowerPoint+presentation+PPTX
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:30:31.003+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Template Creation Tutorial > (2026x Refresh1) Linking report templates to the modeling tool > (2026x Refresh1) Supported file types
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1351510228

1351510229

1351510227

**On this page**

3

1351510229

To create a template for an OpenDocument Presentation (ODP) or Microsoft PowerPoint presentation (*PPTX*)

1. Open OpenOffice.org Impress or Microsoft PowerPoint.
2. Add the code to the template file.
3. Save the template file.
4. Add the template to Report Wizard.

A presentation document is a special document template that does not contain a content order. It uses a text box to insert text as the content of the document. A text box is an image structure (an image structure keeps the position of each image in x, y coordinates). You can change the position of a text box, or place it in the same position as others.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Writing VTL in a text box in [OpenOffice.org](http://OpenOffice.org) Impress.

The following are the rules that you need to follow when using the **#if,****#elseif**, **#else**, **#foreach**, **#forpage**, and **#macro** directives in **.odp* and *.pptx* documents:

- Statement VTL statements that maintain a sequence order, such as **#if** and **#foreach**, must begin and end the statements inside the same text box. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Valid use of multi-line statement in ODP. Do not split the statements into several text boxes, as this will cause an error. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Invalid use of a multi-line statement in ODP.
- #macro statement A #macro statement must be declared in the same text box. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Valid use of macro statement in ODP.
- #forpage statement The **#forpage** statement is different from the others, as it does not contain any order. It can begin and end the statement in different text boxes. Its value can also be used in different text boxes. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Valid use of the #forpage statement in ODP. To use a structured statement such as **#if**, **#macro**, or **#foreach** with the **#forpage** statement, ensure that the statement is in the same text box. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Valid use of #if and #foreach with #forpage statement. Using an example from the previous figure, the generated result will be as follows: [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The end result of using #if and #foreach with #forpage statement.

#### Glossary

- VTL TheVelocity Template Language(VTL) is meant to provide an easy, simple, and clean way to incorporate dynamic content in a web page. A VTL statementbegins with the # character and contains a directive: set .

#### More information about working with Velocity Template Language (VTL)

To learn more about working with Velocity Template Language (VTL), visit the following pages:

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Velocity templating language' space='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Velocity variable' space='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Velocity Directives' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7a2ec743-3420-4720-b3d8-3c06d684e641"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1351510228</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5d10119f-1405-49c4-944e-cf8c321c91fc"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1351510229</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c34177ea-8f9e-4c1e-a117-c23cc4f0e7f2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1351510227</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fd883afe-f4ef-41e1-a2cf-8ae062b63342"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bed55076-77f3-42e8-b47d-4af02f555900"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1351510229</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To create a template for an OpenDocument Presentation (ODP) or Microsoft PowerPoint presentation (<em>PPTX</em>)</p><hr /><ol><li>Open <a href="http://OpenOffice.org">OpenOffice.org</a> Impress or Microsoft PowerPoint.</li><li>Add the code to the template file.</li><li>Save the template file.</li><li>Add the template to Report Wizard.</li></ol><p>A presentation document is a special document template that does not contain a content order. It uses a text box to insert text as the content of the document. A text box is an image structure (an image structure keeps the position of each image in x, y coordinates). You can change the position of a text box, or place it in the same position as others.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="Writing VTL in a Text Box in OpenOffice.org Impress" ac:alt="Writing VTL in a Text Box in OpenOffice.org Impress"><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddav8b64e92d6f5eba4553f1cc84c1b328cf.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating a template for an OpenDocument Presentation (ODP) or Microsoft PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Writing VTL in a text box in <a href="http://OpenOffice.org">OpenOffice.org</a> Impress.</h6><p>The following are the rules that you need to follow when using the <strong>#if, </strong><strong>#elseif</strong>, <strong>#else</strong>, <strong>#foreach</strong>, <strong>#forpage</strong>, and <strong>#macro</strong> directives in <em>*.odp</em> and <em>.pptx</em> documents:</p><ul><li>Statement<br /><p><span>VTL statements that maintain a sequence order, such as <strong>#if</strong> and <strong>#foreach</strong>, must begin and end the statements inside the same text box.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:title="Valid Usage of a Multi-line Statement in ODP" ac:alt="Valid Usage of a Multi-line Statement in ODP"><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddav41267f4b975cce11e3785bbf28c92e47.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating a template for an OpenDocument Presentation (ODP) or Microsoft PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6> Valid use of multi-line statement in ODP.</h6><p>Do not split the statements into several text boxes, as this will cause an error.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Invalid Usage of a Multi-line Statement in ODP" ac:alt="Invalid Usage of a Multi-line Statement in ODP"><ri:attachment ri:filename="odp-and-.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating a template for an OpenDocument Presentation (ODP) or Microsoft PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Invalid use of a multi-line statement in ODP.</h6></li><li><p>#macro statement</p><p>A #macro statement must be declared in the same text box.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Valid Usage of Macro Statement in ODP" ac:alt="Valid Usage of Macro Statement in ODP"><ri:attachment ri:filename="odp-and-macro.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating a template for an OpenDocument Presentation (ODP) or Microsoft PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Valid use of macro statement in ODP.</h6></li><li><p>#forpage statement</p><p>The <strong>#forpage</strong> statement is different from the others, as it does not contain any order. It can begin and end the statement in different text boxes.  Its value can also be used in different text boxes.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddaveb0191f630de81137cd71fc63cc21822.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating a template for an OpenDocument Presentation (ODP) or Microsoft PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Valid use of the #forpage statement in ODP.</h6><p>To use a structured statement such as <strong>#if</strong>, <strong>#macro</strong>, or <strong>#foreach</strong> with the <strong>#forpage</strong> statement, ensure that the statement is in the same text box.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddav70aacaede91de71fd23bf54a8d48685b.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating a template for an OpenDocument Presentation (ODP) or Microsoft PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Valid use of #if and #foreach with #forpage statement.</h6><p>Using an example from the previous figure, the generated result will be as follows: </p><p><ac:image ac:title="The Generated Output" ac:alt="The Generated Output"><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddav25139ea583311cb91cae05041ba335df.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating a template for an OpenDocument Presentation (ODP) or Microsoft PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>The end result of using <span>#if and #foreach with #forpage statement.</span></h6></li></ul><h2>Glossary</h2><ul><li><strong>VTL</strong><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><br />The </span>Velocity Template Language<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> (</span>VTL<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">) is meant to provide an easy, simple, and clean way to incorporate dynamic content in a web page. A VTL statement </span>begins with the </span><code class="literal" style="text-align: justify;">#</code><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> character and contains a directive: </span><code class="literal" style="text-align: justify;">set</code><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">. </span></li></ul><h2>More information about working with Velocity Template Language (VTL)</h2><p>To learn more about working with Velocity Template Language (VTL), visit the following pages:</p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Velocity templating language" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Velocity template language]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Velocity variable" /></ac:link></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Velocity Directives" /></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000476 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00360: (2026x Refresh1) Creating a template for an OpenDocument spreadsheet or Microsoft Excel spreadsheet

- page_id: `304000476`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000476/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+a+template+for+an+OpenDocument+spreadsheet+or+Microsoft+Excel+spreadsheet
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:32:15.240+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Template Creation Tutorial > (2026x Refresh1) Linking report templates to the modeling tool > (2026x Refresh1) Supported file types
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a template for an OpenDocument spreadsheet or Microsoft Excel spreadsheet

1. Open OpenOffice.org Calc or Microsoft Excel.
2. Add the code to the template file.
3. Save the template file.
4. Add the template to Report Wizard.

All multiline directives such as **#if, #else,****#elseif**, **#foreach**, and **#macro** must be used under the following conditions:

1. The beginning and end of a statement must be declared within a single cell. A #macro statement must be declared in a single cell. [ATTACHMENT filename='worddav195c3297d20439835819f6152c9fb7fe.png']
2. You can use #foreach in a single cell record only. To create data for multiple rows, use the #forrow directive instead, see figure below. [ATTACHMENT filename='worddav6f99f5e91aea399e24eac62e0953d947.png']
3. You can use #forcol to create data for multiple columns, see figure below. You can use the statement in conjunction with #forrow . [ATTACHMENT filename='worddav55c194a898ab2d279c85ddb229cdf770.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a template for an OpenDocument spreadsheet or Microsoft Excel spreadsheet</p><hr /><ol><li>Open OpenOffice.org Calc or Microsoft Excel.</li><li>Add the code to the template file.</li><li>Save the template file.</li><li>Add the template to Report Wizard.</li></ol><p>All multiline directives such as <strong>#if, #else, </strong><strong>#elseif</strong>, <strong>#foreach</strong>, and <strong>#macro</strong> must be used under the following conditions:</p><ol><li>The beginning and end of a statement must be declared within a single cell. A <strong>#macro</strong> statement must be declared in a single cell.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddav195c3297d20439835819f6152c9fb7fe.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating a template for an OpenDocument spreadsheet or Microsoft Excel spreadsheet" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>You can use <strong>#foreach</strong> in a single cell record only. To create data for multiple rows, use the <strong>#forrow</strong> directive instead, see figure below.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddav6f99f5e91aea399e24eac62e0953d947.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating a template for an OpenDocument spreadsheet or Microsoft Excel spreadsheet" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>You can use <strong>#forcol</strong> to create data for multiple columns, see figure below. You can use the statement in conjunction with <strong>#forrow</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddav55c194a898ab2d279c85ddb229cdf770.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating a template for an OpenDocument spreadsheet or Microsoft Excel spreadsheet" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000539 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00361: (2026x Refresh1) Creating a UAF project

- page_id: `304000539`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000539/2026x+Refresh1+Creating+a+UAF+project
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:32:24.271+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling
- labels: ['new-project', 'create-blank-uaf-project', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A project is a physical working unit that consists of model, model visualizations(diagrams, tables, matrices, etc.), configuration data and referencesto other elements residing in used projects.

To create a project

1. Do one of the following:
  - Select **File**> **New** **Project**.
  - On the main toolbar, click the **New** **Project** button.
  - Press Ctrl+Shift+N.
2. In the **New****Project** dialog, under **Enterprise Modeling**, select the****project type you want to create.
3. Specify name, location, and other preferences if needed.
4. Click **OK** when you are done.

By default, **UAF Project** will be selected in the **General** property group. You can select any of the available Architectural Frameworks.

You can always change the Architectural Framework in the **Project Options** dialog **General project options** group.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

When creating a new project, in the New Project dialog, you can choose which Framework views will be created and how will they be ordered.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

You can select any of the available Architectural Frameworks.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="998fe568-6567-43b5-9d11-9a5ccd847db7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can always change the Architectural Framework in the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog <strong>General project options</strong> group.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_project.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating a UAF project" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>When creating a new project, in the New Project dialog, you can choose which Framework views will be created and how will they be ordered.<br /><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span></p>
